solution_text: We have {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{4}-x\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{x^{5}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{x^{4}} d x{/tex} Put, {tex}1-\frac{1}{x^{3}}=1-x^{-3}=t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3}{x^{4}} d x=d t{/tex} Therefore {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{4}-x\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{x^{5}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \int t^{\frac{1}{4}} d t=\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{4}{5} t^{\frac{5}{4}}+C=\frac{4}{15}\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{\frac{5}{4}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{4}-x\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{x^{5}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{x^{4}} d x{/tex} 
Put, {tex}1-\frac{1}{x^{3}}=1-x^{-3}=t{/tex}  {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3}{x^{4}} d x=d t{/tex} 
Therefore {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{4}-x\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{x^{5}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \int t^{\frac{1}{4}} d t=\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{4}{5} t^{\frac{5}{4}}+C=\frac{4}{15}\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{3}}\right)^{\frac{5}{4}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: We have {tex}\frac{x^{4}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=(x+1)+\frac{1}{x^{3}-x^{2}+x-1}{/tex} Using Partitial Fraction, = {tex}(x+1)+\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} ......(i) Now express {tex}\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{\mathrm{A}}{(x-1)}+\frac{\mathrm{B} x+\mathrm{C}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} .....(ii) So 1 = A (x 2 + 1) + (Bx + C) (x - 1) = (A + B) x 2 + (C - B) x + A - C Equating coefficients on both sides, we get A + B = 0, C - B = 0 and A - C = 1, which gives {tex}A=\frac{1}{2}, B=C=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} Substituting values of A, B and C in (ii), we get {tex}\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{1}{2} \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}-\frac{1}{2\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} .....(iii) Again, substituting (iii) in (i), we have {tex}\frac{x^{4}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)}{/tex} = {tex}(x+1)+\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{1}{2} \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}-\frac{1}{2\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} Therefore {tex}\int \frac{x^{4}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+x+\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|-\frac{1}{4} \log \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have {tex}\frac{x^{4}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=(x+1)+\frac{1}{x^{3}-x^{2}+x-1}{/tex} 
Using Partitial Fraction, 
= {tex}(x+1)+\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} ......(i)
Now express {tex}\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{\mathrm{A}}{(x-1)}+\frac{\mathrm{B} x+\mathrm{C}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} .....(ii)
So 1 = A (x2 + 1) + (Bx + C) (x - 1)
= (A + B) x2 + (C - B) x + A - C
Equating coefficients on both sides, we get A + B = 0, C - B = 0 and A - C = 1, which gives 
{tex}A=\frac{1}{2}, B=C=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
Substituting values of A, B and C in (ii), we get
{tex}\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{1}{2} \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}-\frac{1}{2\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} .....(iii)
Again, substituting (iii) in (i), we have
{tex}\frac{x^{4}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)}{/tex} = {tex}(x+1)+\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{1}{2} \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}-\frac{1}{2\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} 
Therefore
{tex}\int \frac{x^{4}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+x+\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|-\frac{1}{4} \log \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: According to the question , {tex} I = \int \left[ \log ( \log x ) + \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } \right] d x{/tex} By Using integration by parts for first integral, we get {tex} = \int {\log } (\mathop {\mathop{\rm l}\nolimits} \limits_Iogx) \cdot \mathop 1\limits_{II} dx + \int {\frac{1}{{{{(\log x)}^2}}}} dx{/tex} {tex}I = log (log x) \int 1 d x - \int \left[ \frac { d } { d x } \log ( \log x )\Big]\Big[ \int 1 d x \right] d x + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex} {tex}= x log (log x) - \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) } d x + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex} {tex}= x log (log x)-\int (\mathop {\mathop{\rm\frac { 1}{logx}}\nolimits} \limits_I ) \cdot \mathop 1\limits_{II} dx + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex} By Using integration by parts for second integral, we get {tex}= x log (log x) -\frac{x}{logx}+ \int [\frac {d}{dx}(\frac {1}{logx})]. (\int 1dx)dx+ \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex} {tex}= x log (log x) -\frac{x}{logx}- \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x \frac {1}{x} . x+ \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex} {tex}= x log (log x) -\frac{x}{logx}- \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex} {tex} = x \log ( \log x )-\frac{x}{logx}+ C{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = x \log ( \log x ) -\frac{x}{logx}+ C{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question ,  {tex} I = \int \left[ \log ( \log x ) + \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } \right] d x{/tex}
By Using integration by parts for first integral, we get
{tex} = \int {\log } (\mathop {\mathop{\rm l}\nolimits} \limits_Iogx) \cdot \mathop 1\limits_{II} dx + \int {\frac{1}{{{{(\log x)}^2}}}} dx{/tex}
{tex}I = log (log x) \int 1 d x - \int \left[ \frac { d } { d x } \log ( \log x )\Big]\Big[ \int 1 d x \right] d x + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}

{tex}= x log (log x) - \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) } d x + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex}

{tex}= x log (log x)-\int (\mathop {\mathop{\rm\frac { 1}{logx}}\nolimits} \limits_I ) \cdot \mathop 1\limits_{II} dx + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex}

By Using integration by parts for second integral, we get

{tex}= x log (log x) -\frac{x}{logx}+ \int [\frac {d}{dx}(\frac {1}{logx})]. (\int 1dx)dx+ \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex}
{tex}= x log (log x) -\frac{x}{logx}- \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x \frac {1}{x} . x+ \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex}

{tex}= x log (log x) -\frac{x}{logx}- \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x + \int \frac { 1 } { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } d x {/tex}
{tex} = x \log ( \log x )-\frac{x}{logx}+ C{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = x \log ( \log x ) -\frac{x}{logx}+ C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int [ \sqrt { \cot x } + \sqrt { \tan x } ] d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{tanx}} + \sqrt{tanx} \ [\because cotx = \frac {1}{tanx}]{/tex} {tex} = \int \sqrt { \tan x } \Bigg[1 +\frac {1}{\Big(\sqrt { \tan x }\Big)^2}\Bigg] d x{/tex} put {tex} tan x = t^2 \implies sec^2 x dx = 2t dt{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { sec^2x}dt{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { 1 +tan^2x }dt{/tex} {tex}[ \because 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } x = \sec ^ { 2 } x]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { 1 + (t ^ { 2 })^2 }{/tex} {tex}[ tan x = t^2 ]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { 1 + t ^ { 4 } }{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int t \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) \frac { 2 t } { \left( 1 + t ^ { 4 } \right) } d t [\because tanx =t^2 \implies \sqrt {tanx} = t]{/tex} {tex} = 2 \int \frac { t ^ { 2 } + 1 } { t ^ { 4 } + 1 } d t{/tex} On dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}t^2{/tex} , we get {tex} I = 2 \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( t ^ { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) } d t {/tex} {tex}= 2 \int \frac { 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } } { t ^ { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } - 2 + 2 } d t{/tex} {tex} = 2 \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right) ^ { 2 } + 2 } d t{/tex} Again, put {tex} t-\frac { 1 } { t } = y \Rightarrow \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) d t = d y{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = 2 \int \frac { d y } { y ^ { 2 } + ( \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } } {/tex} {tex} I = \frac { 2 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { y } { \sqrt { 2 } } + C{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { a } \right) + c \right]{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { \left( t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right) } { \sqrt { 2 } } + C \quad \left[ \text { put } y = t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right]{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } t } \right) + C{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \tan x - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 \tan x } } \right) + C{/tex} {tex}[Put \ t^2 = tan x]{/tex} {tex}I = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \tan x - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 \tan x } } \right) + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let  {tex}I = \int [ \sqrt { \cot x } + \sqrt { \tan x } ] d x{/tex}

{tex}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{tanx}} + \sqrt{tanx} \ [\because cotx = \frac {1}{tanx}]{/tex}
{tex} = \int \sqrt { \tan x } \Bigg[1 +\frac {1}{\Big(\sqrt { \tan x }\Big)^2}\Bigg] d x{/tex}
put {tex} tan x = t^2 \implies sec^2 x dx = 2t dt{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { sec^2x}dt{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { 1 +tan^2x }dt{/tex}{tex}[ \because 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } x = \sec ^ { 2 } x]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { 1 + (t ^ { 2 })^2 }{/tex}{tex}[ tan x = t^2 ]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \frac { 2 t } { 1 + t ^ { 4 } }{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int t \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) \frac { 2 t } { \left( 1 + t ^ { 4 } \right) } d t [\because tanx =t^2 \implies \sqrt {tanx} = t]{/tex}
{tex} = 2 \int \frac { t ^ { 2 } + 1 } { t ^ { 4 } + 1 } d t{/tex}
On dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}t^2{/tex} , we get
{tex} I = 2 \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( t ^ { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) } d t {/tex}

{tex}= 2 \int \frac { 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } } { t ^ { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } - 2 + 2 } d t{/tex}
{tex} = 2 \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right) ^ { 2 } + 2 } d t{/tex}
Again, put  {tex} t-\frac { 1 } { t } = y \Rightarrow \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) d t = d y{/tex}
{tex} \therefore I = 2 \int \frac { d y } { y ^ { 2 } + ( \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } } {/tex}

{tex} I = \frac { 2 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { y } { \sqrt { 2 } } + C{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { a } \right) + c \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { \left( t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right) } { \sqrt { 2 } } + C \quad \left[ \text { put } y = t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } t } \right) + C{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \tan x - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 \tan x } } \right) + C{/tex}   {tex}[Put \ t^2 = tan x]{/tex}

{tex}I = \sqrt { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \tan x - 1 } { \sqrt { 2 \tan x } } \right) + C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find {tex}\int \frac{\sin 2 x \cos 2 x d x}{\sqrt{9-\cos ^{4}(2 x)}}{/tex}
question_html: Find {tex}\int \frac{\sin 2 x \cos 2 x d x}{\sqrt{9-\cos ^{4}(2 x)}}{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int \frac{\sin 2 x \cos 2 x}{\sqrt{9-\cos ^{4} 2 x}} d x{/tex} Put cos 2 (2x) = t so that 2 sin 2x cos 2x dx = -dt Therefore, I = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{9-t^{2}}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{3}\right)+\mathrm{C}=-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{1}{3} \cos ^{2} 2 x\right]+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \frac{\sin 2 x \cos 2 x}{\sqrt{9-\cos ^{4} 2 x}} d x{/tex} 
Put cos2 (2x) = t so that 2 sin 2x cos 2x dx = -dt
Therefore,
I = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{9-t^{2}}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{3}\right)+\mathrm{C}=-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{1}{3} \cos ^{2} 2 x\right]+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

solution_text: Here f (x) = | x sin {tex}\pi{/tex} x| = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{l} {x \sin \pi x \text { for }-1 \leq x \leq 1} \\ {-x \sin \pi x \text { for } 1 \leq x \leq \frac{3}{2}} \end{array}\right.{/tex} Therefore {tex}\int_{-1}^{\frac{3}{2}}|x \sin \pi x| d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} x \sin \pi x d x+\int_{1}^{\frac{3}{2}}-x \sin \pi x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} x \sin \pi x d x-\int_{1}^{\frac{3}{2}} x \sin \pi x d x{/tex} Integrating both integrals on righthand side, we get {tex}\int_{-1}^{\frac{3}{2}}|x \sin \pi x| d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{-x \cos \pi x}{\pi}+\frac{\sin \pi x}{\pi^{2}}\right]_{-1}^{1}-\left[\frac{-x \cos \pi x}{\pi}+\frac{\sin \pi x}{\pi^{2}}\right]_{1}^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{\pi}-\left[-\frac{1}{\pi^{2}}-\frac{1}{\pi}\right]=\frac{3}{\pi}+\frac{1}{\pi^{2}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Here f (x) = | x sin {tex}\pi{/tex}x| = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{l} {x \sin \pi x \text { for }-1 \leq x \leq 1} \\ {-x \sin \pi x \text { for } 1 \leq x \leq \frac{3}{2}} \end{array}\right.{/tex} 
Therefore {tex}\int_{-1}^{\frac{3}{2}}|x \sin \pi x| d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} x \sin \pi x d x+\int_{1}^{\frac{3}{2}}-x \sin \pi x d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} x \sin \pi x d x-\int_{1}^{\frac{3}{2}} x \sin \pi x d x{/tex} 
Integrating both integrals on righthand side, we get
{tex}\int_{-1}^{\frac{3}{2}}|x \sin \pi x| d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{-x \cos \pi x}{\pi}+\frac{\sin \pi x}{\pi^{2}}\right]_{-1}^{1}-\left[\frac{-x \cos \pi x}{\pi}+\frac{\sin \pi x}{\pi^{2}}\right]_{1}^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{\pi}-\left[-\frac{1}{\pi^{2}}-\frac{1}{\pi}\right]=\frac{3}{\pi}+\frac{1}{\pi^{2}}{/tex}

question_text: Evaluate {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
solution_text: According to the question , {tex} I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} ...(ii) On adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get , {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( x + \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex} we know that, {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 a } f ( x ) d x = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { a }f(x) dx \ , \ if \ \ f(2a - x) = f(x){/tex} Here, {tex} a^2 cos^2( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ){/tex} {tex}= a^2 cos^2 x + b^2 sin^2x{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex} On dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}cos^2x{/tex} we get , {tex} 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } \tan ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} Put {tex}tan x = t \implies sec^2 x dx = dt{/tex} Lower limit when x = 0, then {tex}t = tan 0 = 0{/tex} Upper limit when x = {tex} \frac { \pi } { 2 },{/tex} then {tex} t =\tan \frac { \pi } { 2 } = \infty{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } t ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + ( b t ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \frac { \pi } { b ^ { 2 } } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { \left( \frac { a } { b } \right) ^ { 2 } + t ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { b t } { a } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty - \tan ^ { - 1 } 0 \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty = \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) = \frac { \pi } { 2 } } \\ { \text { and } \tan ^ { - 1 } 0 = \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan 0 ^ { \circ } \right) = 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = \frac { \pi ^ { 2 } } { 2 a b }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question , {tex} I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} ...(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} ...(ii)
On adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get ,
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { ( x + \pi - x ) } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex}
we know that,{tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 a } f ( x ) d x = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { a }f(x) dx \ , \ if \ \ f(2a - x) = f(x){/tex}
Here, {tex} a^2 cos^2( \pi - x ) + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } ( \pi - x ){/tex}
{tex}= a^2 cos^2 x + b^2 sin^2x{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } x + b ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } x }{/tex}
On dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}cos^2x{/tex} we get ,
{tex} 2 I = 2 \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } \tan ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}tan x = t \implies   sec^2 x dx = dt{/tex}
Lower limit when x = 0, then {tex}t = tan 0 = 0{/tex}
Upper limit when x = {tex} \frac { \pi } { 2 },{/tex}then {tex} t =\tan \frac { \pi } { 2 } = \infty{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + b ^ { 2 } t ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { a ^ { 2 } + ( b t ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex} = \frac { \pi } { b ^ { 2 } } \int _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \frac { d t } { \left( \frac { a } { b } \right) ^ { 2 } + t ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { b t } { a } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \infty } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { a ^ { 2 } + x ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty - \tan ^ { - 1 } 0 \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { \pi } { a b } \left[ \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 \right]{/tex}{tex} \left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \tan ^ { - 1 } \infty = \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) = \frac { \pi } { 2 } } \\ { \text { and } \tan ^ { - 1 } 0 = \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan 0 ^ { \circ } \right) = 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \therefore I = \frac { \pi ^ { 2 } } { 2 a b }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function sin 2x by the method of inspection.
question_html: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function sin 2x by the method of inspection.
solution_text: We know that {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\cos 2 x)=-2 \sin 2 x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin 2 x=-\frac{1}{2} \frac{d}{d x}(\cos 2 x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(-\frac{1}{2} \cos 2 x\right){/tex} Therefore, the anti-derivative of sin2x is {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cos 2 x{/tex}
solution_html:
We know that
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\cos 2 x)=-2 \sin 2 x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin 2 x=-\frac{1}{2} \frac{d}{d x}(\cos 2 x){/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(-\frac{1}{2} \cos 2 x\right){/tex} 
Therefore, the anti-derivative of sin2x is {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cos 2 x{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function by the method of inspection cos 3x.
question_html: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function by the method of inspection cos 3x.
solution_text: We know that {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\sin 3 x)=3 \cos 3 x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \cos 3 x=\frac{1}{3} \frac{d}{d x}(\sin 3 x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{3} \sin 3 x\right){/tex} Therefore, the anti-derivative of cos3x is {tex}\frac{1}{3} \sin 3 x{/tex} .
solution_html:
We know that {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(\sin 3 x)=3 \cos 3 x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos 3 x=\frac{1}{3} \frac{d}{d x}(\sin 3 x){/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{3} \sin 3 x\right){/tex}
Therefore, the anti-derivative of cos3x is {tex}\frac{1}{3} \sin 3 x{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function by the method of inspection e 2x .
question_html: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function by the method of inspection e2x .
solution_text: We know that {tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{2 x}\right)=2 e^{2 x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}=\frac{1}{2} \frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{2 x}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{2} e^{2 x}\right){/tex} Therefore, the anti-derivative of e 2x is {tex}\frac{1}{2} e^{2 x}{/tex} .
solution_html:
We know that
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{2 x}\right)=2 e^{2 x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}=\frac{1}{2} \frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{2 x}\right){/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{2} e^{2 x}\right){/tex} 
Therefore, the anti-derivative of e2x is {tex}\frac{1}{2} e^{2 x}{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function by the method of inspection (ax + b) 2
question_html: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of function by the method of inspection (ax + b)2
solution_text: We know that {tex}\frac{d}{d x}(a x+b)^{3}=3 a(a x+b)^{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(a x+b)^{2}=\frac{1}{3 a} \frac{d}{d x}(a x+b)^{3}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{3 a}(a x+b)^{3}\right){/tex} Therefore, the anti-derivative of (ax + b) 2 is {tex}\frac{1}{3 a}(a x+b)^{3}{/tex} .
solution_html:
We know that 
{tex}\frac{d}{d x}(a x+b)^{3}=3 a(a x+b)^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow(a x+b)^{2}=\frac{1}{3 a} \frac{d}{d x}(a x+b)^{3}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{3 a}(a x+b)^{3}\right){/tex}
Therefore, the anti-derivative of (ax + b)2 is {tex}\frac{1}{3 a}(a x+b)^{3}{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of the function by the method of inspection sin 2x - 4e 3x
question_html: Find an anti derivative (or integral) of the function by the method of inspection sin 2x - 4e3x
solution_text: We know that, {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\cos 2x} \right) = - 2\sin 2x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\cos 2x} \right) = \sin 2x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x} \right) = \sin 2x{/tex} ...(i) Again {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{3x}} = 3{e^{3x}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{1}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) = {e^{3x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 4}}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) = - 4{e^{3x}}{/tex} [Multiplying both sides by -4] ...(ii) Adding eq. (i) and (ii), we get {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 4}}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) {/tex} = sin 2x + (-4e 3x ) {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x - \frac{4}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) {/tex} = sin 2x - 4e 3x {tex}\therefore {/tex} An anti-derivative of sin 2x - 4e 3x is {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x - \frac{4}{3}{e^{3x}}.{/tex}
solution_html:
We know that, {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\cos 2x} \right) = - 2\sin 2x{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\cos 2x} \right) = \sin 2x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x} \right) = \sin 2x{/tex} ...(i)
Again {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{e^{3x}} = 3{e^{3x}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{1}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) = {e^{3x}}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 4}}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) = - 4{e^{3x}}{/tex}   [Multiplying both sides by -4] ...(ii)
Adding eq. (i) and (ii), we get
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x} \right) + \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 4}}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) {/tex} = sin 2x + (-4e3x) 
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x - \frac{4}{3}{e^{3x}}} \right) {/tex} = sin 2x - 4e3x
{tex}\therefore {/tex} An anti-derivative of sin 2x - 4e3x is {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{2}\cos 2x - \frac{4}{3}{e^{3x}}.{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(4 e^{3 x}+1\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(4 e^{3 x}+1\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int\left(4 e^{3 x}+1\right) d x=4 \int e^{3 x} d x+\int 1 d x{/tex} = {tex}4\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}^{3 \mathrm{x}}}{3}\right)+\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{3} e^{3 x}+x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\left(4 e^{3 x}+1\right) d x=4 \int e^{3 x} d x+\int 1 d x{/tex}
= {tex}4\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}^{3 \mathrm{x}}}{3}\right)+\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{4}{3} e^{3 x}+x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int x^{2}\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int x^{2}\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int x^{2}\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(x^{2}-1\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int x^{2} d x-\int 1 d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{3}}{3}-x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int x^{2}\left(1-\frac{1}{x^{2}}\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int\left(x^{2}-1\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int x^{2} d x-\int 1 d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{3}}{3}-x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(a x^{2}+b x+c\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(a x^{2}+b x+c\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int\left(a x^{2}+b x+c\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}a \int x^{2} d x+b \int x d x+c \int 1 d x{/tex} = {tex}a\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right)+b\left(\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right)+c x+c{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a x^{3}}{3}+\frac{b x^{2}}{2}+c x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\left(a x^{2}+b x+c\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}a \int x^{2} d x+b \int x d x+c \int 1 d x{/tex} 
= {tex}a\left(\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right)+b\left(\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right)+c x+c{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{a x^{3}}{3}+\frac{b x^{2}}{2}+c x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(2 x^{2}+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(2 x^{2}+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int\left(2 x^{2}+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int x^{2} d x+\int e^{x} d x{/tex} = {tex}2\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}^{3}}{3}\right)+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x^{3}}{3}+e^{x}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\left(2 x^{2}+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}2 \int x^{2} d x+\int e^{x} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}^{3}}{3}\right)+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2 x^{3}}{3}+e^{x}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int\left(\sqrt{x}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(x+\frac{1}{x}-2\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int x d x+\int \frac{1}{x} d x-2 \int 1 d x{/tex} Now we know that, {tex}^{\int x^{n} d x}=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+c{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\int x d x+\int \frac{1}{x} d x-2 \int 1 d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+\log |x|-2 x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\left(\sqrt{x}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int\left(x+\frac{1}{x}-2\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int x d x+\int \frac{1}{x} d x-2 \int 1 d x{/tex} 
Now we know that,
{tex}^{\int x^{n} d x}=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+c{/tex} 
Therefore,
{tex}\int x d x+\int \frac{1}{x} d x-2 \int 1 d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+\log |x|-2 x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+5 x^{2}-4}{x^{2}} d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+5 x^{2}-4}{x^{2}} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+5 x^{2}-4}{x^{2}} d x{/tex} Separating the terms we get, I = {tex}\int\left(x+5-4 x^{-2}\right) d x{/tex} Applying the formula, {tex}\int x^{n} d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+c{/tex} , gives I = {tex}\int x d x+5 \int 1 d x-4 \int x^{-2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+5 x-4\left(\frac{x^{-1}}{-1}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+5 x+\frac{4}{x}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+5 x^{2}-4}{x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
Separating the terms we get,
I = {tex}\int\left(x+5-4 x^{-2}\right) d x{/tex} 
Applying the formula,
{tex}\int x^{n} d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+c{/tex} , gives
I = {tex}\int x d x+5 \int 1 d x-4 \int x^{-2} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+5 x-4\left(\frac{x^{-1}}{-1}\right)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+5 x+\frac{4}{x}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+3 x+4}{\sqrt{x}} d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+3 x+4}{\sqrt{x}} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+3 x+4}{\sqrt{x}} d x{/tex} Separating the terms we get, I = {tex}\int\left(x^{\frac{5}{2}}+3 x^{\frac{1}{2}}+4 x^{-\frac{1}{2}}\right) d x{/tex} Applying the formula, {tex}\int x^{n} d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+c{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{x^{\frac{7}{2}}}{\frac{7}{2}}+\frac{3\left(x^{\frac{3}{2}}\right)}{\frac{3}{2}}+\frac{4\left(x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)}{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{7} x^{\frac{7}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+8 x^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{7} x^{\frac{7}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+8 \sqrt{x}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I ={tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+3 x+4}{\sqrt{x}} d x{/tex} 
Separating the terms we get,
I ={tex}\int\left(x^{\frac{5}{2}}+3 x^{\frac{1}{2}}+4 x^{-\frac{1}{2}}\right) d x{/tex} 
Applying the formula,
{tex}\int x^{n} d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+c{/tex} 
I = {tex}\frac{x^{\frac{7}{2}}}{\frac{7}{2}}+\frac{3\left(x^{\frac{3}{2}}\right)}{\frac{3}{2}}+\frac{4\left(x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)}{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{7} x^{\frac{7}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+8 x^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{7} x^{\frac{7}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+8 \sqrt{x}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}-x^{2}+x-1}{x-1} d x{/tex} Now the numerator can be factorized as, x 3 - x 2 + x - 1 = x 2 (x - 1) + 1(x - 1) x 3 - x 2 + x - 1 = (x 2 + 1)(x - 1) Now putting this in given integral we get, I = {tex}\frac{x^{3}-x^{2}+x-1}{x-1}=\frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)}{x-1}=x^{2}+1{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(x^{2}+1\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int x^{2} d x+\int 1 . d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{3}}{3}+x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}-x^{2}+x-1}{x-1} d x{/tex} 
Now the numerator can be factorized as,
x3 - x2 + x - 1 = x2(x - 1) + 1(x - 1)
x3 - x2 + x - 1 = (x2 + 1)(x - 1)
Now putting this in given integral we get,
I = {tex}\frac{x^{3}-x^{2}+x-1}{x-1}=\frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)}{x-1}=x^{2}+1{/tex} 
= {tex}\int\left(x^{2}+1\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int x^{2} d x+\int 1 . d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{3}}{3}+x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int \sqrt{x}\left(3 x^{2}+2 x+3\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int \sqrt{x}\left(3 x^{2}+2 x+3\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int \sqrt{x}\left(3 x^{2}+2 x+3\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(3 x^{\frac{5}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+3 x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}3\left(\frac{x^{\frac{7}{2}}}{\frac{7}{2}}\right)+2\left(\frac{x^{\frac{5}{2}}}{\frac{5}{2}}\right)+3\left(\frac{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{7} x^{\frac{7}{2}}+\frac{4}{5} x^{\frac{5}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int \sqrt{x}\left(3 x^{2}+2 x+3\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int\left(3 x^{\frac{5}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+3 x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}3\left(\frac{x^{\frac{7}{2}}}{\frac{7}{2}}\right)+2\left(\frac{x^{\frac{5}{2}}}{\frac{5}{2}}\right)+3\left(\frac{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}\right)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{6}{7} x^{\frac{7}{2}}+\frac{4}{5} x^{\frac{5}{2}}+2 x^{\frac{3}{2}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(2 x-3 \cos x+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(2 x-3 \cos x+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int\left(2 x-3 cos x+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int x d x-3 \int \cos x d x+\int e^{x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x^{2}}{2}-3(\sin x)+e^{x}+C{/tex} = {tex}x^{2}-3 \sin x+e^{x}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\left(2 x-3 cos x+e^{x}\right) d x{/tex}

= {tex}x^{2}-3 \sin x+e^{x}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(2 x^{2}-3 \sin x+5 \sqrt{x}\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex}\int\left(2 x^{2}-3 \sin x+5 \sqrt{x}\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int\left(2 x^{2}-3 \sin x+5 \sqrt{x}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}2 \int x^{2} d x-3 \int \sin x d x+5 \int x^{\frac{1}{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x^{3}}{3}+3 \cos x+5\left(\frac{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x^{3}}{3}+3 \cos x+\frac{10}{3} x^{\frac{3}{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:

= {tex}\frac{2 x^{3}}{3}+3 \cos x+\frac{10}{3} x^{\frac{3}{2}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral: {tex} \int \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { cosec ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral: {tex} \int \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { cosec ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { cosec ^ { 2 } x } d x = \int \frac { \left( \frac { 1 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) } { \left( \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 2 } x } \right) } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \tan ^ { 2 } x d x = \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - 1 \right) d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \tan ^ { 2 } x = \sec ^ { 2 } x - 1 \right]{/tex} {tex} = \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int 1 d x = \tan x - x + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac { \sec ^ { 2 } x } { cosec ^ { 2 } x } d x = \int \frac { \left( \frac { 1 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) } { \left( \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 2 } x } \right) } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \int \tan ^ { 2 } x d x = \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - 1 \right) d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \tan ^ { 2 } x = \sec ^ { 2 } x - 1 \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int 1 d x = \tan x - x + c{/tex}

= {tex}\int 2 \sec ^{2} x d x-3 \int \tan x \sec x d x{/tex} 
= 2tan x - 3sec x + C

question_text: If {tex}\frac{d}{d x} f(x)=4 x^{3}-\frac{3}{x^{4}}{/tex} such that f(2) = 0. Then f(x) is
question_html: If {tex}\frac{d}{d x} f(x)=4 x^{3}-\frac{3}{x^{4}}{/tex} such that f(2) = 0. Then f(x) is

options.explanation: It if given that {tex}\frac{d}{d x} f(x)=4 x^{3}-\frac{3}{x^{4}}{/tex} {tex}f(x)=\int 4 x^{3}-\frac{3}{x^{4}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow f(x)=4 \int x^{3} d x-3 \int\left(x^{-4}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow f(x)=4\left(\frac{x^{4}}{4}\right)-3\left(\frac{x^{-3}}{-3}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow f(x)=x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}+C{/tex} Also, It is given that f(2) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow f(2)=(2)^{4}+\frac{1}{(2)^{3}}+\mathrm{C}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 16+\frac{1}{8}+C=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{C}=-\left(16+\frac{1}{8}\right)=-\frac{129}{8}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}f(x)=x^{4}+\frac{1}{x^{3}}-\frac{129}{8}{/tex} . Which is the required solution.

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let 1 + x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt Now, {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{1+x^{2}} d x=\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} = log |t| + C = log |1 + x 2 | + C = log (1 + x 2 ) + C
solution_html:

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Putting 1 + log x = t {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{x} = {dt}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{x} = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int {\frac{1}{{x + x\log x}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{1 + \log x}}\frac{{dx}}{x}} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{t}dt = \log \left| t \right| + c} {/tex} = log |1 + log x| + c
solution_html:

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function: sin x sin(cos x)
question_html: Integrate the function: sin x sin(cos x)
solution_text: Putting cos x = t {tex}\Rightarrow - \sin x = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow - \sin xdx = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int {\sin x\sin \left( {\cos x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\sin \left( {\cos x} \right)\left( { - \sin xdx} \right)} {/tex} {tex}= - \int {\sin t} dt{/tex} = - (-cos t) + c = cos t + c = cos (cos x) + c
solution_html:
Putting cos x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow - \sin x = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow - \sin xdx = dt{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \int {\sin x\sin \left( {\cos x} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int {\sin \left( {\cos x} \right)\left( { - \sin xdx} \right)} {/tex}
{tex}= - \int {\sin t} dt{/tex}
= - (-cos t) + c
= cos t + c = cos (cos x) + c

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int \sin (a x+b) \cos (a x+b) d x{/tex} We know that, sin 2A = 2sinA.cosA Therefore, sin (ax + b) cos (ax + b) = {tex}\frac{2 \sin (a x+b) \cos (a x+b)}{2}=\frac{\sin 2(a x+b)}{2}{/tex} Let 2(ax + b) = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2adx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{\sin 2(a x+b)}{2} d x=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sin t}{2 a} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4 a}[-\cos t]+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{4 a} \cos 2(a x+b)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \sin (a x+b) \cos (a x+b) d x{/tex} 
We know that,
sin 2A = 2sinA.cosA
Therefore, sin (ax + b) cos (ax + b) = {tex}\frac{2 \sin (a x+b) \cos (a x+b)}{2}=\frac{\sin 2(a x+b)}{2}{/tex} 
Let 2(ax + b) = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2adx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{\sin 2(a x+b)}{2} d x=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sin t}{2 a} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4 a}[-\cos t]+C{/tex}
={tex}\frac{-1}{4 a} \cos 2(a x+b)+C{/tex}

solution_text: Let I = {tex} \int x \sqrt{x+2} d x{/tex} Put (x + 2) = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int x \sqrt{x+2} d x=\int(t-2) \sqrt{t} d t{/tex} {tex}=\int\left(t^{\frac{3}{2}}-2 t^{\frac{t}{2}}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}=\int t^{\frac{3}{2}} d t-2 \int t^{\frac{1}{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}=\frac{t^{\frac{5}{2}}}{\frac{5}{2}}-2\left(\frac{t^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2}{5} t^{\frac{5}{2}}-\frac{4}{3} t^{\frac{3}{2}}+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2}{5}(x+2)^{\frac{5}{2}}-\frac{4}{3}(x+2)^{\frac{3}{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:

solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\frac{1}{{x - \sqrt x }}dx{/tex} ...(i) Putting {tex}\sqrt x = t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = {t^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{{dt}} = 2t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow dx = 2tdt{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{{t^2} - t}}2t} dt{/tex} {tex}= 2\int {\frac{t}{{t\left( {t - 1} \right)}}} dt{/tex} {tex} = 2\int {\frac{1}{{\left( {t - 1} \right)}}} dt{/tex} = 2 log |t - 1| + c {tex} = 2\log \left| {\sqrt x - 1} \right| + c{/tex}
solution_html:

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let x + 4 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x}{\sqrt{x+4}} d x=\int \frac{(t-4)}{\sqrt{t}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(\sqrt{t}-\frac{4}{\sqrt{t}}\right) d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{t^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}-4\left(\frac{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}(t)^{\frac{3}{2}}-8(t)^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex} = {tex} \frac{2}{3} t \cdot t^{\frac{1}{2}}-8(t)^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}(x+4)^{\frac{1}{2}}(x+4-12)+C{/tex} = {tex} \frac{2}{3} \sqrt{x+4}(x-8)+C{/tex} .
solution_html:
Let x + 4 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x}{\sqrt{x+4}} d x=\int \frac{(t-4)}{\sqrt{t}} d t{/tex}
= {tex}\int\left(\sqrt{t}-\frac{4}{\sqrt{t}}\right) d t{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{t^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}-4\left(\frac{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right)+C{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2}{3}(t)^{\frac{3}{2}}-8(t)^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex}
= {tex} \frac{2}{3} t \cdot t^{\frac{1}{2}}-8(t)^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2}{3}(x+4)^{\frac{1}{2}}(x+4-12)+C{/tex}
= {tex} \frac{2}{3} \sqrt{x+4}(x-8)+C{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let x 3 - 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x 2 dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow\int\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} x^{5} d x=\int\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} x^{3} \cdot x^{2} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int t^{\frac{1}{3}}(t+1) \frac{d t}{3}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{3} \int\left(t^{\frac{4}{3}}+t^{\frac{1}{3}}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{7}{3}}}{\frac{7}{3}}+\frac{t^{\frac{4}{3}}}{\frac{4}{3}}\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{3}{7} t^{\frac{7}{3}}+\frac{3}{4} t^{\frac{4}{3}}\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{7}\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{7}{3}}+\frac{1}{4}\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{4}{3}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let x3 - 1 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow\int\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} x^{5} d x=\int\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} x^{3} \cdot x^{2} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int t^{\frac{1}{3}}(t+1) \frac{d t}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{3} \int\left(t^{\frac{4}{3}}+t^{\frac{1}{3}}\right) d t{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{7}{3}}}{\frac{7}{3}}+\frac{t^{\frac{4}{3}}}{\frac{4}{3}}\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{3}{7} t^{\frac{7}{3}}+\frac{3}{4} t^{\frac{4}{3}}\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{7}\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{7}{3}}+\frac{1}{4}\left(x^{3}-1\right)^{\frac{4}{3}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{{\left( {2 + 3{x^3}} \right)}^3}}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{9}\int {\frac{{9{x^2}}}{{{{\left( {2 + 3{x^3}} \right)}^3}}}dx} {/tex} …(i) Putting 2 + 3x 3 = t {tex} \Rightarrow 9{x^2} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 9{x^2}dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \frac{1}{9}\int\frac{1}{t^3}dt{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{9}\int {{t^{ - 3}}dt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{9}.\frac{{{t^{ - 2}}}}{{ - 2}} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{18{t^2}}} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{18{{\left( {2 + 3{x^3}} \right)}^2}}} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{{\left( {2 + 3{x^3}} \right)}^3}}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{9}\int {\frac{{9{x^2}}}{{{{\left( {2 + 3{x^3}} \right)}^3}}}dx} {/tex}…(i)

Putting 2 + 3x3 = t

{tex} \Rightarrow 9{x^2} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 9{x^2}dx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \frac{1}{9}\int\frac{1}{t^3}dt{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{9}\int {{t^{ - 3}}dt} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{9}.\frac{{{t^{ - 2}}}}{{ - 2}} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{18{t^2}}} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{18{{\left( {2 + 3{x^3}} \right)}^2}}} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{x{{\left( {\log x} \right)}^m}}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\frac{1}{x}dx}}{{{{\left( {\log x} \right)}^m}}}} dx{/tex} …(i) Putting {tex}\log x = t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{x} = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^m}}} = \int {{t^{ - m}}dt} } {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{t^{ - m + 1}}}}{{ - m + 1}} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{{\left( {\log x} \right)}^{1 - m}}}}{{1 - m}} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{x{{\left( {\log x} \right)}^m}}}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\frac{{\frac{1}{x}dx}}{{{{\left( {\log x} \right)}^m}}}} dx{/tex}…(i)
Putting {tex}\log x = t{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{x} = dt{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^m}}} = \int {{t^{ - m}}dt} } {/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{{t^{ - m + 1}}}}{{ - m + 1}} + c{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{{{\left( {\log x} \right)}^{1 - m}}}}{{1 - m}} + c{/tex}

solution_text: Let x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2xdx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x}{e^{x^{2}}} d x=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{e^{t}} d t{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int e^{-t} d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{e^{-t}}{-1}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-1}{2} e^{-x^{2}}+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-1}{2 e^{x^{2}}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: We have, {tex}\frac{e^{2 x}-1}{e^{2 x}+1}{/tex} Dividing numerator and denominator by e x , we get, {tex}\frac{\frac{e^{2 x}-1}{e^{x}}}{\frac{e^{2 x}+1}{e^{-x}}}=\frac{e^{x}-e^{-x}}{e^{x}+e^{-x}}{/tex} Let e x + e -x = t Differentiating both sides, we get, (e x - e -x )dx = dt Now the integral becomes, = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{t}{/tex} = log |t| + C = log|e x + e -x | + C
solution_html:
We have, 
{tex}\frac{e^{2 x}-1}{e^{2 x}+1}{/tex} 
Dividing numerator and denominator by ex, we get,
{tex}\frac{\frac{e^{2 x}-1}{e^{x}}}{\frac{e^{2 x}+1}{e^{-x}}}=\frac{e^{x}-e^{-x}}{e^{x}+e^{-x}}{/tex} 
Let ex + e-x = t
Differentiating both sides, we get,
(ex - e-x )dx = dt
Now the integral becomes,
= {tex}\int \frac{d t}{t}{/tex} 
= log |t| + C
= log|ex + e-x| + C

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrated the function: {tex}\int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
question_html: Integrated the function: {tex}\int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}e^{2x} + e^{-2x} = t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( 2 e ^ { 2 x } - 2 e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \frac { d } { d x } \left( e ^ { a x } \right) = a e ^ { a x } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = { d t } {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { t } {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t | + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C \quad \text { [ put } t = e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } ]{/tex} {tex}\therefore I =\frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}e^{2x} + e^{-2x} = t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( 2 e ^ { 2 x } - 2 e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \frac { d } { d x } \left( e ^ { a x } \right) = a e ^ { a x } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = { d t } {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { t } {/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t | + C{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C \quad \text { [ put } t = e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } ]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I =\frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}\int {{{\tan }^2}\left( {2x - 3} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {\left\{ {{{\sec }^2}\left( {2x - 3} \right) - 1} \right\}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {{{\sec }^2}\left( {2x - 3} \right)} dx - \int {1dx} {/tex} Using {tex}\int {{{\sec }^2}\left( {ax + b} \right)} dx = \frac{{\tan \left( {ax + b} \right)}}{a} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\tan \left( {2x - 3} \right)}}{2} - x + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {{{\tan }^2}\left( {2x - 3} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex} = \int {{{\sec }^2}\left( {2x - 3} \right)} dx - \int {1dx} {/tex}

Using {tex}\int {{{\sec }^2}\left( {ax + b} \right)} dx = \frac{{\tan \left( {ax + b} \right)}}{a} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\tan \left( {2x - 3} \right)}}{2} - x + c{/tex}

question_text: Integrate the function: {tex}\frac{{2\cos x - 3\sin x}}{{6\cos x + 4\sin x}}{/tex}
question_html: Integrate the function: {tex}\frac{{2\cos x - 3\sin x}}{{6\cos x + 4\sin x}}{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{2\cos x - 3\sin x}}{{6\cos x + 4\sin x}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{{2\cos x - 3\sin x}}{{2\left( {2\sin x + 3\cos x} \right)}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2\cos x - 3\sin x}}{{2\sin x + 3\cos x}}dx} {/tex} …(i) Putting 2 sin x + 3 cos x = t {tex} \Rightarrow 2\cos x - 3\sin x = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} (2 cos x - 3 sin x)dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{dt}}{t} = \frac{1}{2}\log \left| t \right| + c} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {2\sin x + 3\cos x} \right| + c{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} (2 cos x - 3 sin x)dx = dt

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {2\sin x + 3\cos x} \right| + c{/tex}
