subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\sin x}}{{{{\left( {1 + \cos x} \right)}^2}}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= - \int {\frac{{ - \sin x}}{{{{\left( {1 + \cos x} \right)}^2}}}dx} {/tex} …(i) Putting 1 + cos x = t {tex} \Rightarrow - \sin x = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} -sin xdx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = - \int {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^2}}}} {/tex} {tex} = - \int {{t^{-2}}dt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - {t^{ - 1}}}}{{ - 1}} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{t} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{1 + \cos x}} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\sin x}}{{{{\left( {1 + \cos x} \right)}^2}}}dx} {/tex}
{tex}= - \int {\frac{{ - \sin x}}{{{{\left( {1 + \cos x} \right)}^2}}}dx} {/tex}…(i)
Putting 1 + cos x = t
{tex} \Rightarrow - \sin x = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} -sin xdx = dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = - \int {\frac{{dt}}{{{t^2}}}} {/tex}
{tex} = - \int {{t^{-2}}dt} {/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{ - {t^{ - 1}}}}{{ - 1}} + c{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{t} + c{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{{1 + \cos x}} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int \frac{1}{1+\cot x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{1}{1+\frac{\cos x}{\sin x}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\sin x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(\sin x+\cos x)+(\sin x-\cos x)}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int 1 . d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(\sin x-\cos x)}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(\sin x-\cos x)}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} Let sinx + cosx = t {tex}\Rightarrow \cos x-\sin x=\frac{d t}{d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (cosx-sinx)dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow -(\sin x-\cos x) dx = -dt{/tex} Therefore, {tex}I=\frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-d t}{t}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \log |t|+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \log |\sin x+\cos x|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \frac{1}{1+\cot x} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{1}{1+\frac{\cos x}{\sin x}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\int \frac{\sin x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(\sin x+\cos x)+(\sin x-\cos x)}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int 1 . d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(\sin x-\cos x)}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(\sin x-\cos x)}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} 
Let sinx + cosx = t
{tex}\Rightarrow \cos x-\sin x=\frac{d t}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (cosx-sinx)dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow -(\sin x-\cos x) dx = -dt{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}I=\frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-d t}{t}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \log |t|+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \log |\sin x+\cos x|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \tan x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}}}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\left( {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x}}} \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} Adding and subtracting sin x in the numerator, {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x + \sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\left( {\cos x - \sin x} \right) + \left( {\sin x + \cos x} \right)}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {1 + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {1dx-\int {\frac{{ - \sin x - \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} } } \right]dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {x - \log \left| {\cos x - \sin x} \right|} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}[\because \int \frac{f'(x)}{f(x)} dx=log|f(x)|+c]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \tan x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{1 - \frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}}}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\left( {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x}}} \right)}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2\cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

Adding and subtracting sin x in the numerator,

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x + \sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\left( {\cos x - \sin x} \right) + \left( {\sin x + \cos x} \right)}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {1 + \frac{{\sin x + \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {1dx-\int {\frac{{ - \sin x - \cos x}}{{\cos x - \sin x}}} } } \right]dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {x - \log \left| {\cos x - \sin x} \right|} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}[\because \int \frac{f'(x)}{f(x)} dx=log|f(x)|+c]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function: {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\sin x \cos x}{/tex}
question_html: Integrate the function: {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\sin x \cos x}{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\sin x \cos x}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x} \cdot \cos x}{\sin x \cos x \cdot \cos x} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\tan x \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac{\sec ^{2} x d x}{\sqrt{\tan x}}{/tex} Let tan x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec 2 x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} {tex}=2 \sqrt{t}+C{/tex} {tex}=2 \sqrt{\tan x}+c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\sin x \cos x}{/tex} 
{tex}=\int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x} \cdot \cos x}{\sin x \cos x \cdot \cos x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\tan x \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int \frac{\sec ^{2} x d x}{\sqrt{\tan x}}{/tex} 
Let tan x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec2x dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} 
{tex}=2 \sqrt{t}+C{/tex} 
{tex}=2 \sqrt{\tan x}+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\left( {1 + \log x} \right)}^2}}}{x}dx}{/tex} ...(i) Putting {tex}1 + \log x = t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{x} = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {{t^2}dt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{t^3}}}{3} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}{\left( {1 + \log x} \right)^3} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\left( {1 + \log x} \right)}^2}}}{x}dx}{/tex} ...(i)

Putting {tex}1 + \log x = t{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{dx}}{x} = dt{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {{t^2}dt} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{t^3}}}{3} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{3}{\left( {1 + \log x} \right)^3} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\left( {x + 1} \right){{\left( {x + \log x} \right)}^2}}}{x}dx} {/tex} ...(i) Putting x + log x = t {tex} \Rightarrow 1 + \frac{1}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{x + 1}}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{x + 1}}{x}} \right)dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {{t^2}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{t^3}}}{3} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}{\left( {x + \log x} \right)^3} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\left( {x + 1} \right){{\left( {x + \log x} \right)}^2}}}{x}dx} {/tex} ...(i)

Putting x + log x = t

{tex} \Rightarrow 1 + \frac{1}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{{x + 1}}{x} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{x + 1}}{x}} \right)dx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {{t^2}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{t^3}}}{3} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{3}{\left( {x + \log x} \right)^3} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^3}\sin \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}{x^4}} \right)}}{{1 + {x^8}}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{4}\int {\sin \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}{x^4}} \right).\frac{{4{x^3}}}{{1 + {x^8}}}dx} {/tex} …(i) Putting tan -1 x 4 = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{{1 + {{\left( {{x^4}} \right)}^2}}}\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^4} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{4{x^3}}}{{1 + {x^8}}}dx = dt{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \frac{1}{4}\int {\sin tdt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{4}\cos t + c{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{4}\cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}{x^4}} \right) + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^3}\sin \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}{x^4}} \right)}}{{1 + {x^8}}}dx} {/tex} 
{tex} = \frac{1}{4}\int {\sin \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}{x^4}} \right).\frac{{4{x^3}}}{{1 + {x^8}}}dx} {/tex}…(i)
Putting tan-1x4 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{{1 + {{\left( {{x^4}} \right)}^2}}}\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^4} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{4{x^3}}}{{1 + {x^8}}}dx = dt{/tex} 
From eq. (i), {tex}I = \frac{1}{4}\int {\sin tdt} {/tex}  
{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{4}\cos t + c{/tex} 
{tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{4}\cos \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}{x^4}} \right) + c{/tex}

options.explanation: Let x 10 + 10 x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (10x 9 + 10 x log e 10)dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{10 x^{9}+10^{x} \log _{e} 10}{x^{10}+10^{x}} d x=\int \frac{d t}{t}{/tex} = log t + C = log(x 10 + 10 x ) + C

Let I = {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x}=\int \frac{1}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x~~~{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x+\int \frac{\cos ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^{2} x d x+\int cosec ^{2} x d x{/tex} {tex}{/tex} = tanx - cotx + C

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrals of the function sin 2 (2x + 5)
question_html: Find the integrals of the function sin2(2x + 5)
solution_text: {tex}\int {{{\sin }^2}\left( {2x + 5} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{2}\left\{ {1 - \cos 2\left( {2x + 5} \right)} \right\}dx} {/tex} Using {tex}{\sin ^2}\theta = \frac{{1 - \cos 2\theta }}{2}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{2}\int {\left\{ {1 - \cos \left( {4x + 10} \right)} \right\}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {1dx - \int {\cos \left( {4x + 10} \right)dx} } } \right]{/tex} Using {tex}\int {\cos \left( {ax + b} \right)dx = \frac{{\sin \left( {ax + b} \right)}}{a} + c} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {x - \frac{{\sin \left( {4x + 10} \right)}}{4}} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}x - \frac{1}{8}\sin \left( {4x + 10} \right) + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {{{\sin }^2}\left( {2x + 5} \right)dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{2}\left\{ {1 - \cos 2\left( {2x + 5} \right)} \right\}dx} {/tex}

Using {tex}{\sin ^2}\theta = \frac{{1 - \cos 2\theta }}{2}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{2}\int {\left\{ {1 - \cos \left( {4x + 10} \right)} \right\}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {1dx - \int {\cos \left( {4x + 10} \right)dx} } } \right]{/tex}

Using {tex}\int {\cos \left( {ax + b} \right)dx = \frac{{\sin \left( {ax + b} \right)}}{a} + c} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {x - \frac{{\sin \left( {4x + 10} \right)}}{4}} \right] + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}x - \frac{1}{8}\sin \left( {4x + 10} \right) + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrals of the function sin 3x cos 4x
question_html: Find the integrals of the function sin 3x cos 4x
solution_text: {tex}\int {\sin 3x\cos 4xdx = \frac{1}{2}\int {2\sin 3x\cos 4xdx} }{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{2}\int {\left\{ {\sin \left( {4x + 3x} \right) - \sin \left( {4x - 3x} \right)} \right\}dx} {/tex} [Using 2 sin B cos A = sin (A + B) - sin (A - B)] {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {\sin 7x - \sin x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {\sin 7xdx - \int {\sin xdx} } } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{{ - \cos 7x}}{7} - \left( { - \cos x} \right)} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{14}}\cos 7x + \frac{1}{2}\cos x + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\sin 3x\cos 4xdx = \frac{1}{2}\int {2\sin 3x\cos 4xdx} }{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{2}\int {\left\{ {\sin \left( {4x + 3x} \right) - \sin \left( {4x - 3x} \right)} \right\}dx} {/tex}  [Using 2 sin B cos A = sin (A + B) - sin (A - B)]

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {\sin 7x - \sin x} \right)dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\int {\sin 7xdx - \int {\sin xdx} } } \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{{ - \cos 7x}}{7} - \left( { - \cos x} \right)} \right] + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{14}}\cos 7x + \frac{1}{2}\cos x + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrals of the function cos 2x cos 4x cos 6x
question_html: Find the integrals of the function cos 2x cos 4x cos 6x
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\int \cos 2 x \cos 4 x \cos 6 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \cos 2 x\left[\frac{1}{2}\{\cos (4 x+6 x)+\cos (4 x-6 x)\}\right] d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int\{\cos 2 x \cos 10 x+\cos 2 x \cos (-2 x)\} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\cos 2 x \cos 10 x+\cos ^{2} 2 x\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\left[\frac{1}{2} \left\{\cos (2 \mathrm{x}+10 \mathrm{x})+\cos (2 \mathrm{x}-10 \mathrm{x})\right\}+\left(\frac{1+\cos 4 \mathrm{x}}{2}\right)\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4} \int(\cos 12 x+\cos 8 x+1+\cos 4 x) d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{\sin 12 \mathrm{x}}{12}+\frac{\sin 8 \mathrm{x}}{8}+\mathrm{x}+\frac{\sin 4 \mathrm{x}}{4}\right]+\mathrm{c}{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly,
{tex}\int \cos 2 x \cos 4 x \cos 6 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \cos 2 x\left[\frac{1}{2}\{\cos (4 x+6 x)+\cos (4 x-6 x)\}\right] d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int\{\cos 2 x \cos 10 x+\cos 2 x \cos (-2 x)\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\cos 2 x \cos 10 x+\cos ^{2} 2 x\right\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\left[\frac{1}{2} \left\{\cos (2 \mathrm{x}+10 \mathrm{x})+\cos (2 \mathrm{x}-10 \mathrm{x})\right\}+\left(\frac{1+\cos 4 \mathrm{x}}{2}\right)\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4} \int(\cos 12 x+\cos 8 x+1+\cos 4 x) d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{\sin 12 \mathrm{x}}{12}+\frac{\sin 8 \mathrm{x}}{8}+\mathrm{x}+\frac{\sin 4 \mathrm{x}}{4}\right]+\mathrm{c}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function sin 3 (2x + 1)
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int \sin ^{3}(2 x+1) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sin ^{3}(2 x+1) d x=\int \sin ^{2}(2 x+1) \cdot \sin (2 x+1) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int\left(1-\cos ^{2}(2 x+1)\right) \sin (2 x+1) d x{/tex} Let cos (2x + 1) = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2sin(2x + 1)dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin(2x + 1)dx = {tex}\frac{-\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore~ I=\frac{-1}{2} \int\left(1-t^{2}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-1}{2}\left\{t-\frac{t^{3}}{3}\right\}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-1}{2}\left\{\cos (2 \mathrm{x}+1)-\frac{\cos ^{3}(2 \mathrm{x}+1)}{3}\right\}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-\cos (2 \mathrm{x}+1)}{2}+\frac{\cos ^{3}(2 \mathrm{x}+1)}{6}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \sin ^{3}(2 x+1) d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \sin ^{3}(2 x+1) d x=\int \sin ^{2}(2 x+1) \cdot \sin (2 x+1) d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int\left(1-\cos ^{2}(2 x+1)\right) \sin (2 x+1) d x{/tex} 
Let cos (2x + 1) = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2sin(2x + 1)dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin(2x + 1)dx = {tex}\frac{-\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore~ I=\frac{-1}{2} \int\left(1-t^{2}\right) d t{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{-1}{2}\left\{t-\frac{t^{3}}{3}\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{-1}{2}\left\{\cos (2 \mathrm{x}+1)-\frac{\cos ^{3}(2 \mathrm{x}+1)}{3}\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{-\cos (2 \mathrm{x}+1)}{2}+\frac{\cos ^{3}(2 \mathrm{x}+1)}{6}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function sin 3 x cos 3 x
question_html: Find the integral of the function sin3 x cos3 x
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int \sin ^{3} x \cos ^{3} x \cdot d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos ^{3} x \cdot \sin ^{2} x \cdot \sin x \cdot d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos ^{3} x\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right) \sin x \cdot d x{/tex} Let cos x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -sinx.dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\int \mathrm{t}^{3}\left(1-\mathrm{t}^{2}\right) \mathrm{dt}{/tex} {tex}=-\int\left(t^{3}-t^{5}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}=-\left\{\frac{t^{4}}{4}-\frac{t^{6}}{6}\right\}+C{/tex} {tex}=-\left\{\frac{\cos ^{4} x}{4}-\frac{\cos ^{6} x}{6}\right\}+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\cos ^{6} x}{6}-\frac{\cos ^{4} x}{4}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \sin ^{3} x \cos ^{3} x \cdot d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos ^{3} x \cdot \sin ^{2} x \cdot \sin x \cdot d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos ^{3} x\left(1-\cos ^{2} x\right) \sin x \cdot d x{/tex} 
Let cos x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -sinx.dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\int \mathrm{t}^{3}\left(1-\mathrm{t}^{2}\right) \mathrm{dt}{/tex} 
{tex}=-\int\left(t^{3}-t^{5}\right) d t{/tex} 
{tex}=-\left\{\frac{t^{4}}{4}-\frac{t^{6}}{6}\right\}+C{/tex} 
{tex}=-\left\{\frac{\cos ^{4} x}{4}-\frac{\cos ^{6} x}{6}\right\}+C{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{\cos ^{6} x}{6}-\frac{\cos ^{4} x}{4}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrals of the function sin x sin 2x sin 3x
question_html: Find the integrals of the function sin x sin 2x sin 3x
solution_text: {tex}\int \sin x \sin 2 x \sin 3 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sin x \cdot \frac{1}{2}[\{\cos (2 x-3 x)-\cos (2 x+3 x)\}] d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\{\sin x \cos (-x)-\sin x \cos 5 x\} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\{\sin x \cos x-\sin x \cos 5 x\} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sin 2 x}{2} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \sin x \cos 5 x d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{-\cos 2 x}{2}\right]-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{2}\left\{ \sin (x+5 x)+\sin (x-5 x)\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-\cos 2 x}{8}-\frac{1}{4} \int(\sin 6 x+\sin (-4 x)) d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-\cos 2 x}{8}-\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{-\cos 6 x}{6}+\frac{\cos 4 x}{4}\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{-\cos 2 x}{8}-\frac{1}{8}\left[\frac{-\cos 6 x}{3}+\frac{\cos 4 x}{2}\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{8}\left[\frac{\cos 6 x}{3}-\frac{\cos 4 x}{2}-\cos 2 x\right]+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int \sin x \sin 2 x \sin 3 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sin x \cdot \frac{1}{2}[\{\cos (2 x-3 x)-\cos (2 x+3 x)\}] d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\{\sin x \cos (-x)-\sin x \cos 5 x\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\{\sin x \cos x-\sin x \cos 5 x\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sin 2 x}{2} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \sin x \cos 5 x d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{-\cos 2 x}{2}\right]-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{2}\left\{ \sin (x+5 x)+\sin (x-5 x)\right\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{-\cos 2 x}{8}-\frac{1}{4} \int(\sin 6 x+\sin (-4 x)) d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{-\cos 2 x}{8}-\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{-\cos 6 x}{6}+\frac{\cos 4 x}{4}\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{-\cos 2 x}{8}-\frac{1}{8}\left[\frac{-\cos 6 x}{3}+\frac{\cos 4 x}{2}\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{8}\left[\frac{\cos 6 x}{3}-\frac{\cos 4 x}{2}-\cos 2 x\right]+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrals of the function sin 4x sin 8x
question_html: Find the integrals of the function sin 4x sin 8x
solution_text: {tex}\int \sin 4 x \sin 8 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left\{\ cos (4 x-8 x)-\cos (4 x+8 x)\right\} d x{/tex} , {As Sin(A)Sin(B)=Cos(A-B)-Cos(A+B)} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int(\cos (-4 x)-\cos 12 x d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\{\cos 4 x-\cos 12 x\} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{\sin 4 x}{4}-\frac{\sin 12 x}{12}\right]+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int \sin 4 x \sin 8 x d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}\left\{\ cos (4 x-8 x)-\cos (4 x+8 x)\right\} d x{/tex} , {As Sin(A)Sin(B)=Cos(A-B)-Cos(A+B)}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int(\cos (-4 x)-\cos 12 x d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int\{\cos 4 x-\cos 12 x\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{\sin 4 x}{4}-\frac{\sin 12 x}{12}\right]+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}{/tex}
solution_text: We have {tex}\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}=\frac{2 \sin ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{2 \cos ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}= \tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}=\left(\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}-1\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x} d x=\int\left(\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}-1\right) d x = \int \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} d x-\int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\tan \frac{x}{2}}{\frac{1}{2}}-x+C~~ \begin{equation} \left(\because \int \sec ^{2}(a x+b) d x=\frac{\tan (a x+b)}{a}+C\right) \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}= 2 \tan \frac{\mathrm{x}}{2}-\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
We have {tex}\frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x}=\frac{2 \sin ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}{2 \cos ^{2} \frac{x}{2}}= \tan ^{2} \frac{x}{2}=\left(\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}-1\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1-\cos x}{1+\cos x} d x=\int\left(\sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}-1\right) d x = \int \sec ^{2} \frac{x}{2} d x-\int 1 d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{\tan \frac{x}{2}}{\frac{1}{2}}-x+C~~ \begin{equation} \left(\because \int \sec ^{2}(a x+b) d x=\frac{\tan (a x+b)}{a}+C\right) \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}= 2 \tan \frac{\mathrm{x}}{2}-\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{{\cos x}}{{1 + \cos x}}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{{\cos x}}{{1 + \cos x}}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{{\cos x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{1 + \cos x - 1}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{1 + \cos x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} - \frac{1}{{1 + \cos x}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex} As {tex}2{\cos ^2}\frac{\theta }{2} = 1 + \cos \theta{/tex} {tex}= \int 1 dx - \frac{1}{2}\int {{{\sec }^2}\frac{x}{2}dx} {/tex} {tex}= x - \frac{1}{2}\frac{{\tan \frac{x}{2}}}{{\frac{1}{2}}} + c{/tex} {tex}[\because\int {{{\sec }^2}\left( {ax + b} \right)} dx = \frac{{\tan \left( {ax + b} \right)}}{a} + c]{/tex} {tex}= x - \tan \frac{x}{2} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\frac{{\cos x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{1 + \cos x - 1}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{1 + \cos x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} - \frac{1}{{1 + \cos x}}dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex}

As {tex}2{\cos ^2}\frac{\theta }{2} = 1 + \cos \theta{/tex}

{tex}= \int 1 dx - \frac{1}{2}\int {{{\sec }^2}\frac{x}{2}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= x - \frac{1}{2}\frac{{\tan \frac{x}{2}}}{{\frac{1}{2}}} + c{/tex} {tex}[\because\int {{{\sec }^2}\left( {ax + b} \right)} dx = \frac{{\tan \left( {ax + b} \right)}}{a} + c]{/tex}

{tex}= x - \tan \frac{x}{2} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function sin 4 x
solution_text: We can write, sin 4 x = sin 2 xsin 2 x {tex}= \left(\frac{1-\cos 2 x}{2}\right)\left(\frac{1-\cos 2 x}{2}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{4}(1-\cos 2 x)^{2}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\cos ^{2} 2 x-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\left(\frac{1+\cos 4 x}{2}\right)-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{3}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \sin ^{4} x d x=\frac{1}{4} \int\left[\frac{3}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x-2 \cos 2 x\right] d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{3}{2} x+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{\sin 4 x}{4}\right)-\frac{2 \sin 2 x}{2}\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{8}\left[3 \mathrm{x}+\left(\frac{\sin 4 \mathrm{x}}{4}\right)-2 \sin 2 \mathrm{x}\right]+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{3 \mathrm{x}}{8}-\frac{1}{4} \sin 2 \mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{32} \sin 4 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
We can write, sin4x = sin2xsin2x
{tex}= \left(\frac{1-\cos 2 x}{2}\right)\left(\frac{1-\cos 2 x}{2}\right){/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{4}(1-\cos 2 x)^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\cos ^{2} 2 x-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\left(\frac{1+\cos 4 x}{2}\right)-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{3}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x-2 \cos 2 x\right]{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \sin ^{4} x d x=\frac{1}{4} \int\left[\frac{3}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x-2 \cos 2 x\right] d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{3}{2} x+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{\sin 4 x}{4}\right)-\frac{2 \sin 2 x}{2}\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{8}\left[3 \mathrm{x}+\left(\frac{\sin 4 \mathrm{x}}{4}\right)-2 \sin 2 \mathrm{x}\right]+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{3 \mathrm{x}}{8}-\frac{1}{4} \sin 2 \mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{32} \sin 4 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function cos 4 2x
solution_text: cos 4 2x = (cos 2 2x) 2 {tex}= \left(\frac{1+\cos 4 x}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\cos ^{2} 4 x+2 \cos 4 x\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\left(\frac{1+\cos 8 x}{2}\right)+2 \cos 4 x\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{3}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 8 x+2 \cos 4 x\right]{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \cos ^{4} 2 x d x=\int\left[\frac{3}{8}+\frac{1}{8} \cos 8 x+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x\right] d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{3 x}{8}+\frac{1}{64} \sin 8 x+\frac{1}{8} \sin 4 x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
cos42x = (cos22x)2 
{tex}= \left(\frac{1+\cos 4 x}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\cos ^{2} 4 x+2 \cos 4 x\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[1+\left(\frac{1+\cos 8 x}{2}\right)+2 \cos 4 x\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{3}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \cos 8 x+2 \cos 4 x\right]{/tex} 
 Now, {tex}\int \cos ^{4} 2 x d x=\int\left[\frac{3}{8}+\frac{1}{8} \cos 8 x+\frac{1}{2} \cos 4 x\right] d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{3 x}{8}+\frac{1}{64} \sin 8 x+\frac{1}{8} \sin 4 x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{{1 + \cos x}}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{{1 + \cos x}}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{1 - {{\cos }^2}x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {1 - \cos x} \right)\left( {1 + \cos x} \right)}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\left( {1 - \cos x} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {1dx - \int {\cos x} } dx{/tex} = x - sin x + c
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\frac{{{{\sin }^2}x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{1 - {{\cos }^2}x}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {1 - \cos x} \right)\left( {1 + \cos x} \right)}}{{1 + \cos x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\left( {1 - \cos x} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {1dx - \int {\cos x} } dx{/tex}

= x - sin x + c

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}{\frac{{\cos 2x - \cos 2\alpha }}{{\cos x - \cos \alpha }}}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral of the function {tex}{\frac{{\cos 2x - \cos 2\alpha }}{{\cos x - \cos \alpha }}}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{{\cos 2x - \cos 2\alpha }}{{\cos x - \cos \alpha }}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {2{{\cos }^2}x - 1} \right) - \left( {2{{\cos }^2}\alpha - 1} \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {2{{\cos }^2}x} \right) - \left( {2{{\cos }^2}\alpha} \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{{2\left( {\cos^2 x - \cos^2 \alpha } \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{{2\left( {\cos x + \cos \alpha } \right)\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int 2({cos x + cos \alpha })\ dx{/tex} {tex}= 2\left( {\sin x + x\cos \alpha } \right) + c{/tex} .
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\frac{{\cos 2x - \cos 2\alpha }}{{\cos x - \cos \alpha }}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {2{{\cos }^2}x - 1} \right) - \left( {2{{\cos }^2}\alpha - 1} \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {2{{\cos }^2}x} \right) - \left( {2{{\cos }^2}\alpha} \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex}

{tex} = \int {\frac{{2\left( {\cos^2 x - \cos^2 \alpha } \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex}

{tex} = \int {\frac{{2\left( {\cos x + \cos \alpha } \right)\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}{{\left( {\cos x - \cos \alpha } \right)}}} dx{/tex}
{tex} = \int 2({cos x + cos \alpha })\ dx{/tex}
{tex}= 2\left( {\sin x + x\cos \alpha } \right) + c{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x}{/tex}
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x\right)+2 \sin x \cos x}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex} Let sinx + cosx = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (cosx-sinx)dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x} d x=\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac{d t}{t^{2}}{/tex} = -t -1 + C {tex}=-\frac{1}{t}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x} d x = \frac{-1}{\sin x+\cos x}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, 
{tex}\frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x\right)+2 \sin x \cos x}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}}{/tex} 
Let sinx + cosx = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (cosx-sinx)dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x} d x=\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{(\sin x+\cos x)^{2}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int \frac{d t}{t^{2}}{/tex} 
= -t-1 + C
{tex}=-\frac{1}{t}+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{1+\sin 2 x} d x = \frac{-1}{\sin x+\cos x}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function tan 3 2x sec 2x
solution_text: Clearly, tan 3 2x sec 2x = tan 2 2x tan 2x sec 2x = (sec 2 2x - 1) tan 2x sec 2x = sec 2 2x.tan 2x sec 2x - tan 2x sec 2x {tex}\Rightarrow \int \tan ^{3} 2 x \sec 2 x d x=\int \sec ^{2} 2 x \tan 2 x \sec 2 x d x-\int \tan 2 x \sec 2 x d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^{2} 2 x \tan 2 x \sec 2 x d x-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex} Now, Let sec 2x = t ⇒ 2 sec 2x tan 2x dx = dt Thus, {tex}\int \tan ^{3} 2 x \sec 2 x d x=\frac{1}{2} \int t^{2} d t-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{t^{3}}{6}-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{(\sec 2 x)^{3}}{6}-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, tan3 2x sec 2x = tan2 2x tan 2x sec 2x
= (sec2 2x - 1) tan 2x sec 2x
= sec2 2x.tan 2x sec 2x - tan 2x sec 2x
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \tan ^{3} 2 x \sec 2 x d x=\int \sec ^{2} 2 x \tan 2 x \sec 2 x d x-\int \tan 2 x \sec 2 x d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int \sec ^{2} 2 x \tan 2 x \sec 2 x d x-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex} 
Now, Let sec 2x = t
⇒ 2 sec 2x tan 2x dx = dt
Thus, {tex}\int \tan ^{3} 2 x \sec 2 x d x=\frac{1}{2} \int t^{2} d t-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{t^{3}}{6}-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{(\sec 2 x)^{3}}{6}-\frac{\sec 2 x}{2}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function tan 4 x
solution_text: tan 4 x = tan 2 x.tan 2 x = (sec 2 x - 1) tan 2 x = sec 2 x tan 2 x - tan 2 x = sec 2 x tan 2 x - (sec 2 x - 1) = sec 2 x tan 2 x - sec 2 x + 1 Now, {tex}\int \tan ^{4} x d x=\int \sec ^{2} x \tan ^{2} x d x-\int \sec ^{2} x d x+\int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^{2} x \tan ^{2} x d x-\tan x+x+C{/tex} Now, let tanx = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec 2 x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \sec ^{2} x \tan ^{2} x d x=\int t^{2} d t=\frac{t^{3}}{3}=\frac{\tan ^{3} x}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \tan ^{4} x d x=\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{3} x-\tan x+x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
tan4 x = tan2 x.tan2 x
= (sec2 x - 1) tan2 x
= sec2 x tan2 x - tan2 x
= sec2 x tan2 x - (sec2 x - 1)
= sec2 x tan2 x - sec2 x + 1
Now, {tex}\int \tan ^{4} x d x=\int \sec ^{2} x \tan ^{2} x d x-\int \sec ^{2} x d x+\int 1 d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int \sec ^{2} x \tan ^{2} x d x-\tan x+x+C{/tex} 
Now, let tanx = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec2x dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \sec ^{2} x \tan ^{2} x d x=\int t^{2} d t=\frac{t^{3}}{3}=\frac{\tan ^{3} x}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \tan ^{4} x d x=\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{3} x-\tan x+x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{{{{\sin }^3}x + {{\cos }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{{{{\sin }^3}x + {{\cos }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{{{{\sin }^3}x + {{\cos }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}} + \frac{{{{\cos }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}}dx}{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\sin x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}} + \frac{{\cos x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x\cos x}} + \frac{{\cos x}}{{\sin x\sin x}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\left( {\tan x\sec x + \cot x\cos ecx} \right)dx} {/tex} = sec x - cosecx + c {tex}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\frac{{{{\sin }^3}x + {{\cos }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}} + \frac{{{{\cos }^3}x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x{{\cos }^2}x}}dx}{/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{\sin x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}} + \frac{{\cos x}}{{{{\sin }^2}x}}dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {\frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x\cos x}} + \frac{{\cos x}}{{\sin x\sin x}}dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {\left( {\tan x\sec x + \cot x\cos ecx} \right)dx} {/tex}

= sec x - cosecx + c

{tex}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrals of the function {tex}\frac{{\cos 2x + 2{{\sin }^2}x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integrals of the function {tex}\frac{{\cos 2x + 2{{\sin }^2}x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{{\cos 2x + 2{{\sin }^2}x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {1 - 2{{\sin }^2}x} \right) + 2{{\sin }^2}x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex} using {tex}\cos 2\theta = 1 - 2{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{1}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {{{\sec }^2}xdx} {/tex} = tan x + c
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\frac{{\cos 2x + 2{{\sin }^2}x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {1 - 2{{\sin }^2}x} \right) + 2{{\sin }^2}x}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex}

using {tex}\cos 2\theta = 1 - 2{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex}

{tex} = \int {\frac{1}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {{{\sec }^2}xdx} {/tex}

= tan x + c

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrals of the function {tex}\frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integrals of the function {tex}\frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x}{/tex}
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x}=\frac{\sin x}{\cos ^{3} x}+\frac{1}{\sin x \cos x}{/tex} {tex}= \tan x \sec ^{2} x+\frac{\frac{1}{\cos ^{2} x}}{\frac{\sin x \cos x}{\cos ^{2} x}}{/tex} {tex}= \tan x \sec ^{2} x+\frac{\sec ^{2} x}{\tan x}{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x} d x=\int \tan x \sec ^{2} x d x+\int \frac{\sec ^{2} x}{\tan x} d x{/tex} Let tan x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec 2 x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x} d x=\int \operatorname{td} t+\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac{t^{2}}{2}+\log |t|+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{2} x+\log |\tan x|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, {tex}\frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x}=\frac{\sin x}{\cos ^{3} x}+\frac{1}{\sin x \cos x}{/tex} 
{tex}= \tan x \sec ^{2} x+\frac{\frac{1}{\cos ^{2} x}}{\frac{\sin x \cos x}{\cos ^{2} x}}{/tex} 
{tex}= \tan x \sec ^{2} x+\frac{\sec ^{2} x}{\tan x}{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x} d x=\int \tan x \sec ^{2} x d x+\int \frac{\sec ^{2} x}{\tan x} d x{/tex} 
Let tan x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec2 x dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sin x \cos ^{3} x} d x=\int \operatorname{td} t+\int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{t^{2}}{2}+\log |t|+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{2} x+\log |\tan x|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}}{/tex}
question_html: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}}{/tex}
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}}=\frac{\cos 2 x}{\cos ^{2} x+\sin ^{2} x+2 \sin x \cos x}=\frac{\cos 2 x}{1+\sin 2 x}{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}} d x=\int \frac{\cos 2 x}{1+\sin 2 x} d x{/tex} Let 1 + sin2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2cos2x dx = dt Thus, {tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}} d x=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \log |\mathrm{t}|+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \log |1+\sin 2 x|+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \log \left|(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}\right|+C{/tex} = log|sinx + cosx| + C
solution_html:
Clearly, {tex}\frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}}=\frac{\cos 2 x}{\cos ^{2} x+\sin ^{2} x+2 \sin x \cos x}=\frac{\cos 2 x}{1+\sin 2 x}{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}} d x=\int \frac{\cos 2 x}{1+\sin 2 x} d x{/tex} 
Let 1 + sin2x = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2cos2x dx = dt
Thus, {tex}\int \frac{\cos 2 x}{(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}} d x=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{t} d t{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \log |\mathrm{t}|+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \log |1+\sin 2 x|+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \log \left|(\cos x+\sin x)^{2}\right|+C{/tex} 
= log|sinx + cosx| + C

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integrate of the function sin -1 (cos x)
question_html: Find the integrate of the function sin-1(cos x)
solution_text: {tex}\int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{\pi }{2}dx - \int x dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{\pi }{2}\int {1dx - \int {xdx} } {/tex} {tex}= \frac{\pi }{2}x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\cos x} \right)dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {\frac{\pi }{2}dx - \int x dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{\pi }{2}\int {1dx - \int {xdx} } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{\pi }{2}x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Find the integral of the function {tex}\frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}{/tex}
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\frac{\sin [(x-b)-(x-a)]}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\frac{\sin (x-b) \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[\tan (x-b)-\tan (x-a)]{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int[\tan (x-b)-\tan (x-a)] d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[-\log |\cos (x-b)|+\log |\cos (x-a)|]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\log \left|\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right|\right]+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, {tex}\frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\frac{\sin [(x-b)-(x-a)]}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\frac{\sin (x-b) \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[\tan (x-b)-\tan (x-a)]{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int[\tan (x-b)-\tan (x-a)] d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[-\log |\cos (x-b)|+\log |\cos (x-a)|]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\log \left|\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right|\right]+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: {tex}\int \frac{\sin ^{2} x-\cos ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} is equals to
question_html: {tex}\int \frac{\sin ^{2} x-\cos ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} is equals to

options.explanation: {tex}\int \frac{\sin ^{2} x-\cos ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x=\int\left(\frac{\sin ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x}-\frac{\cos ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(\sec ^{2} x-cosec ^{2} x\right) d x{/tex} We know that, {tex}\int \sec ^{2} x d x{/tex} = tan x + C, and {tex}\int cosec ^{2} x d x{/tex} = - cot x + C {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\sin ^{2} x-\cos ^{2} x}{\sin ^{2} x \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} = tanx + cotx + C

Let x.e x = t Differentiating both sides we get, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (e x .x + e x .1)dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} e x (x + 1)dx = dt Now, {tex}\int \frac{e^{x}(1+x)}{\cos ^{2}\left(e^{x} x\right)} d x=\int \frac{d t}{\cos ^{2} t}{/tex} = {tex}\int \sec ^{2} t \cdot d t{/tex} = tan t + C = tan(e x .x) + C

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let x 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x 2 dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{3 x^{2}}{x^{6}+1} d x=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+1}{/tex} = tan -1 t + C = tan -1 (x 3 ) + C
solution_html:
Let x3 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x2 dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{3 x^{2}}{x^{6}+1} d x=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+1}{/tex} 
= tan-1t + C
= tan-1(x3) + C

solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 + 4{x^2}} }}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {2x} \right)}^2} + {{\left( 1 \right)}^2}} }}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\log \left| {\left( {2x} \right) + \sqrt {{{\left( {2x} \right)}^2} + {1^2}} } \right|}}{2}+c{/tex} ...[dividing by 2 as coefficient of x is 2] {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + {a^2}} }}dx = \log \left| {x + \sqrt {{x^2} + {a^2}} } \right|} } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {2x + \sqrt {4{x^2} + 1} } \right| + c{/tex}

solution_text: Let 2 - x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(2-x)^{2}+1}} d x=-\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t^{2}+1}}{/tex} {tex}=-[\log |\mathrm{t}+\sqrt{\mathrm{t}^{2}+1}|]+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} =\log |t+\sqrt{t^{2}+1}|^{-1}+C \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} =\log \frac{1}{|t+\sqrt{t^{2}+1}|}+C \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}=\log \left|\frac{1}{(2-x)+\sqrt{x^{2}-4 x+5}}\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Here, {tex}\int \frac{x-1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x=\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex} For {tex}\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex} , Let x 2 - 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{t}}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int t^{-\frac{1}{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[2 t^{\frac{1}{2}}\right]=\sqrt{t}=\sqrt{x^{2}-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}-1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}= \sqrt{x^{2}-1}- \log |x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}|+C~~~{/tex}
solution_html:
Here, {tex}\int \frac{x-1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x=\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex}
For {tex}\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex} , Let x2 - 1 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{t}}}{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int t^{-\frac{1}{2}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[2 t^{\frac{1}{2}}\right]=\sqrt{t}=\sqrt{x^{2}-1}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}-1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}= \sqrt{x^{2}-1}- \log |x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}|+C~~~{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{\sqrt {{x^6} + {a^6}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}\int {\frac{{{3x^2}}}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {{x^3}} \right)}^2} + {a^6}} }}dx} {/tex} ...(i) Putting x 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow 3{x^2} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3{x^2}dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \frac{1}{3}\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt {t^2+a^6}}{/tex} {tex}I = \frac{1}{3}\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt {t^2+(a^3)^2}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}\log \left| {t + \sqrt {{t^2} + {{\left( {{a^3}} \right)}^2}} } \right| + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}\log \left| {{x^3} + \sqrt {{x^6} + {a^6}} } \right| + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{\sqrt {{x^6} + {a^6}} }}dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{3}\int {\frac{{{3x^2}}}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {{x^3}} \right)}^2} + {a^6}} }}dx} {/tex} ...(i)
Putting x3 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow 3{x^2} = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 3{x^2}dx = dt{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i),
{tex}I = \frac{1}{3}\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt {t^2+a^6}}{/tex}
{tex}I = \frac{1}{3}\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt {t^2+(a^3)^2}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{3}\log \left| {t + \sqrt {{t^2} + {{\left( {{a^3}} \right)}^2}} } \right| + c{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{3}\log \left| {{x^3} + \sqrt {{x^6} + {a^6}} } \right| + c{/tex}

(i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt {{t^2} + 4} }}} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{t^2} + {{\left( 2 \right)}^2}} }}dt} {/tex} {tex} = \log \left| {t + \sqrt {{t^2} + {{\left( 2 \right)}^2}} } \right| + c{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {\tan x + \sqrt {{{\tan }^2}x + 4} } \right| + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+2 x+2}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x+1)^{2}+(1)^{2}}} d x{/tex} Let x + 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x+1)^{2}+(1)^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t^{2}+1}} d t{/tex} {tex}=\log |t+\sqrt{t^{2}+1}|+C{/tex} {tex}=\log |(x+1)+\sqrt{(x+1)^{2}+1}|+C{/tex} {tex}=\log |(x+1)+\sqrt{x^{2}+2 x+2}|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+2 x+2}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x+1)^{2}+(1)^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
Let x + 1 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x+1)^{2}+(1)^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t^{2}+1}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}=\log |t+\sqrt{t^{2}+1}|+C{/tex} 
{tex}=\log |(x+1)+\sqrt{(x+1)^{2}+1}|+C{/tex} 
{tex}=\log |(x+1)+\sqrt{x^{2}+2 x+2}|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}9 x^{2}+6 x+5{/tex} = {tex}(3 x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{9 x^{2}+6 x+5} d x=\int \frac{1}{(3 x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}} d x{/tex} Let 3x + 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3dx = dt {tex}\therefore~~ \int \frac{1}{(3 x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}} d x=\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{1}{t^{2}+2^{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right)\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{6} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{x}+1}{2}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, {tex}9 x^{2}+6 x+5{/tex} = {tex}(3 x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{9 x^{2}+6 x+5} d x=\int \frac{1}{(3 x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}} d x{/tex} 
Let 3x + 1 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3dx = dt
{tex}\therefore~~ \int \frac{1}{(3 x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}} d x=\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{1}{t^{2}+2^{2}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right)\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{6} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{x}+1}{2}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {7 - 6x - {x^2}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} - 6x + 7} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left( {{x^2} + 6x - 7} \right)} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left( {{x^2} + 6x + 9 - 9 - 7} \right)} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left\{ {{{\left( {x + 3} \right)}^2} - 16} \right\}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( 16 \right)}} - {{\left( {x + 3} \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( 4 \right)}^2} - {{\left( {x + 3} \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{x + 3}}{4}} \right) + c{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \int\ {\frac{1}{\sqrt{{a^2} - {x^2}}}dx = {{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{a}} } \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Clearly, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x-1)(x-2)}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-3 x+2}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} d x{/tex} Let {tex}x-\frac{3}{2}=t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(t)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} d t{/tex} {tex}= \log |t+\sqrt{(t)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}|+C{/tex} {tex}= \log \left|\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)+\sqrt{x^{2}-3 x+2}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x-1)(x-2)}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-3 x+2}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
Let {tex}x-\frac{3}{2}=t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(t)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}= \log |t+\sqrt{(t)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}|+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \log \left|\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)+\sqrt{x^{2}-3 x+2}\right|+C{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {8 + 3x - {x^2}} }}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} + 3x + 8} }}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left( {{x^2} - 3x - 8} \right)} }}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left\{ {{x^2} - 3x + {{\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)}^2} - {{\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)}^2} - 8} \right\}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left\{ {{{\left( {x - \frac{3}{2}} \right)}^2} - \frac{{41}}{4}} \right\}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {\frac{{\sqrt {41} }}{2}} \right)}^2} - {{\left( {x - \frac{3}{2}} \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}\frac{{x - \frac{3}{2}}}{{\frac{{\sqrt {41} }}{2}}} + c{/tex} {tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x - 3}}{{\sqrt {41} }}} \right) + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {8 + 3x - {x^2}} }}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - {x^2} + 3x + 8} }}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left( {{x^2} - 3x - 8} \right)} }}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt { - \left\{ {{x^2} - 3x + {{\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)}^2} - {{\left( {\frac{3}{2}} \right)}^2} - 8} \right\}} }}dx} {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: We know that, (x - a)(x - b) can be written as x 2 - (a + b)x + ab. Then, x 2 - (a + b)x + ab = x 2 - (a + b)x + {tex}\frac{(a+b)^{2}}{4}-\frac{(a+b)^{2}}{4}+a b{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[\mathrm{x}-\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}}{2}\right)\right]^{2}-\frac{(\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})^{2}}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{b})}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left\{\mathrm{x}-\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}}{2}\right)\right\}^{2}-\frac{(\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})^{2}}{4}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} Let {tex}x-\left(\frac{a+b}{2}\right)=t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left\{x-\left(\frac{a+b}{2}\right)\right\}^{2}-\frac{(a-b)^{2}}{4}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t^{2}-\frac{(a-b)^{2}}{4}}}{/tex} {tex}= \log |t+\sqrt{t^{2}-\frac{(a-b)^{2}}{4}}|+C{/tex} {tex}=\log \left|\left\{\mathrm{x}-\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}}{2}\right)\right\}+\sqrt{(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{b})}\right|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
We know that, (x - a)(x - b) can be written as x2 - (a + b)x + ab.
Then, x2 - (a + b)x + ab = x2 - (a + b)x + {tex}\frac{(a+b)^{2}}{4}-\frac{(a+b)^{2}}{4}+a b{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[\mathrm{x}-\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}}{2}\right)\right]^{2}-\frac{(\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})^{2}}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{b})}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left\{\mathrm{x}-\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}}{2}\right)\right\}^{2}-\frac{(\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})^{2}}{4}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
Let {tex}x-\left(\frac{a+b}{2}\right)=t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left\{x-\left(\frac{a+b}{2}\right)\right\}^{2}-\frac{(a-b)^{2}}{4}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t^{2}-\frac{(a-b)^{2}}{4}}}{/tex} 
{tex}= \log |t+\sqrt{t^{2}-\frac{(a-b)^{2}}{4}}|+C{/tex} 
{tex}=\log \left|\left\{\mathrm{x}-\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}}{2}\right)\right\}+\sqrt{(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{b})}\right|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

solution_text: The given Integrand is: Let 2x 2 + x – 3 =t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4x + 1) dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{4 x+1}{\sqrt{2 x^{2}+x-3}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}} d t{/tex} {tex}=2 \sqrt{t}+c{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{2 x^{2}+x-3}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
The given Integrand is: 

Let 2x2 + x – 3 =t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4x + 1) dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{4 x+1}{\sqrt{2 x^{2}+x-3}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}=2 \sqrt{t}+c{/tex} 
= {tex}2 \sqrt{2 x^{2}+x-3}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Clearly, we can write, x + 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}(2 x)+2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x=\int \frac{\frac{1}{2}(2 x)+2}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}+2 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} Now in, {tex}I_1{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex} Let x 2 - 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x)dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow I_1 = \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{t}}}=\frac{1}{2}[2 \sqrt{\mathrm{t}}]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[2 \sqrt{\mathrm{x^2-1}}]{/tex} And {tex}2 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x=2 \log |x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}+2}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}+2 \log |\mathrm{x}+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Clearly, we can write, x + 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}(2 x)+2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x=\int \frac{\frac{1}{2}(2 x)+2}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}+2 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
Now in, {tex}I_1{/tex}= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x{/tex} 
Let x2 - 1 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x)dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow I_1 = \frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{t}}}=\frac{1}{2}[2 \sqrt{\mathrm{t}}]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[2 \sqrt{\mathrm{x^2-1}}]{/tex} 
And {tex}2 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-1}} d x=2 \log |x+\sqrt{x^{2}-1}|{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}+2}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}} \mathrm{dx}=\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}+2 \log |\mathrm{x}+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-1}|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function {tex}\frac{5 x-2}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}}{/tex}
question_html: Integrate the function {tex}\frac{5 x-2}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}}{/tex}
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\frac{5 x-2}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}}{/tex} Let 5x - 2 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(1+2 x+3 x^{2}\right)+B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x - 2 = A(2 + 6x) + B Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get, 6A = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow A=\frac{5}{6}{/tex} 2A + B = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow B=-\frac{11}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 5 x-2=\frac{5}{6}(2+6 x)+\left(-\frac{11}{3}\right){/tex} {tex}\therefore~ \int \frac{5 x+1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x=\int \frac{\frac{5}{6}(2+6 x)-\frac{11}{3}}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{6} \int \frac{2+6 x}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x-\frac{11}{3} \int \frac{1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} Now, in {tex}\int \frac{2+6 x}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} Let 1 + 2x + 3x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 + 6x)dx = dt {tex}\therefore~\int \frac{2+6 \mathrm{x}}{1+2 \mathrm{x}+3 \mathrm{x}^{2}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{t}}=\log |\mathrm{t}|{/tex} = log|1 + 2x + 3x 2 | ....…(1) Also in {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}1+2 x+3 x^{2}=1+3\left(x^{2}+\frac{2}{3} x\right){/tex} {tex}=3\left[\left(\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{3}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}\right)^{2}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x=\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{1}{\left[\left(x+\frac{1}{3}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}\right)^{2}\right]} d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{1}{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+\frac{1}{3}}{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{x}+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 x+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex} ......(ii) Thus, from (1) and (2), we get, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x+1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x=\frac{5}{6}\left[\log \left|1+2 x+3 x^{2}\right|\right]-\frac{11}{3}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 x+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{6}\left[\log \left|1+2 \mathrm{x}+3 \mathrm{x}^{2}\right|\right]-\frac{11}{3 \sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{x}+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\frac{5 x-2}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}}{/tex}
Let 5x - 2 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(1+2 x+3 x^{2}\right)+B{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x - 2 = A(2 + 6x) + B
Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get,
6A = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow A=\frac{5}{6}{/tex}
 2A + B = -2 {tex}\Rightarrow B=-\frac{11}{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 5 x-2=\frac{5}{6}(2+6 x)+\left(-\frac{11}{3}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\therefore~ \int \frac{5 x+1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x=\int \frac{\frac{5}{6}(2+6 x)-\frac{11}{3}}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{5}{6} \int \frac{2+6 x}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x-\frac{11}{3} \int \frac{1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
Now, in {tex}\int \frac{2+6 x}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
Let 1 + 2x + 3x2 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 + 6x)dx = dt
{tex}\therefore~\int \frac{2+6 \mathrm{x}}{1+2 \mathrm{x}+3 \mathrm{x}^{2}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{t}}=\log |\mathrm{t}|{/tex} 
= log|1 + 2x + 3x2| ....…(1)
Also in {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}1+2 x+3 x^{2}=1+3\left(x^{2}+\frac{2}{3} x\right){/tex} 
{tex}=3\left[\left(\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{3}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}\right)^{2}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x=\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{1}{\left[\left(x+\frac{1}{3}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}\right)^{2}\right]} d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{1}{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+\frac{1}{3}}{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}}\right)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{3}\left[\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{x}+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right]{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 x+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right){/tex} ......(ii)
Thus, from (1) and (2), we get,
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x+1}{1+2 x+3 x^{2}} d x=\frac{5}{6}\left[\log \left|1+2 x+3 x^{2}\right|\right]-\frac{11}{3}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 x+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{5}{6}\left[\log \left|1+2 \mathrm{x}+3 \mathrm{x}^{2}\right|\right]-\frac{11}{3 \sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{x}+1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let 6x + 7 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}-9 x+20\right)+B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x + 7 = A(2x - 9) + B Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get, 2A = 6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 3 Also, -9A + B = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 34 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x + 7 = 3 (2x - 9) + 34 {tex}\therefore~ \int \frac{6 x+7}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{3(2 x-9)+34}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} {tex}= 3 \int \frac{2 x-9}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x+34 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} Now, in {tex}\int \frac{2 x-9}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} Let x 2 - 9x + 20 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 9)dx = dt {tex}\therefore \int \frac{2 x-9}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} {tex}=2 \sqrt{t}{/tex} {tex}=2 \sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}{/tex} ...(i) And in {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} , we have x 2 - 9x + 20 = {tex}x^{2}-9 x+20+\frac{81}{4}-\frac{81}{4}{/tex} {tex}= \left(x-\frac{9}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-9 \mathrm{x}+20}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(\mathrm{x}-\frac{9}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} = {tex}\log \left| (x-\frac{9}{2}\right)+\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20} |{/tex} .....(ii) Thus, from (i) and (ii), we get, {tex} \int \frac{6 x+7}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x=3[2 \sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}]+34\left[\log \left|\left(x-\frac{9}{2}\right)+\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}\right|\right]+C{/tex} {tex}= 6 \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-9 \mathrm{x}+20}+34 \log \left[\left(\mathrm{x}-\frac{9}{2}\right)+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-9 \mathrm{x}+20}\right]+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let 6x + 7 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}-9 x+20\right)+B{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x + 7 = A(2x - 9) + B
Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get,
2A = 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 3
Also, -9A + B = 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 34
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6x + 7 = 3 (2x - 9) + 34
{tex}\therefore~ \int \frac{6 x+7}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\int \frac{3(2 x-9)+34}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex}
{tex}= 3 \int \frac{2 x-9}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x+34 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} 
Now, in {tex}\int \frac{2 x-9}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex} 
Let x2 - 9x + 20 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 9)dx = dt
{tex}\therefore \int \frac{2 x-9}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \sqrt{t}{/tex} 
{tex}=2 \sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}{/tex}   ...(i)
And in {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x{/tex}, we have
x2 - 9x + 20 = {tex}x^{2}-9 x+20+\frac{81}{4}-\frac{81}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}= \left(x-\frac{9}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-9 \mathrm{x}+20}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(\mathrm{x}-\frac{9}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left| (x-\frac{9}{2}\right)+\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20} |{/tex} .....(ii)
Thus, from (i) and (ii), we get,
{tex} \int \frac{6 x+7}{\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}} d x=3[2 \sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}]+34\left[\log \left|\left(x-\frac{9}{2}\right)+\sqrt{x^{2}-9 x+20}\right|\right]+C{/tex} 
{tex}= 6 \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-9 \mathrm{x}+20}+34 \log \left[\left(\mathrm{x}-\frac{9}{2}\right)+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}-9 \mathrm{x}+20}\right]+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let x + 2 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(4 x-x^{2}\right)+B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 2 = A(4 -2x) + B Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get, -2A = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow A=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and 4A + B = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow x+2=-\frac{1}{2}(4-2 x)+4{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{-\frac{1}{2}(4-2 x)+4}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow -\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(4-2 x)}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x+4 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} Now, let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{(4-2 x)}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} Let 4x - x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4 - 2x) dx = dt {tex}\therefore \int \frac{(4-2 x)}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}=2 \sqrt{t}=2 \sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}{/tex} .......(i) And, Now let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} Then, 4x - x 2 = -(-4x + x 2 ) = (-4x + x 2 + 4 - 4) = 4 - (x - 2) 2 = (2) 2 - (x - 2) 2 {tex}\therefore \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right){/tex} ......(ii) using eq. (i) and (ii), we get, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=-\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}+4 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let x + 2 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(4 x-x^{2}\right)+B{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 2 = A(4 -2x) + B
Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get,
-2A = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow A=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
and 4A + B = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow x+2=-\frac{1}{2}(4-2 x)+4{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{-\frac{1}{2}(4-2 x)+4}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow -\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(4-2 x)}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x+4 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
Now, let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{(4-2 x)}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
Let 4x - x2 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (4 - 2x) dx = dt
{tex}\therefore \int \frac{(4-2 x)}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}=2 \sqrt{t}=2 \sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}{/tex} .......(i)
And, Now let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
Then, 4x - x2 = -(-4x + x2)
= (-4x + x2 + 4 - 4)
= 4 - (x - 2)2 
= (2)2 - (x - 2)2 
{tex}\therefore \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right){/tex} ......(ii)
using eq. (i) and (ii), we get,
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}} d x=-\sqrt{4 x-x^{2}}+4 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}{2}\right)+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex} Let Linear = A {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{/tex} (Quadratic) + B {tex}\Rightarrow x + 2 = A\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + 2x + 3} \right) + B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = A(2x + 2) + B …(ii) {tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = 2Ax + 2A + B Comparing coefficients of x, 2A = 1 {tex} \Rightarrow A = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} Comparing constants, 2A + B = 2 On solving, we get {tex}A = \frac{1}{2},{/tex} B = 1 Putting the values of A and B in eq. (ii), {tex}x + 2 = \frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1{/tex} Putting this value of x+2 in eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{{\frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx + \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} } {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}{I_1} + {I_2}{/tex} …(iii) Now {tex}{I_1} = \int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}} dx{/tex} Putting x 2 + 2x + 3 = t {tex} \Rightarrow 2x + 2 = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} (2x + 2)dx = dt {tex}\therefore {I_1} = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \int {{t^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}dt} } {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}}}{{\frac{1}{2}}} = 2\sqrt t {/tex} {tex} = 2\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} {/tex} …(iv) Again {tex}{I_2} = \int {\frac{1}{\sqrt{{x^2} + 2x + 3}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 1 + 2} }}} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} } \right|{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right|{/tex} …(v) Putting values of I 1 and I 2 in eq. (iii), {tex}I = \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} + \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right| + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex}

Let Linear = A {tex}\frac{d}{{dx}}{/tex} (Quadratic) + B

{tex}\Rightarrow x + 2 = A\frac{d}{{dx}}\left( {{x^2} + 2x + 3} \right) + B{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = A(2x + 2) + B …(ii)

{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} x + 2 = 2Ax + 2A + B

Comparing coefficients of x, 2A = 1 {tex} \Rightarrow A = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

Comparing constants, 2A + B = 2

On solving, we get {tex}A = \frac{1}{2},{/tex} B = 1

Putting the values of A and B in eq. (ii),

{tex}x + 2 = \frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1{/tex}

Putting this value of x+2 in eq. (i),

{tex}I = \int {\frac{{\frac{1}{2}\left( {2x + 2} \right) + 1}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} {/tex}

{tex}I = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx + \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}dx} } {/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}{I_1} + {I_2}{/tex} …(iii)

Now {tex}{I_1} = \int {\frac{{2x + 2}}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} }}} dx{/tex}

Putting x2 + 2x + 3 = t

{tex} \Rightarrow 2x + 2 = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} (2x + 2)dx = dt

{tex}\therefore {I_1} = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \int {{t^{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}dt} } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}}}{{\frac{1}{2}}} = 2\sqrt t {/tex}

{tex} = 2\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} {/tex} …(iv)

Again {tex}{I_2} = \int {\frac{1}{\sqrt{{x^2} + 2x + 3}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 1 + 2} }}} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} }}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{{\left( {x + 1} \right)}^2} + {{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} } \right|{/tex}

{tex}= \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right|{/tex}…(v)

Putting values of I1 and I2 in eq. (iii),

{tex}I = \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} + \log \left| {x + 1 + \sqrt {{x^2} + 2x + 3} } \right| + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let x + 3 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}-2 x-5\right)+B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 3 = A(2x - 2) + B Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get, 2A = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow A=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} -2A + B = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow x+3=\frac{1}{2}(2 x-2)+4{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \frac{x+3}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\int \frac{\frac{1}{2}(2 x-2)+4}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x-2}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x+4 \int \frac{1}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} Now, Let us consider {tex}\int \frac{2 x-2}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} Let x 2 - 2x - 5 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 2)dx = dt {tex}\therefore \int \frac{2 x-2}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\int \frac{d t}{t}=\log |t|=\log \left|x^{2}-2 x-5\right|{/tex} .......(i) And, now let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{1}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}-2 x+1\right)-6} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{2}+(\sqrt{6})^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{6}} \log \left(\frac{x-1-\sqrt{6}}{x-1+\sqrt{6}}\right){/tex} ......(ii) Using eq. (i) and (ii), we get, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+3}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\frac{1}{2} \log \left|x^{2}-2 x-5\right|+\frac{4}{2 \sqrt{6}} \log \left(\frac{x-1-\sqrt{6}}{x-1+\sqrt{6}}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+3}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\frac{1}{2} \log \left|x^{2}-2 x-5\right|+\frac{2}{\sqrt{6}} \log \left(\frac{x-1-\sqrt{6}}{x-1+\sqrt{6}}\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let x + 3 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}-2 x-5\right)+B{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 3 = A(2x - 2) + B
Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get,
2A = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow A=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
-2A + B = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow x+3=\frac{1}{2}(2 x-2)+4{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \frac{x+3}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\int \frac{\frac{1}{2}(2 x-2)+4}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x-2}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x+4 \int \frac{1}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} 
Now, Let us consider {tex}\int \frac{2 x-2}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} 
Let x2 - 2x - 5 = t
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 2)dx = dt
{tex}\therefore \int \frac{2 x-2}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\int \frac{d t}{t}=\log |t|=\log \left|x^{2}-2 x-5\right|{/tex} .......(i)
And, now let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{1}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}-2 x+1\right)-6} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{2}+(\sqrt{6})^{2}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{6}} \log \left(\frac{x-1-\sqrt{6}}{x-1+\sqrt{6}}\right){/tex} ......(ii)
Using eq. (i) and (ii), we get,
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+3}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\frac{1}{2} \log \left|x^{2}-2 x-5\right|+\frac{4}{2 \sqrt{6}} \log \left(\frac{x-1-\sqrt{6}}{x-1+\sqrt{6}}\right)+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{x+3}{x^{2}-2 x-5} d x=\frac{1}{2} \log \left|x^{2}-2 x-5\right|+\frac{2}{\sqrt{6}} \log \left(\frac{x-1-\sqrt{6}}{x-1+\sqrt{6}}\right)+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let 5x + 3 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}+4 x+10\right)+B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x + 3 = A(2x + 4) + B Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get, 2A = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow A=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} 4A + B = 3 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = -7 {tex}\Rightarrow 5 x+3=\frac{5}{2}(2 x+4)-7{/tex} Again, {tex}\int \frac{5 x+3}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\int \frac{\frac{5}{2}(2 x+4)-7}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5}{2} \int \frac{2 x+4}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x-7 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} Now, let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{2 x+4}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} Let x 2 + 4x + 10 = t ⇒ (2x + 4) dx = dt {tex}\therefore \int \frac{2 x+4}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt t}=2 \sqrt{t}=2 \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}{/tex} ......(i) And, Now let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\int \frac{1}{(\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+4})+\sqrt6} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{(x+2)^{2}+(\sqrt{6})^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\log |(x+2) \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}|{/tex} .....(ii) using eq. (i) and (ii), we get, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x+3}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\frac{5}{2}[2 \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}]-7 \log (x+2) \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10} |+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x+3}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=5 \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}-7 \log |(x+2) \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let 5x + 3 = {tex}A \frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}+4 x+10\right)+B{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x + 3 = A(2x + 4) + B
Now, equating the coefficients of x and constant term on both sides, we get,
2A = 5
{tex}\Rightarrow A=\frac{5}{2}{/tex} 
4A + B = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = -7
{tex}\Rightarrow 5 x+3=\frac{5}{2}(2 x+4)-7{/tex} 
Again, {tex}\int \frac{5 x+3}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\int \frac{\frac{5}{2}(2 x+4)-7}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5}{2} \int \frac{2 x+4}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x-7 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} 
Now, let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{2 x+4}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} 
Let x2 + 4x + 10 = t
⇒ (2x + 4) dx = dt
{tex}\therefore \int \frac{2 x+4}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt t}=2 \sqrt{t}=2 \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}{/tex} ......(i)
And, Now let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\int \frac{1}{(\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+4})+\sqrt6} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{(x+2)^{2}+(\sqrt{6})^{2}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}=\log |(x+2) \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}|{/tex} .....(ii)
using eq. (i) and (ii), we get, 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x+3}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=\frac{5}{2}[2 \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}]-7 \log (x+2) \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10} |+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x+3}{\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}} d x=5 \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}-7 \log |(x+2) \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+10}|+C{/tex}

options.explanation: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}+2 x+2}=\int \frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+2 x+1\right)+1}{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac{1}{(x+1)^{2}+(1)^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\left[\tan ^{-1}(x+1)\right]+C{/tex}

{tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{9 x-4 x^{2}}}=\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{-4\left(x^{2}-\frac{9}{4} x\right)}}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{-4\left(x^{2}-\frac{9}{4} x+\frac{81}{64}-\frac{81}{64}\right)}}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{-4\left[\left(x-\frac{9}{8}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{9}{8}\right)^{2}\right.}]}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-\frac{9}{8}}{\frac{9}{8}}\right)\right]+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{8 x-9}{9}\right)\right]+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{x}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = A(x + 2) + B(x + 1) On comparing the coefficients of x and constant term, we get, A + B = 1 2A + B = 0 On solving above two equations, we get, A = -1 and B = 2 Thus, {tex}\frac{x}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\frac{-1}{(x+1)}+\frac{2}{(x+2)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{x}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\int\left\{\frac{-1}{(x+1)}+\frac{2}{(x+2)}\right\} d x{/tex} = -log|x + 1| + 2 log|x + 2| + C = log (x + 2) 2 - log |x + 1| + C = {tex}\log \frac{(x+2)^{2}}{(x+1)}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{x}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = A(x + 2) + B(x + 1)
On comparing the coefficients of x and constant term, we get,
A + B = 1
2A + B = 0
On solving above two equations, we get,
A = -1 and B = 2
Thus,
{tex}\frac{x}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\frac{-1}{(x+1)}+\frac{2}{(x+2)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}\int \frac{x}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\int\left\{\frac{-1}{(x+1)}+\frac{2}{(x+2)}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= -log|x + 1| + 2 log|x + 2| + C
= log (x + 2)2 - log |x + 1| + C
= {tex}\log \frac{(x+2)^{2}}{(x+1)}+C{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - 9}}dx} {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - {3^2}}}dx}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{2 \times 3}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 3}}{{x + 3}}} \right| + c{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - {a^2}}}dx = \frac{1}{{2a}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - a}}{{x + a}}} \right|} } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{6}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 3}}{{x + 3}}} \right| + c{/tex} . Which is the required solution.
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - 9}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}=\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - {3^2}}}dx}{/tex}
