subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-2)}+\frac{C}{(x-3)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x -1 = A(x - 2)(x - 3) + B(x - 1)(x - 3) + C(x - 1)(x - 2) ........(i) Substituting x = 1, 2 and 3 respectively in equation (i), we get, A = 1, B = -5 and C = 4 Thus, {tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{1}{(x-1)}-\frac{5}{(x-2)}+\frac{4}{(x-3)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow~\int \frac{3 x-1}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{(x-1)}-\frac{5}{(x-2)}+\frac{4}{(x-3)}\right\} d x{/tex} = log|x - 1| - 5 log|x - 2| + 4 log|x - 3| + C
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-2)}+\frac{C}{(x-3)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x -1 = A(x - 2)(x - 3) + B(x - 1)(x - 3) + C(x - 1)(x - 2) ........(i)
Substituting x = 1, 2 and 3 respectively in equation (i), we get,
A = 1, B = -5 and C = 4
Thus,
{tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{1}{(x-1)}-\frac{5}{(x-2)}+\frac{4}{(x-3)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow~\int \frac{3 x-1}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{(x-1)}-\frac{5}{(x-2)}+\frac{4}{(x-3)}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= log|x - 1| - 5 log|x - 2| + 4 log|x - 3| + C

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-2)}+\frac{C}{(x-3)}{/tex} ⇒ x = A(x - 2)(x - 3) + B(x - 1)(x - 3) + C(x - 1)(x - 2) ......(i) Substituting x = 1, 2 and 3 respectively in equation (1), we get, {tex}A=\frac{1}{2}, B=-2{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{3}{2}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{2}{(x-2)}+\frac{3}{2(x-3)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{2}{(x-2)}+\frac{3}{2(x-3)}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|-2 \log |x-2|+\frac{3}{2} \log |x-3|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-2)}+\frac{C}{(x-3)}{/tex} 
⇒ x = A(x - 2)(x - 3) + B(x - 1)(x - 3) + C(x - 1)(x - 2) ......(i)
Substituting x = 1, 2 and 3 respectively in equation (1), we get,
{tex}A=\frac{1}{2}, B=-2{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{3}{2}{/tex} 
Thus, 
{tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)}=\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{2}{(x-2)}+\frac{3}{2(x-3)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)(x-3)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{2(x-1)}-\frac{2}{(x-2)}+\frac{3}{2(x-3)}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|-2 \log |x-2|+\frac{3}{2} \log |x-3|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2}{/tex}
question_html: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2}{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = A(x + 2)+ B(x + 1) …(i) Substituting x = -1 and -2 respectively in equation (i), we get, A = -2, B = 4 Thus, {tex}\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2}=\frac{-2}{(x+1)}+\frac{4}{(x+2)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2} d x=\int\left\{\frac{-2}{(x+1)}+\frac{4}{(x+2)}\right\} d x{/tex} = 4 log|x + 2| - 2 log|x + 1| + C
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = A(x + 2)+ B(x + 1) …(i)
Substituting x = -1 and -2 respectively in equation (i), we get, 
A = -2, B = 4
Thus,
{tex}\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2}=\frac{-2}{(x+1)}+\frac{4}{(x+2)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+3 x+2} d x=\int\left\{\frac{-2}{(x+1)}+\frac{4}{(x+2)}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= 4 log|x + 2| - 2 log|x + 1| + C

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: On dividing 1 - x 2 by x(1 - 2x), we get, {tex}\frac{1-x^{2}}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2-x}{x(1-2 x)}\right){/tex} .......(i) Now, let {tex}\frac{2-x}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{A}{x}+\frac{B}{(1-2 x)}{/tex} (2 - x) = A(1 - 2x) + Bx …...(ii) Now, substituting x = 0 and {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} in equation (ii), we get, A = 2 and B = 3 Thus, {tex}\frac{2-x}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{(1-2 x)}{/tex} Now, putting this value in equation (ii), we get, {tex}\frac{1-x^{2}}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{(1-2 x)}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1-x^{2}}{x(1-2 x)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{(1-2 x)}\right)\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{2}+\log |x|+\frac{3}{2(-2)} \log |1-2 x|+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{2}+\log |x|-\frac{3}{4} \log |1-2 x|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
On dividing 1 - x2 by x(1 - 2x), we get,

{tex}\frac{1-x^{2}}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2-x}{x(1-2 x)}\right){/tex}.......(i)
Now, let {tex}\frac{2-x}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{A}{x}+\frac{B}{(1-2 x)}{/tex} 
(2 - x) = A(1 - 2x) + Bx …...(ii)
Now, substituting x = 0 and {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} in equation (ii), we get,
A = 2 and B = 3
Thus, {tex}\frac{2-x}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{(1-2 x)}{/tex} 
Now, putting this value in equation (ii), we get,
{tex}\frac{1-x^{2}}{x(1-2 x)}=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{(1-2 x)}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{1-x^{2}}{x(1-2 x)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{(1-2 x)}\right)\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x}{2}+\log |x|+\frac{3}{2(-2)} \log |1-2 x|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x}{2}+\log |x|-\frac{3}{4} \log |1-2 x|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C}{(x-1)}{/tex} x = (Ax + B)(x - 1) + C (x 2 + 1) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = Ax 2 - Ax + Bx - B + Cx 2 + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = (A+C)x 2 - (A-B)x - (B+C) Equating the coefficients of x 2 , x and constant term, we get, A + C = 0 -A + B = 1 -B + C = 0 On solving these equation, we get, {tex}A=-\frac{1}{2}, B=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)}=\frac{\left(-\frac{1}{2} x+\frac{1}{2}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{(x-1)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow~~\int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)} d x=-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{x^{2}+1} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{x-1} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|+C{/tex} Now, let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} Let (x 2 + 1) = t 2xdx = dt Thus, {tex}\int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)} d x=-\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\mathrm{x}^{2}+1\right|+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|-\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{2}+1\right|+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let  {tex}\frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C}{(x-1)}{/tex} 
x = (Ax + B)(x - 1) + C (x2 + 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = Ax2 - Ax + Bx - B + Cx2 + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = (A+C)x2 - (A-B)x - (B+C)
Equating the coefficients of x2, x and constant term, we get,
A + C = 0
-A + B = 1
-B + C = 0
On solving these equation, we get,
{tex}A=-\frac{1}{2}, B=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
Thus, 
{tex}\frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)}=\frac{\left(-\frac{1}{2} x+\frac{1}{2}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{\frac{1}{2}}{(x-1)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow~~\int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)} d x=-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{x^{2}+1} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{x-1} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|+C{/tex} 
Now, let us consider, {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} Let (x2 + 1) = t
2xdx = dt
Thus,
{tex}\int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)(x-1)} d x=-\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\mathrm{x}^{2}+1\right|+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|+C{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|-\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{2}+1\right|+\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)^{2}(x+2)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{C}{(x+2)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = A(x - 1)(x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C( x - 1) 2 …(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = (A+C)x 2 + (A+3B-2C)x - 2A+2B+C Substituting x = 1 in equation (i), we get, {tex}B=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} Equating the coefficients of x 2 and constant term, we get, A + C = 0 -2A + 2B + C = 0 {tex}A=\frac{2}{9}{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{-2}{9}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)^{2}(x+2)}=\frac{2}{9(x-1)}+\frac{1}{3(x-1)^{2}}-\frac{2}{9(x+2)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{x}{(x-1)^{2}(x+2)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{2}{9(x-1)}+\frac{1}{3(x-1)^{2}}-\frac{2}{9(x+2)}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{9} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x+\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{2}} d x-\frac{2}{9} \int \frac{1}{(x+2)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{9} \log |x-1|+\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{-1}{x-1}\right)-\frac{2}{9} \log |x+2|+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{9} \log \left|\frac{x-1}{x+2}\right|+\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{-1}{x-1}\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)^{2}(x+2)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{C}{(x+2)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = A(x - 1)(x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C( x - 1)2 …(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = (A+C)x2 + (A+3B-2C)x - 2A+2B+C
Substituting x = 1 in equation (i), we get,
{tex}B=\frac{1}{3}{/tex} 
Equating the coefficients of x2 and constant term, we get,
A + C = 0
-2A + 2B + C = 0
{tex}A=\frac{2}{9}{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{-2}{9}{/tex}
Thus,
{tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)^{2}(x+2)}=\frac{2}{9(x-1)}+\frac{1}{3(x-1)^{2}}-\frac{2}{9(x+2)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{x}{(x-1)^{2}(x+2)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{2}{9(x-1)}+\frac{1}{3(x-1)^{2}}-\frac{2}{9(x+2)}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{9} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x+\frac{1}{3} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{2}} d x-\frac{2}{9} \int \frac{1}{(x+2)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{9} \log |x-1|+\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{-1}{x-1}\right)-\frac{2}{9} \log |x+2|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{9} \log \left|\frac{x-1}{x+2}\right|+\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{-1}{x-1}\right)+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: We have, {tex}\frac{3 x+5}{x^{3}-x^{2}-x+1}=\frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{C}{(x+1)}{/tex} ⇒3x + 5 = A(x - 1)(x + 1) + B(x + 1) + C( x - 1) 2 ⇒3x + 5 = A(x 2 - 1) + B(x + 1) + C( x 2 + 1 - 2x) …(i) Substituting x = 1, in equation (i), we get, B = 4 Equating the coefficients of x 2 and x, we get, A + C = 0 B – 2C = 3 {tex}A=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)}=\frac{-1}{2(x-1)}+\frac{4}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{1}{2(x+1)}{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left\{\frac{-1}{2(x-1)}+\frac{4}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{1}{2(x+1)}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x+4 \int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{2}} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|+4\left(\frac{-1}{x-1}\right)+\frac{1}{2} \log |x+1|+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log \left|\frac{x+1}{x-1}\right|-\frac{4}{x-1}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\frac{3 x+5}{x^{3}-x^{2}-x+1}=\frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}\frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{C}{(x+1)}{/tex} 
⇒3x + 5 = A(x - 1)(x + 1) + B(x + 1) + C( x - 1)2
⇒3x + 5 = A(x2 - 1) + B(x + 1) + C( x2 + 1 - 2x) …(i)
Substituting x = 1, in equation (i), we get,
B = 4
Equating the coefficients of x2 and x, we get,
A + C = 0
B – 2C = 3
{tex}A=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}C=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
Thus,
{tex}\frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)}=\frac{-1}{2(x-1)}+\frac{4}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{1}{2(x+1)}{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\int \frac{3 x+5}{(x-1)^{2}(x+1)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int\left\{\frac{-1}{2(x-1)}+\frac{4}{(x-1)^{2}}+\frac{1}{2(x+1)}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x+4 \int \frac{1}{(x-1)^{2}} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \log |x-1|+4\left(\frac{-1}{x-1}\right)+\frac{1}{2} \log |x+1|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log \left|\frac{x+1}{x-1}\right|-\frac{4}{x-1}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {x - 1} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{A}{{x - 1}} + \frac{B}{{x + 1}} + \frac{C}{{2x + 3}}{/tex} ....(i) {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 2x - 3 = A(x + 1)(2x + 3) + B(x - 1)(2x + 3) + C(x - 1)(x + 1) {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 2x - 3 = A(2x 2 + 5x + 3) + B(2x 2 + x - 3) + C(x 2 - 1) {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 2x - 3 = 2Ax 2 + 5Ax + 3A + 2Bx 2 + Bx - 3B + Cx 2 - C Comparing coefficients of x 2 : 2A + 2B + C = 0 ...(ii) Comparing coefficients of x : 5A + B = 2 .....(iii) Comparing constants: 3A – 3B – C = –3 .....(iv) On solving eq. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}},B = \frac{5}{2},C = \frac{{ - 24}}{5}{/tex} Putting the values of A, B and C in eq. (i), {tex}\frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}}}{{x - 1}} + \frac{{\frac{5}{2}}}{{x + 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 24}}{5}}}{{2x + 3}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \int {\frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}\int {\frac{1}{{x - 1}}dx + \frac{5}{2}} \int {\frac{1}{{x + 1}}dx - \frac{{24}}{5}\int {\frac{1}{{2x + 3}}dx} } {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}\log \left| {x - 1}+ \right|\frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{{24}}{5}\frac{{\log \left| {2x + 3} \right|}}{{2 }} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}\log \left| {x - 1}|+ \right|\frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{{12}}{5}\log \left| {2x + 3} \right| + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{1}{{10}}\log \left| {x - 1} \right| - \frac{{12}}{5}\log \left| {2x + 3} \right| + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {x - 1} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{A}{{x - 1}} + \frac{B}{{x + 1}} + \frac{C}{{2x + 3}}{/tex} ....(i)

{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 2x - 3 = A(x + 1)(2x + 3) + B(x - 1)(2x + 3) + C(x - 1)(x + 1)

{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 2x - 3 = A(2x2 + 5x + 3) + B(2x2 + x - 3) + C(x2 - 1)

{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 2x - 3 = 2Ax2 + 5Ax + 3A + 2Bx2 + Bx - 3B + Cx2 - C

Comparing coefficients of x2: 2A + 2B + C = 0 ...(ii)

Comparing coefficients of x : 5A + B = 2 .....(iii)

Comparing constants: 3A – 3B – C = –3 .....(iv)

On solving eq. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}},B = \frac{5}{2},C = \frac{{ - 24}}{5}{/tex}

Putting the values of A, B and C in eq. (i),

{tex}\frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}}}{{x - 1}} + \frac{{\frac{5}{2}}}{{x + 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 24}}{5}}}{{2x + 3}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \int {\frac{{2x - 3}}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {2x + 3} \right)}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}\int {\frac{1}{{x - 1}}dx + \frac{5}{2}} \int {\frac{1}{{x + 1}}dx - \frac{{24}}{5}\int {\frac{1}{{2x + 3}}dx} } {/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}\log \left| {x - 1}+ \right|\frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{{24}}{5}\frac{{\log \left| {2x + 3} \right|}}{{2 }} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{{10}}\log \left| {x - 1}|+ \right|\frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{{12}}{5}\log \left| {2x + 3} \right| + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{1}{{10}}\log \left| {x - 1} \right| - \frac{{12}}{5}\log \left| {2x + 3} \right| + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {x + 2} \right)\left( {x - 2} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{A}{{x + 1}} + \frac{B}{{x + 2}} + \frac{C}{{x - 2}}{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x + 2) + B(x + 1)(x - 2) + C(x + 1)(x + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x 2 4) + B(x 2 - x - 2) + C(x 2 + 3x + 2) {tex}\Rightarrow 2x {/tex} = Ax 2 - 4A + Bx 2 - Bx - 2B + Cx 2 + 3Cx + 2C Comparing coefficients of x 2 : A + B + C = 0 .......(ii) Comparing coefficients of x: B + 3C= 5 .......(iii) Comparing constants: 4A 2B + 2C = 0 .......(iv) On solving eq. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{5}{3},B = \frac{{ - 5}}{2},C = \frac{5}{6}{/tex} Putting the values of A, B and C in eq. (i), {tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\frac{5}{3}}}{{x + 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}}}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}} + \frac{{\frac{5}{6}}}{{x - 2}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{3}\int {\frac{1}{{x + 1}}dx - \frac{5}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}}dx + \frac{5}{6}} \int {\frac{1}{{x - 2}}} } dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{5}{3}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 2} \right| + \frac{5}{6}\log \left| {x - 2} \right| + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {x + 2} \right)\left( {x - 2} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{A}{{x + 1}} + \frac{B}{{x + 2}} + \frac{C}{{x - 2}}{/tex} ...(i)

{tex} \Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x + 2) + B(x + 1)(x - 2) + C(x + 1)(x + 2)

{tex}\Rightarrow 5x {/tex} = A(x2 4) + B(x2 - x - 2) + C(x2 + 3x + 2)

{tex}\Rightarrow 2x {/tex} = Ax2 - 4A + Bx2 - Bx - 2B + Cx2 + 3Cx + 2C

Comparing coefficients of x2: A + B + C = 0 .......(ii)
Comparing coefficients of x: B + 3C= 5 .......(iii)
Comparing constants: 4A 2B + 2C = 0 .......(iv)
On solving eq. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{5}{3},B = \frac{{ - 5}}{2},C = \frac{5}{6}{/tex}
Putting the values of A, B and C in eq. (i),

{tex}\frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{\frac{5}{3}}}{{x + 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}}}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}} + \frac{{\frac{5}{6}}}{{x - 2}}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \int \frac{{5x}}{{\left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} - 4} \right)}}dx{/tex}{tex}= \frac{5}{3}\int {\frac{1}{{x + 1}}dx - \frac{5}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{\left( {x + 2} \right)}}dx + \frac{5}{6}} \int {\frac{1}{{x - 2}}} } dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{5}{3}\log \left| {x + 1} \right| - \frac{5}{2}\log \left| {x + 2} \right| + \frac{5}{6}\log \left| {x - 2} \right| + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Given function is {tex}\frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} Dividing (x 3 + x + 1) by x 2 - 1, we get, {tex}\frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}=x+\frac{2 x+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{2 x+1}{x^{2}-1}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B}{(x-1)}{/tex} Now, 2x + 1 = A(x – 1) + B(x + 1) ...(i) Substituting x = 1 and -1 in equation (i), we get, {tex}A=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}B=\frac{3}{2}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}=x+\frac{1}{2(x+1)}+\frac{3}{2(x-1)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow~~\int \frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}=\int x d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x+\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \log |x+1|+\frac{3}{2} \log |x-1|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given function is {tex}\frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} 
Dividing (x3 + x + 1) by x2 - 1, we get,
{tex}\frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}=x+\frac{2 x+1}{x^{2}-1}{/tex} 
Let {tex}\frac{2 x+1}{x^{2}-1}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B}{(x-1)}{/tex} 
Now, 2x + 1 = A(x – 1) + B(x + 1) ...(i)
Substituting x = 1 and -1 in equation (i), we get,
{tex}A=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}B=\frac{3}{2}{/tex} 
Thus, {tex}\frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}=x+\frac{1}{2(x+1)}+\frac{3}{2(x-1)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow~~\int \frac{x^{3}+x+1}{x^{2}-1}=\int x d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x+\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \log |x+1|+\frac{3}{2} \log |x-1|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{2}{(1-x)\left(1+x^{2}\right)}=\frac{A}{(1-x)}+\frac{Bx + C}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{/tex} ⇒ 2 = A(1 + x 2 ) + (Bx + C)(1 - x) ⇒ 2 = A + Ax 2 + Bx - Bx 2 + C - Cx On comparing the coefficients of x 2 , x and constant term, we get, A - B = 0 B - C = 0 A + C = 2 On solving these equations, we get, A = 1, B =1 and C = 1 Thus, {tex}\frac{2}{(1-x)\left(1+x^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{(1-x)}+\frac{x+1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2}{(1-x)\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x=\int \frac{1}{(1-x)} d x+\int \frac{x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x+\int \frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x+\int \frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\log |x-1|+\frac{1}{2} \log \left|1+x^{2}\right|+\tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{2}{(1-x)\left(1+x^{2}\right)}=\frac{A}{(1-x)}+\frac{Bx + C}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{/tex} 
⇒ 2 = A(1 + x2) + (Bx + C)(1 - x)
⇒ 2 = A + Ax2 + Bx - Bx2 + C - Cx
On comparing the coefficients of x2, x and constant term, we get,
A - B = 0
B - C = 0
A + C = 2
On solving these equations, we get,
A = 1, B =1 and C = 1
Thus,
{tex}\frac{2}{(1-x)\left(1+x^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{(1-x)}+\frac{x+1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{2}{(1-x)\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x=\int \frac{1}{(1-x)} d x+\int \frac{x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x+\int \frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}-\int \frac{1}{(x-1)} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x+\int \frac{1}{\left(1+x^{2}\right)} d x{/tex}
= {tex}-\log |x-1|+\frac{1}{2} \log \left|1+x^{2}\right|+\tan ^{-1} x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x+2)^{2}}=\frac{A}{(x+2)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x -1 = A(x + 2) + B Equating the coefficients of x and constant term, we get, A = 3 2A + B = -1 B = -7 Thus, {tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x+2)^{2}}=\frac{3}{(x+2)}-\frac{7}{(x+2)^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{3 x-1}{(x+2)^{2}}=\int\left\{\frac{3}{(x+2)}-\frac{7}{(x+2)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}3 \log |x+2|-7\left(\frac{-1}{(x+2)}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}3 \log |x+2|+\frac{7}{(x+2)}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x+2)^{2}}=\frac{A}{(x+2)}+\frac{B}{(x+2)^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x -1 = A(x + 2) + B
Equating the coefficients of x and constant term, we get,
A = 3
2A + B = -1
B = -7
Thus,
{tex}\frac{3 x-1}{(x+2)^{2}}=\frac{3}{(x+2)}-\frac{7}{(x+2)^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{3 x-1}{(x+2)^{2}}=\int\left\{\frac{3}{(x+2)}-\frac{7}{(x+2)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}3 \log |x+2|-7\left(\frac{-1}{(x+2)}\right)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}3 \log |x+2|+\frac{7}{(x+2)}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)}}{/tex} Putting {tex}{x^2} = y{/tex} ,we get, {tex}\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{{\left( {y - 1} \right)\left( {y + 1} \right)}} {/tex} {tex}Let\ \frac{1}{{\left( {y - 1} \right)\left( {y + 1} \right)}} = \frac{A}{{y - 1}} + \frac{B}{{y + 1}}{/tex} .....(i) {tex}\Rightarrow 1 = A\left( {y + 1} \right) + B\left( {y - 1} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 1 = Ay + A + By - B{/tex} Comparing the coefficients of y, A + B = 0 ......(ii) Comparing constants A – B = 1 .......(iii) On solving the eq. (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{1}{2},B = \frac{{ - 1}}{2}{/tex} Putting the values of A, B and y in eq. (i), {tex}\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}} = \frac{{\frac{1}{2}}}{{{x^2} - 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}{{{x^2} + 1}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int {\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}}dx = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - 1}}dx - \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} + 1}}dx} } } {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}.\frac{1}{{2.1}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{\left( {{x^2} - 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)}}{/tex}

Putting {tex}{x^2} = y{/tex},we get,

{tex}\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{1}{{\left( {y - 1} \right)\left( {y + 1} \right)}} {/tex}

{tex}Let\ \frac{1}{{\left( {y - 1} \right)\left( {y + 1} \right)}} = \frac{A}{{y - 1}} + \frac{B}{{y + 1}}{/tex} .....(i)

{tex}\Rightarrow 1 = A\left( {y + 1} \right) + B\left( {y - 1} \right){/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 1 = Ay + A + By - B{/tex}

Comparing the coefficients of y, A + B = 0 ......(ii)

Comparing constants A – B = 1 .......(iii)

On solving the eq. (ii) and (iii), we get {tex}A = \frac{1}{2},B = \frac{{ - 1}}{2}{/tex}

Putting the values of A, B and y in eq. (i),

{tex}\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}} = \frac{{\frac{1}{2}}}{{{x^2} - 1}} + \frac{{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}}{{{x^2} + 1}}{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \int {\frac{1}{{{x^4} - 1}}dx = \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - 1}}dx - \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{1}{{{x^2} + 1}}dx} } } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}.\frac{1}{{2.1}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}{/tex} [Hint: multiply numerator and denominator by x n-1 and put x n = t]
question_html: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}{/tex}  [Hint: multiply numerator and denominator by xn-1 and put xn = t]
solution_text: Given function is, {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}{/tex} Multiplying numerator and denominator by x n-1 , we get, {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}=\frac{x^{n-1}}{x^{n-1} x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}=\frac{x^{n-1}}{x^{n}\left(x^{n}+1\right)}{/tex} Let x n = t nx n-1 dx = dt Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)} d x=\int \frac{x^{n-1}}{x^{n}\left(x^{n}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{n} \int \frac{1}{t(t+1)} d t{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{1}{t(t+1)}=\frac{A}{t}+\frac{B}{(t+1)}{/tex} 1 = A(1 + t) + Bt ...(i) Substituting t = 0, -1 in equation (i), we get, A =1 and B = -1 Thus, {tex}\frac{1}{t(t+1)}=\frac{1}{t}-\frac{1}{(1+t)}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{n} \int\left\{\frac{1}{t}-\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right\} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{n}[\log |t|-\log |t+1|]+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{n}\left[\log \left|x^{n}\right|-\log \left|x^{n}+1\right|\right]+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{n} \log \left|\frac{x^{n}}{x^{n}+1}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given function is, {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}{/tex} 
Multiplying numerator and denominator by xn-1, we get,
{tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}=\frac{x^{n-1}}{x^{n-1} x\left(x^{n}+1\right)}=\frac{x^{n-1}}{x^{n}\left(x^{n}+1\right)}{/tex} 
Let xn = t
nxn-1dx = dt
Therefore,
{tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)} d x=\int \frac{x^{n-1}}{x^{n}\left(x^{n}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{n} \int \frac{1}{t(t+1)} d t{/tex} 
Let {tex}\frac{1}{t(t+1)}=\frac{A}{t}+\frac{B}{(t+1)}{/tex} 
1 = A(1 + t) + Bt ...(i)
Substituting t = 0, -1 in equation (i), we get,
A =1 and B = -1
Thus,
{tex}\frac{1}{t(t+1)}=\frac{1}{t}-\frac{1}{(1+t)}{/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{n}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{n} \int\left\{\frac{1}{t}-\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right\} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{n}[\log |t|-\log |t+1|]+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{n}\left[\log \left|x^{n}\right|-\log \left|x^{n}+1\right|\right]+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{n} \log \left|\frac{x^{n}}{x^{n}+1}\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)}{/tex} [Hint: Put sin x = t]
question_html: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)}{/tex} [Hint: Put sin x = t]
solution_text: Given function is {tex}\frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)}{/tex} Let sin x = t Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)} d x=\int \frac{d t}{(1-t)(2-t)}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{1}{(1-t)(2-t)}=\frac{A}{(1-t)}+\frac{B}{(2-t)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(2 - t) + B (1 - t) …(i) Substituting t = 2 and then t = 1 in equation (i), we get, Therefore, {tex}\frac{1}{(1-t)(2-t)}=\frac{1}{(1-t)}-\frac{1}{(2-t)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{(1-t)}-\frac{1}{(2-t)}\right\} d t{/tex} = {tex}-log |1-t|+\log |2-t|+C{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{2-t}{1-t}\right|+C{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{2-\sin x}{1-\sin x}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given function is {tex}\frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)}{/tex} 
Let sin x = t
Therefore,
{tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)} d x=\int \frac{d t}{(1-t)(2-t)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}\frac{1}{(1-t)(2-t)}=\frac{A}{(1-t)}+\frac{B}{(2-t)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(2 - t) + B (1 - t) …(i)
Substituting t = 2 and then t = 1 in equation (i), we get,
Therefore,
{tex}\frac{1}{(1-t)(2-t)}=\frac{1}{(1-t)}-\frac{1}{(2-t)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)(2-\sin x)} d x=\int\left\{\frac{1}{(1-t)}-\frac{1}{(2-t)}\right\} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}-log |1-t|+\log |2-t|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\frac{2-t}{1-t}\right|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\frac{2-\sin x}{1-\sin x}\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: We have, {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=1-\frac{\left(4 x^{2}+10\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{\left(4 x^{2}+10\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 4x 2 + 10 = (Ax + B)(x 2 + 4) + (Cx + D)(x 2 + 3) ⇒ 4x 2 + 10 = Ax 3 + 4Ax + Bx 2 + 4B + Cx 3 + 3Cx + Dx 2 + 3D ⇒ 4x 2 + 10 = (A+C)x 3 + (B + D)x 2 + (4A + 3C)x + (4B + 3D) Equating the coefficients of x 3 , x 2 , x and constant term, we get, A + C = 0 B + D = 4 4A + 3C = 0 4B + 3 D = 10 On solving these equations, we get, A = 0, B = -2, C = 0 and D = 6 Therefore, {tex}\frac{\left(4 x^{2}+10\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{-2}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}+\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=1-\left(\frac{-2}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}+\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}\right){/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x=\int\left\{1+\frac{2}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}-\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\left\{1+\frac{2}{x^{2}+(\sqrt{3})^{2}}-\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+2^{2}\right)}\right\}{/tex} = {tex}x+2\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}\right)-6\left(\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}x+\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}-3 \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=1-\frac{\left(4 x^{2}+10\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}\frac{\left(4 x^{2}+10\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
4x2 + 10 = (Ax + B)(x2 + 4) + (Cx + D)(x2 + 3)
⇒ 4x2 + 10 = Ax3 + 4Ax + Bx2 + 4B + Cx3 + 3Cx + Dx2 + 3D
⇒ 4x2 + 10 = (A+C)x3 + (B + D)x2 + (4A + 3C)x + (4B + 3D)
Equating the coefficients of x3, x2, x and constant term, we get,
A + C = 0
B + D = 4
4A + 3C = 0
4B + 3 D = 10
On solving these equations, we get,
A = 0, B = -2, C = 0 and D = 6
Therefore,
{tex}\frac{\left(4 x^{2}+10\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{-2}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}+\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=1-\left(\frac{-2}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}+\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x=\int\left\{1+\frac{2}{\left(x^{2}+3\right)}-\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\left\{1+\frac{2}{x^{2}+(\sqrt{3})^{2}}-\frac{6}{\left(x^{2}+2^{2}\right)}\right\}{/tex} 
= {tex}x+2\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}\right)-6\left(\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}x+\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}-3 \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{2x}}{{\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}dx} {/tex} ....(i) Putting {tex}{x^2} = t \Rightarrow 2xdx = dt{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{2}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}dt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\left( {t + 3} \right) - \left( {t + 1} \right)}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}dt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {\frac{{\left( {t + 3} \right)}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}} - \frac{{\left( {t + 1} \right)}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}} \right)dt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {\frac{1}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)}} - \frac{1}{{\left( {t + 3} \right)}}} \right)dt} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\log \left| {t + 1} \right| - \log \left| {t + 3} \right|} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\log \left| {\frac{{t + 1}}{{t + 3}}} \right|} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{{{x^2} + 1}}{{{x^2} + 3}}} \right| + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left( {\frac{{{x^2} + 1}}{{{x^2} + 3}}} \right) + c{/tex} . Which is the required solution.
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{2x}}{{\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 3} \right)}}dx} {/tex} ....(i)

Putting {tex}{x^2} = t \Rightarrow 2xdx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i),

{tex}I = \int {\frac{{dt}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{2}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}dt} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{\left( {t + 3} \right) - \left( {t + 1} \right)}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}dt} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {\frac{{\left( {t + 3} \right)}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}} - \frac{{\left( {t + 1} \right)}}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)\left( {t + 3} \right)}}} \right)dt} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {\frac{1}{{\left( {t + 1} \right)}} - \frac{1}{{\left( {t + 3} \right)}}} \right)dt} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\log \left| {t + 1} \right| - \log \left| {t + 3} \right|} \right] + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\log \left| {\frac{{t + 1}}{{t + 3}}} \right|} \right] + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{{{x^2} + 1}}{{{x^2} + 3}}} \right| + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left( {\frac{{{x^2} + 1}}{{{x^2} + 3}}} \right) + c{/tex}.

Which is the required solution.

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Given the function, {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)}{/tex} Multiplying numerator and denominator by x 3 , we get, {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)}=\frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}\left(x^{4}+1\right)}{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x=\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x{/tex} Now, let x 4 = t 4x 3 dx = dt Thus, {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t(t-1)}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{A}{t}+\frac{B}{t-1}{/tex} 1 = A(t - 1) + Bt …(i) Substituting t = 0 and 1 in (i), we get A = -1 and B = 1 Therefore {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{-1}{t}+\frac{1}{t-1}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{4} \int \left\{\frac{-1}{t}+\frac{1}{t-1}\right\} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}[-\log |t|+\log |t-1|]+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{t-1}{t}\right|+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{x^{4}-1}{x^{4}}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given the function, {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)}{/tex} 
Multiplying numerator and denominator by x3, we get,
{tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)}=\frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}\left(x^{4}+1\right)}{/tex} 
Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x=\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x{/tex} 
Now, let x4 = t
4x3dx = dt
Thus, {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t(t-1)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{A}{t}+\frac{B}{t-1}{/tex} 
1 = A(t - 1) + Bt …(i)
Substituting t = 0 and 1 in (i), we get
A = -1 and B = 1
Therefore {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{-1}{t}+\frac{1}{t-1}{/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{4}+1\right)} d x=\frac{1}{4} \int \left\{\frac{-1}{t}+\frac{1}{t-1}\right\} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4}[-\log |t|+\log |t-1|]+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{t-1}{t}\right|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{x^{4}-1}{x^{4}}\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the rational function {tex}\frac{1}{\left(e^{x}-1\right)}{/tex} [Hint: e x = t]
solution_text: Given function is, {tex}\frac{1}{\left(e^{x}-1\right)}{/tex} Let e x = t e x dx = dt {tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(e^{x}-1\right)} d x=\int \frac{1}{t-1} \times \frac{d t}{t}=\int \frac{1}{t(t-1)} d t{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{A}{t}+\frac{B}{t-1}{/tex} 1 = A(t - 1) + Bt … (i) Substituting t =1 and t = 0 in equation (i), we get, A = -1 and B = 1 Therefore, {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{-1}{t}+\frac{1}{t-1}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{t(t-1)} d t=\log \left|\frac{t-1}{t}\right|+C{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{e^{x}-1}{e^{x}}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given function is, {tex}\frac{1}{\left(e^{x}-1\right)}{/tex} 
Let ex = t
ex dx = dt
{tex}\int \frac{1}{\left(e^{x}-1\right)} d x=\int \frac{1}{t-1} \times \frac{d t}{t}=\int \frac{1}{t(t-1)} d t{/tex} 
Let {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{A}{t}+\frac{B}{t-1}{/tex} 
1 = A(t - 1) + Bt … (i)
Substituting t =1 and t = 0 in equation (i), we get,
A = -1 and B = 1
Therefore, {tex}\frac{1}{t(t-1)}=\frac{-1}{t}+\frac{1}{t-1}{/tex} 
{tex}\int \frac{1}{t(t-1)} d t=\log \left|\frac{t-1}{t}\right|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\frac{e^{x}-1}{e^{x}}\right|+C{/tex}

options.explanation: Let {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)}=\frac{A}{x-1}+\frac{B}{x-2}{/tex} x = A(x - 2) + B(x - 1) …(i) Substituting x = 1 and 2 in (i), we get, A = -1 and B = 2 Therefore, {tex}\frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)}=-\frac{1}{(x-1)}+\frac{2}{(x-2)}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{x}{(x-1)(x-2)} d x=\int\left\{-\frac{1}{(x-1)}+\frac{2}{(x-2)}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\log |x-1|+2 \log |x-2|+c{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{(x-2)^{2}}{x-1}\right|+C{/tex}

Let {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{A}{x}+\frac{B x+C}{x^{2}+1}{/tex} 1 = A(X 2 + 1) + (Bx + C)x = Ax 2 + A + Bx 2 + Cx = (A + B)x 2 + Cx + A Equating the coefficients of x 2 , x and constant term, we get, A + B = 0 C = 0 A = 1 On solving these equations, we get, A = 1, B = -1 and C = 0 Therefore, {tex}\frac{1}{x\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{1}{x}+\frac{-x}{x^{2}+1}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{x\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\int\left\{\frac{1}{x}+\frac{-x}{x^{2}+1}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\log |x|-\frac{1}{2} \log \left|x^{2}+1\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x sin x
solution_text: {tex}\int {x\sin x} dx{/tex} {tex}= x\int {\sin x} dx - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dx}}x\int {\sin xdx} } \right)dx} {/tex} [Applying product rule] {tex} = x\left( { - \cos x} \right) - \int {1\left( { - \cos x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = -x\cos x - \int { - \cos xdx} {/tex} {tex}= - x\cos x + \int {\cos xdx} {/tex} = -x cos x + sin x + c
solution_html:
{tex}\int {x\sin x} dx{/tex}

{tex}= x\int {\sin x} dx - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dx}}x\int {\sin xdx} } \right)dx} {/tex}

[Applying product rule]

{tex} = x\left( { - \cos x} \right) - \int {1\left( { - \cos x} \right)dx} {/tex}

{tex} = -x\cos x - \int { - \cos xdx} {/tex}

{tex}= - x\cos x + \int {\cos xdx} {/tex}

= -x cos x + sin x + c

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int {x\sin 3xdx} {/tex} {tex}= x\int {\sin 3xdx - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dx}}x\int {\sin 3x} dx} \right)dx} } {/tex} [Applying product rule] {tex}= x\left( {\frac{{ - \cos 3x}}{3}} \right) - \int {1\left( {\frac{{ - \cos 3x}}{3}} \right)dx + c}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{3}x\cos 3x + \frac{1}{3}\int {\cos 3xdx + c} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{3}x\cos 3x + \frac{1}{3}\frac{{\sin 3x}}{3} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{3}x\cos 3x + \frac{1}{9}\sin 3x + c{/tex} {tex}= - \frac{x}{3}\cos 3x + \frac{1}{9}\sin 3x + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {x\sin 3xdx} {/tex}

{tex}= x\int {\sin 3xdx - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dx}}x\int {\sin 3x} dx} \right)dx} } {/tex}

[Applying product rule]

{tex}= x\left( {\frac{{ - \cos 3x}}{3}} \right) - \int {1\left( {\frac{{ - \cos 3x}}{3}} \right)dx + c}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{ - 1}}{3}x\cos 3x + \frac{1}{3}\int {\cos 3xdx + c} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{3}x\cos 3x + \frac{1}{3}\frac{{\sin 3x}}{3} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{ - 1}}{3}x\cos 3x + \frac{1}{9}\sin 3x + c{/tex}

{tex}= - \frac{x}{3}\cos 3x + \frac{1}{9}\sin 3x + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x 2 e x
solution_text: {tex}\int {{x^2}{e^x}} dx{/tex} {tex} = {x^2}\int {{e^x}dx - \int {\left[ {\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2}\int {{e^x}dx} } \right]} dx} {/tex} [Applying product rule] {tex} = {x^2}{e^x} - \int {2x{e^x}dx} {/tex} {tex} = {x^2}{e^x} - 2\int {x{e^x}dx} {/tex} {tex}= {x^2}{e^x} - 2\left[ {{xe^x}dx - \int {\left\{ {\frac{d}{{dx}}x\int {{e^x}dx} } \right\}dx} } \right]{/tex} [Again applying product rule] {tex}= {x^2}{e^x} - 2\left( {x{e^x} - \int {1.{e^x}dx} } \right){/tex} {tex}= {x^2}{e^x} - 2\left( {x{e^x} - \int {{e^x}dx} } \right){/tex} {tex} = {x^2}{e^x} - 2x{e^x} + 2\int {{e^x}dx} {/tex} = x 2 e x - 2xe x + 2e x + c = e x (x 2 - 2x + 2) + c
solution_html:
{tex}\int {{x^2}{e^x}} dx{/tex}

{tex} = {x^2}\int {{e^x}dx - \int {\left[ {\frac{d}{{dx}}{x^2}\int {{e^x}dx} } \right]} dx} {/tex}

[Applying product rule]

{tex} = {x^2}{e^x} - \int {2x{e^x}dx} {/tex}

{tex} = {x^2}{e^x} - 2\int {x{e^x}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= {x^2}{e^x} - 2\left[ {{xe^x}dx - \int {\left\{ {\frac{d}{{dx}}x\int {{e^x}dx} } \right\}dx} } \right]{/tex}

[Again applying product rule]

{tex}= {x^2}{e^x} - 2\left( {x{e^x} - \int {1.{e^x}dx} } \right){/tex}

{tex}= {x^2}{e^x} - 2\left( {x{e^x} - \int {{e^x}dx} } \right){/tex}

{tex} = {x^2}{e^x} - 2x{e^x} + 2\int {{e^x}dx} {/tex}

= x2ex - 2xex + 2ex + c

= ex(x2 - 2x + 2) + c

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x log x
solution_text: Let {tex} I = x log x{/tex} Now, integrating by parts, we get, Taking, Logarithmic function as first function and algebraic function as second function, {tex}I=\log x \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \log x\right) \int x d x\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\log x\left(\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right)-\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \log x}{2}-\int \frac{x}{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \log x}{2}-\frac{x^{2}}{4}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex} I = x log x{/tex}
Now, integrating by parts, we get,
Taking, Logarithmic function as first function and algebraic function as second function,
{tex}I=\log x \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \log x\right) \int x d x\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\log x\left(\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right)-\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \log x}{2}-\int \frac{x}{2} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \log x}{2}-\frac{x^{2}}{4}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int {x\log 2xdx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\left( {\log 2x} \right)xdx} {/tex} {tex}= \left( {\log 2x} \right)\int {xdx - \int {\left[ {\frac{d}{{dx}}\log 2x\int {xdx} } \right]dx} } {/tex} [Applying product rule] {tex}= \left( {\log 2x} \right)\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \int {\frac{1}{{2x}}.2.\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{2}{x^2}\log 2x - \frac{1}{2}\int {xdx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}{x^2}\log 2x - \frac{1}{2}\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}\log 2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{4} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {x\log 2xdx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {\left( {\log 2x} \right)xdx} {/tex}

{tex}= \left( {\log 2x} \right)\int {xdx - \int {\left[ {\frac{d}{{dx}}\log 2x\int {xdx} } \right]dx} } {/tex}

[Applying product rule]

{tex}= \left( {\log 2x} \right)\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \int {\frac{1}{{2x}}.2.\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{2}{x^2}\log 2x - \frac{1}{2}\int {xdx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}{x^2}\log 2x - \frac{1}{2}\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}\log 2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{4} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x 2 log x
solution_text: {tex}\int {{x^2}\log xdx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {\left( {\log x} \right){x^2}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \log x\int {{x^2}dx - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x\int {{x^2}dx} } \right)dx} } {/tex} [Applying product rule] {tex}= \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \int {\frac{1}{x}.\frac{{{x^3}}}{3}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}\log x - \frac{1}{3}\int{x^2}dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}\log x - \frac{1}{3}\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}\log x - \frac{{{x^3}}}{9} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {{x^2}\log xdx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {\left( {\log x} \right){x^2}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \log x\int {{x^2}dx - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dx}}\log x\int {{x^2}dx} } \right)dx} } {/tex}

[Applying product rule]

{tex}= \left( {\log x} \right)\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \int {\frac{1}{x}.\frac{{{x^3}}}{3}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}\log x - \frac{1}{3}\int{x^2}dx{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}\log x - \frac{1}{3}\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}\log x - \frac{{{x^3}}}{9} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x sin -1 x
solution_text: Let I = x sin -1 x Now, integrating by parts, we get, {tex}I=\sin ^{-1} x \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \sin ^{-1} x\right) \int x d x\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2}-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-x^{2}}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\frac{1-x^{2}}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left\{\int \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x\right\}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+\frac{1}{4} \sin ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1} x+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left(2 x^{2}-1\right) \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = x sin-1 x
Now, integrating by parts, we get,
{tex}I=\sin ^{-1} x \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \sin ^{-1} x\right) \int x d x\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2}-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-x^{2}}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\frac{1-x^{2}}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{1}{2}\left\{\int \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x\right\}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \sin ^{-1} x}{2}+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+\frac{1}{4} \sin ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1} x+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left(2 x^{2}-1\right) \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x tan -1 x
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {x{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} dx{/tex}

{tex} = \int {\left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right).x} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right).\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \int {\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}.\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{{x^2}}}{{1 + {x^2}}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{{x^2} + 1 - 1}}{{{x^2} + 1}}dx}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\int {\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{{x^2} + 1}}} \right)dx}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\left( {x - {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right) + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {{x^2}{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x - x + {{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right] + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{x}{2} + \frac{1}{2}{\tan ^{ - 1}}x + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x cos -1 x
solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int{/tex} {tex}x .cos^{-1} x{/tex} Now, integrating by parts, we get, {tex}I=\cos ^{-1} x \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \cos ^{-1} x\right) \int x d x\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}={/tex} {tex}\cos ^{-1} x \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2}-\int \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x{/tex} {tex}={/tex} {tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1-x^{2}-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}+\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} I_{1}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} ...(i) Now, {tex}I_{1}=\int \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{d}{d x} \sqrt{1-x^{2}} \int d x{/tex} {tex}I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{-2 x}{2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}}} x \cdot d x{/tex} = {tex}x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{-x^{2}}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} = {tex}x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{1-x^{2}-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} = {tex}x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\left\{\int \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x+\int \frac{-d x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\therefore I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\left\{I_{1}+\cos ^{-1} x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\cos ^{-1} x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} Now, substituting in (i), we get, {tex}I=\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x\right)-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left(2 x^{2}-1\right)}{4} \cos ^{-1} x-\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int{/tex}{tex}x .cos^{-1} x{/tex}
Now, integrating by parts, we get,
{tex}I=\cos ^{-1} x \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \cos ^{-1} x\right) \int x d x\right\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}={/tex} {tex}\cos ^{-1} x \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2}-\int \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x{/tex} 
{tex}={/tex}{tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1-x^{2}-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int\left\{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}+\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right)\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} I_{1}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} ...(i)
Now, {tex}I_{1}=\int \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{d}{d x} \sqrt{1-x^{2}} \int d x{/tex} 
{tex}I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{-2 x}{2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}}} x \cdot d x{/tex} 
= {tex}x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{-x^{2}}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{1-x^{2}-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\left\{\int \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x+\int \frac{-d x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\left\{I_{1}+\cos ^{-1} x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2I_{1}=x \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\cos ^{-1} x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{1}=\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} 
Now, substituting in (i), we get,
{tex}I=\frac{x^{2} \cos ^{-1} x}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x\right)-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\left(2 x^{2}-1\right)}{4} \cos ^{-1} x-\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Putting {tex}x = \sin \theta \Rightarrow dx = \cos \theta d\theta {/tex} {tex}\therefore \int {{{\left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x} \right)}^2}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {{\theta ^2}\cos \theta } d\theta {/tex} [Applying product rule] {tex} = {\theta ^2}\sin \theta - \int {2\theta \sin \theta d\theta } {/tex} {tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta - 2\int {\theta \sin \theta d\theta }{/tex} [Again applying product rule] {tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta - 2\left[ {\theta \left( { - \cos \theta } \right) - \int {1.\left( { - \cos \theta } \right)d\theta } } \right]{/tex} {tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta + 2\theta \cos \theta - 2\int {\cos \theta d\theta } {/tex} {tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta + 2\theta \cos \theta - 2\sin \theta + c{/tex} {tex}= x{\left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x} \right)^2} + 2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - 2x + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Putting {tex}x = \sin \theta \Rightarrow dx = \cos \theta d\theta {/tex} 

{tex}\therefore \int {{{\left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x} \right)}^2}} dx{/tex}

{tex}= \int {{\theta ^2}\cos \theta } d\theta {/tex}

[Applying product rule]

{tex} = {\theta ^2}\sin \theta - \int {2\theta \sin \theta d\theta } {/tex}

{tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta - 2\int {\theta \sin \theta d\theta }{/tex}

[Again applying product rule]

{tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta - 2\left[ {\theta \left( { - \cos \theta } \right) - \int {1.\left( { - \cos \theta } \right)d\theta } } \right]{/tex}

{tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta + 2\theta \cos \theta - 2\int {\cos \theta d\theta } {/tex}

{tex}= {\theta ^2}\sin \theta + 2\theta \cos \theta - 2\sin \theta + c{/tex}

{tex}= x{\left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x} \right)^2} + 2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {\sin ^{ - 1}}x - 2x + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\frac{x \cos ^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot \cos ^{-1} x d x{/tex} Now, integrating by parts, we get, {tex}I = \frac{-1}{2}\left[\cos ^{-1} x \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \cos ^{-1} x\right) \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x\right\} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left[\cos ^{-1} x \cdot 2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot 2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left[2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}} \cos ^{-1} x+\int 2 d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left[2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}} \cos ^{-1} x+2 x\right]+C{/tex} = {tex}-\left[\sqrt{1-x^{2}} \cos ^{-1} x+x\right]+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\frac{x \cos ^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} 
{tex}I=-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot \cos ^{-1} x d x{/tex} 
Now, integrating by parts, we get,
{tex}I = \frac{-1}{2}\left[\cos ^{-1} x \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \cos ^{-1} x\right) \int \frac{-2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} d x\right\} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left[\cos ^{-1} x \cdot 2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}}-\int \frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot 2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left[2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}} \cos ^{-1} x+\int 2 d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left[2 \sqrt{1-x^{2}} \cos ^{-1} x+2 x\right]+C{/tex} 
= {tex}-\left[\sqrt{1-x^{2}} \cos ^{-1} x+x\right]+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function x sec 2 x
solution_text: Let I = x sec 2 x Now, integrating by parts, we get, {tex}I=x \int \sec ^{2} x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} x\right) \int \sec ^{2} x d x\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}x \tan x-\int (1) (\tan x) d x{/tex} = {tex}x \tan x+\log |\cos x|+c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = x sec2 x
Now, integrating by parts, we get,
{tex}I=x \int \sec ^{2} x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} x\right) \int \sec ^{2} x d x\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}x \tan x-\int (1) (\tan x) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}x \tan x+\log |\cos x|+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let I = tan -1 xdx {tex}= \int {\left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right).1} dx{/tex} {tex} = {\tan ^{ - 1}}x.x - \int {\frac{1}{{1 + {x^2}}}x.dx} {/tex} {tex}= x{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\int {\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= x{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)} \right| + c{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{{f'\left( x \right)}}{{f\left( x \right)}}dx = \log \left| {f\left( x \right)} \right|} } \right]{/tex} {tex}= x{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\log \left( {1 + {x^2}} \right) + c{/tex}
solution_html:

{tex}= \int {\left( {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right).1} dx{/tex}

{tex}= x{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)} \right| + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I=x(\log x)^{2}{/tex} Integrating by parts, we get, {tex}I=\left[(\log x)^2 \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} (\log x)^2 \right) \int x d x\right\} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^2-\int \frac{2 \log x }{x} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2}- \int x \log x \cdot d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2} - [ log x ∫ x dx -∫( \frac {d}{dx} (log x)∫ x dx) dx]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2} - [ \frac {x²}{2}\log x - ∫(\frac1x \cdot\frac{x^2}{2} ) dx{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2}-\frac{x^{2}}{2} \log x+\frac{x^{2}}{4}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=x(\log x)^{2}{/tex} 
Integrating by parts, we get,
{tex}I=\left[(\log x)^2 \int x d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} (\log x)^2 \right) \int x d x\right\} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^2-\int \frac{2 \log x }{x} \cdot \frac{x^{2}}{2} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2}- \int x \log x \cdot d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2} - [ log x ∫ x dx -∫( \frac {d}{dx} (log x)∫ x dx) dx]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2} - [ \frac {x²}{2}\log x - ∫(\frac1x \cdot\frac{x^2}{2} ) dx{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{2}(\log x)^{2}-\frac{x^{2}}{2} \log x+\frac{x^{2}}{4}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int {\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)\log xdx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {\left( {\log x} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)dx} {/tex} [Applying product rule] {tex}= \log x\left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right) - \int {\frac{1}{x}\left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)dx}{/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \int {\left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{3} + 1} \right)dx}{/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \frac{1}{3}\int {{x^2}dx - \int {1dx} } {/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \frac{1}{3}\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - x + c{/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \frac{{{x^3}}}{9} - x + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int {\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)\log xdx} {/tex}
{tex} = \int {\left( {\log x} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)dx} {/tex}
[Applying product rule]
{tex}= \log x\left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right) - \int {\frac{1}{x}\left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)dx}{/tex}
{tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \int {\left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{3} + 1} \right)dx}{/tex}
{tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \frac{1}{3}\int {{x^2}dx - \int {1dx} } {/tex}
{tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \frac{1}{3}\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - x + c{/tex}
{tex}= \left( {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right)\log x - \frac{{{x^3}}}{9} - x + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function e x (sin x + cos x)
solution_text: {tex}I=\int e^{x}(\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} Now, Let sin x = f(x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f'(x) = cos x We know that, {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right\} d x=e^{x} f(x)+c{/tex} Thus, {tex}\int e^{x}(\sin x+\cos x) d x=e^{x} \sin x+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int e^{x}(\sin x+\cos x) d x{/tex} 
Now,
Let sin x = f(x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f'(x) = cos x 
We know that,
{tex}\int e^{x}\left\{f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right\} d x=e^{x} f(x)+c{/tex} 
Thus,
{tex}\int e^{x}(\sin x+\cos x) d x=e^{x} \sin x+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}I=\int \frac{x e^{x}}{(1+x)^{2}} d x=\int e^{x}\left\{\frac{x}{(1+x)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{\frac{1+x-1}{(1+x)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{(1+x)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} Now, Let {tex}\frac{1}{1+x}=f(x) \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=-\frac{1}{(1+x)^{2}}{/tex} We know that, {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right\} d x=e^{x} f(x)+C{/tex} Thus, {tex}\int \frac{x e^{x}}{(1+x)^{2}} d x=\frac{e^{x}}{1+x}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \frac{x e^{x}}{(1+x)^{2}} d x=\int e^{x}\left\{\frac{x}{(1+x)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{\frac{1+x-1}{(1+x)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int e^{x}\left\{\frac{1}{1+x}-\frac{1}{(1+x)^{2}}\right\} d x{/tex} 
Now,
Let {tex}\frac{1}{1+x}=f(x) \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=-\frac{1}{(1+x)^{2}}{/tex} 
We know that,
{tex}\int e^{x}\left\{f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right\} d x=e^{x} f(x)+C{/tex}
Thus,
{tex}\int \frac{x e^{x}}{(1+x)^{2}} d x=\frac{e^{x}}{1+x}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int {{e^x}\frac{{1 + \sin x}}{{1 + \cos x}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {{e^x}.\frac{{1 + 2\sin \frac{x}{2}\cos \frac{x}{2}}}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int {{e^x}\left[ {\frac{1}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}} + \frac{{2\sin \frac{x}{2}\cos \frac{x}{2}}}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}} \right]dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {{e^x}\left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sec }^2}\frac{x}{2} + \tan \frac{x}{2}} \right)dx}{/tex} {tex}= \int {{e^x}\left( {\tan \frac{x}{2} + \frac{1}{2}{{\sec }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}} dx} \right]{/tex} It is in the form of {tex}\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}} dx{/tex} since here {tex}f\left( x \right) = \tan \frac{x}{2}{/tex} and {tex}f'\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{2}{\sec ^2}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} {tex} = {e^x}\tan \frac{x}{2} + c{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx = {e^x}f\left( x \right) + c} } \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {{e^x}\frac{{1 + \sin x}}{{1 + \cos x}}dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {{e^x}.\frac{{1 + 2\sin \frac{x}{2}\cos \frac{x}{2}}}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int {{e^x}\left[ {\frac{1}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}} + \frac{{2\sin \frac{x}{2}\cos \frac{x}{2}}}{{2{{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}}}} \right]dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int {{e^x}\left( {\frac{1}{2}{{\sec }^2}\frac{x}{2} + \tan \frac{x}{2}} \right)dx}{/tex}

{tex}= \int {{e^x}\left( {\tan \frac{x}{2} + \frac{1}{2}{{\sec }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)} dx{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}} dx} \right]{/tex}

It is in the form of {tex}\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}} dx{/tex} since here {tex}f\left( x \right) = \tan \frac{x}{2}{/tex} and {tex}f'\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{2}{\sec ^2}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} 

{tex} = {e^x}\tan \frac{x}{2} + c{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\because \int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx = {e^x}f\left( x \right) + c} } \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = {e^x}\left( {\frac{1}{x} - \frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} \right)dx{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} } \right]{/tex} It is in the form of {tex}{\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} }{/tex} , Here {tex}f\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{x} = {x^{ - 1}}{/tex} and {tex}f'\left( x \right) = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = {e^x}\frac{1}{x} + c{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{e^x}}}{x} + c{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}} dx = {e^x}f\left( x \right) + c} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = {e^x}\left( {\frac{1}{x} - \frac{1}{{{x^2}}}} \right)dx{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} } \right]{/tex}

It is in the form of {tex}{\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} }{/tex}, Here {tex}f\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{x} = {x^{ - 1}}{/tex} and {tex}f'\left( x \right) = \frac{{ - 1}}{{{x^2}}}{/tex} 

{tex} \Rightarrow I = {e^x}\frac{1}{x} + c{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{{e^x}}}{x} + c{/tex}   ...{tex}\left[ {\because \int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}} dx = {e^x}f\left( x \right) + c} \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \frac{{\left( {x - 3} \right){e^x}}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {x - 1} \right) - 2}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}{e^x}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int {{e^x}\left[ {\frac{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}} - \frac{2}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}} \right]} dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int {{e^x}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^2}}} + \frac{{ - 2}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}} \right]} dx{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} } \right]{/tex} It is in the form of {tex}{\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} }{/tex} since here {tex}f\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} and {tex}f'\left( x \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left\{ {{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^{ - 2}}} \right\}{/tex} {tex} = - 2{\left( {x - 1} \right)^{ - 3}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 2}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}{/tex} . {tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{{{e^x}}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^2}}} + c{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx = {e^x}f\left( x \right) + c} } \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \frac{{\left( {x - 3} \right){e^x}}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {\frac{{\left( {x - 1} \right) - 2}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}{e^x}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int {{e^x}\left[ {\frac{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}} - \frac{2}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}} \right]} dx{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int {{e^x}\left[ {\frac{1}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^2}}} + \frac{{ - 2}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}} \right]} dx{/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} } \right]{/tex}
It is in the form of {tex}{\int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx} }{/tex} since here {tex}f\left( x \right) = \frac{1}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^2}}}{/tex} and {tex}f'\left( x \right) = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left\{ {{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^{ - 2}}} \right\}{/tex}
{tex} = - 2{\left( {x - 1} \right)^{ - 3}}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{ - 2}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^3}}}{/tex}.
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{{{e^x}}}{{{{\left( {x - 1} \right)}^2}}} + c{/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int {{e^x}\left\{ {f\left( x \right) + f'\left( x \right)} \right\}dx = {e^x}f\left( x \right) + c} } \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function e 2x sin x
solution_text: Let {tex}I{/tex} = e 2x sin x Integrating by parts, we get, {tex}I=\sin x \int e^{2 x} d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \sin x\right) \int e^{2 x} d x\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\sin x \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2}-\int \cos x \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int e^{2 x} \cos x d x{/tex} Again, integrating by parts, we get, {tex}I=\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\left[\cos x \int e^{2 x} d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \cos x\right) \int e^{2 x} d x\right\} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\left[\cos x \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2}-\int(-\sin x) \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}-\frac{1}{4} I{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I+\frac{1}{4} I=\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5}{4} I=\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4}{5}\left[\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I{/tex} = e2x sin x
Integrating by parts, we get,
{tex}I=\sin x \int e^{2 x} d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \sin x\right) \int e^{2 x} d x\right\} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\sin x \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2}-\int \cos x \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \int e^{2 x} \cos x d x{/tex} 
Again, integrating by parts, we get,
{tex}I=\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\left[\cos x \int e^{2 x} d x-\int\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d x} \cos x\right) \int e^{2 x} d x\right\} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\left[\cos x \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2}-\int(-\sin x) \cdot \frac{e^{2 x}}{2} d x\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}-\frac{1}{4} I{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I+\frac{1}{4} I=\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5}{4} I=\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4}{5}\left[\frac{e^{2 x} \sin x}{2}-\frac{e^{2 x} \cos x}{4}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Putting {tex}x = \tan \theta {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow dx = {\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex} {tex}\therefore \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\tan \theta }}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta }}} \right).{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta }{/tex} {tex}= \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\sin 2\theta } \right).{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } {/tex} {tex}= \int {2\theta {{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta }{/tex} {tex} = 2\int {\theta {{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } {/tex} ...[Applying product rule] {tex}= 2\left[ {\theta .\tan \theta - \int {1.\tan \theta d\theta } } \right]{/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {\theta .\tan \theta - \int {\tan \theta d\theta } } \right]{/tex} {tex} = 2\left[ {\theta \tan \theta - \log \sec \theta } \right] + c{/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x.x - \log \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} } \right] + c{/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {x{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x - \frac{1}{2}\log \left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)} \right] + c{/tex} {tex}= 2x{\tan ^{ - 1}}x - \log \left( {1 + {x^2}} \right) + c{/tex}
solution_html:
Putting {tex}x = \tan \theta {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow dx = {\sec ^2}\theta d\theta {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2x}}{{1 + {x^2}}}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{2\tan \theta }}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\theta }}} \right).{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta }{/tex}
{tex}= \int {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\sin 2\theta } \right).{{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } {/tex}
{tex}= \int {2\theta {{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta }{/tex}
{tex} = 2\int {\theta {{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } {/tex}   ...[Applying product rule]

solution_text: {tex}I=\int \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+6} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+4+2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sqrt{(x+2)^{2}+(\sqrt{2})^{2}} d x{/tex} We know that, {tex}\int \sqrt{x^{2}+a^{2}} d x=\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}+a^{2}}+\frac{a^{2}}{2} \log |x+\sqrt{x^{2}+a^{2}}|+C{/tex} Therefore, {tex}I=\frac{(x+2)}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+6}+\frac{2}{2} \log |(x+2)+\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+6}|+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(x+2)}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+6}+\log |(x+2)+\sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+6}|+C{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int \sqrt{x^{2}+4 x+1} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \sqrt{\left(x^{2}+4 x+4\right)-3} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sqrt{(x+2)^{2}-(\sqrt{3})^{2}} d x{/tex} We know that {tex}\int \sqrt{(x)^{2}-(a)^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}+a^{2}}-\frac{a^{2}}{2} \log |x+\sqrt{x^{2}-a^{2}}|+C{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{(\mathrm{x}+2)}{2} \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}+4 \mathrm{x}+1}-\frac{3}{2} \log |(\mathrm{x}+2)+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}+4 \mathrm{x}+1}|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}I=\int \sqrt{1-4 x-x^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sqrt{1-\left(x^{2}+4 x+4-4\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sqrt{1+4-(x+2)^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sqrt{(\sqrt{5})^{2}-(x+2)^{2}} d x{/tex} We know that, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}} d x=\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}+\frac{a^{2}}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{(\mathrm{x}+2)}{2} \sqrt{1-4 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x}^{2}}+\frac{5}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}+2}{\sqrt{5}}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}I=\int \sqrt{1+3 x-x^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sqrt{1-\left(x^{2}-3 x+\frac{9}{4}-\frac{9}{4}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \sqrt{\left(1+\frac{9}{4}\right)-\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\left(\frac{\sqrt{13}}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} We know that, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}} d x=\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}+\frac{a^{2}}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+C{/tex} Therefore, {tex}I=\frac{x-\frac{3}{2}}{2} \sqrt{1+3 x-x^{2}}+\frac{13}{4 \times 2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{13}}{2}}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 x-3}{4} \sqrt{1+3 x-x^{2}}+\frac{13}{8} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x-3}{\sqrt{13}}\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \sqrt{1+3 x-x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \sqrt{1-\left(x^{2}-3 x+\frac{9}{4}-\frac{9}{4}\right)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \sqrt{\left(1+\frac{9}{4}\right)-\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{\left(\frac{\sqrt{13}}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} 
We know that,
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}} d x=\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{a^{2}-x^{2}}+\frac{a^{2}}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+C{/tex} 
Therefore, 
{tex}I=\frac{x-\frac{3}{2}}{2} \sqrt{1+3 x-x^{2}}+\frac{13}{4 \times 2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{13}}{2}}\right)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2 x-3}{4} \sqrt{1+3 x-x^{2}}+\frac{13}{8} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x-3}{\sqrt{13}}\right)+C{/tex}

We know that

Therefore,
{tex}I=\frac{\left(x+\frac{3}{2}\right)}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}+3 x}-\frac{9}{8} \log \left|\left(x+\frac{3}{2}\right)+\sqrt{x^{2}+3 x}\right|+C{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{(2 \mathrm{x}+3)}{4} \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}+3 \mathrm{x}}-\frac{9}{8} \log \left|\left(\mathrm{x}+\frac{3}{2}\right)+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}+3 \mathrm{x}}\right|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
