subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x{/tex} {tex}I=\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{-1}^{1} \mathrm{x} \mathrm{dx}+\int_{-1}^{1} 1 \cdot \mathrm{dx} ~~~~~\left[As \int \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}+1}}{\mathrm{n}+1}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{-1}^{1}+[x]_{-1}^{1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\left[\frac{1^{2}}{2}-\frac{(-1)^{2}}{2}\right]+[1-(-1)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[\frac{1}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\right]+[1+1]=0+2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = 2 {tex}\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x=2{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x{/tex} 
{tex}I=\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{-1}^{1} \mathrm{x} \mathrm{dx}+\int_{-1}^{1} 1 \cdot \mathrm{dx} ~~~~~\left[As \int \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{n}+1}}{\mathrm{n}+1}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{-1}^{1}+[x]_{-1}^{1}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\left[\frac{1^{2}}{2}-\frac{(-1)^{2}}{2}\right]+[1-(-1)]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[\frac{1}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\right]+[1+1]=0+2{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = 2
{tex}\int_{-1}^{1}(x+1) d x=2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int _ { 2 } ^ { 3 } \frac { 1 } { x } d x.{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int _ { 2 } ^ { 3 } \frac { 1 } { x } d x.{/tex}
solution_text: According to the question , {tex}I =\int _ { 2 } ^ { 3 } \frac { 1 } { x } d x{/tex} {tex} = [ \log | x | ] _ { 2 } ^ { 3 }{/tex} {tex} = \log 3 - \log 2 {/tex} {tex}= \log \frac { 3 } { 2 }\left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \frac { m } { n } \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question , {tex}I =\int _ { 2 } ^ { 3 } \frac { 1 } { x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = [ \log | x | ] _ { 2 } ^ { 3 }{/tex}
{tex} = \log 3 - \log 2 {/tex}
{tex}= \log \frac { 3 } { 2 }\left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \frac { m } { n } \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{1}^{2}\left(4 x^{3}-5 x^{2}+6 x+9\right) d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{1}^{2}\left(4 x^{3}-5 x^{2}+6 x+9\right) d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int_{1}^{2}\left(4 x^{3}-5 x^{2}+6 x+9\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{2}\left(4 \mathrm{x}^{3}-5 \mathrm{x}^{2}+6 \mathrm{x}+9\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{2} 4 \mathrm{x}^{3} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{1}^{2} 5 \mathrm{x}^{2} \mathrm{dx}+\int_{1}^{2} 6 \mathrm{xdx}+\int_{1}^{2} 9 \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=4 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{3} \mathrm{dx}-5 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2} \mathrm{dx}+6 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{xdx}+9 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}4 \times\left[\frac{x^{3+1}}{3+1}\right]_{1}^{2}-5 \times\left[\frac{x^{2+1}}{2+1}\right]_{1}^{2}+6 \times\left[\frac{x^{1+1}}{1+1}\right]_{1}^{2}+9 \times\left[\frac{x^{0+1}}{0+1}\right]_{1}^{2}{/tex} [ {tex}\int x^{n} d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} I = {tex}4 \times\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{4}}{4}\right]_{1}^{2}-5 \times\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{3}}{3}\right]_{1}^{2}+6 \times\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}\right]_{1}^{2}+9 \times[\mathrm{x}]_{1}^{2}{/tex} = {tex}2^{4}-1^{4}-5\left[\frac{2^{3}}{3}-\frac{1^{3}}{3}\right]+6\left[\frac{2^{2}}{2}-\frac{1^{2}}{2}\right]+9(2-1){/tex} = {tex}16-1-5\left[\frac{7}{3}\right]+3(3)+9{/tex} = {tex}33-\frac{35}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{99-35}{3}=\frac{64}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_{1}^{2}\left(4 x^{3}-5 x^{2}+6 x+9\right) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{2}\left(4 \mathrm{x}^{3}-5 \mathrm{x}^{2}+6 \mathrm{x}+9\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{1}^{2} 4 \mathrm{x}^{3} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{1}^{2} 5 \mathrm{x}^{2} \mathrm{dx}+\int_{1}^{2} 6 \mathrm{xdx}+\int_{1}^{2} 9 \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=4 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{3} \mathrm{dx}-5 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{x}^{2} \mathrm{dx}+6 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{xdx}+9 \int_{1}^{2} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}4 \times\left[\frac{x^{3+1}}{3+1}\right]_{1}^{2}-5 \times\left[\frac{x^{2+1}}{2+1}\right]_{1}^{2}+6 \times\left[\frac{x^{1+1}}{1+1}\right]_{1}^{2}+9 \times\left[\frac{x^{0+1}}{0+1}\right]_{1}^{2}{/tex} [{tex}\int x^{n} d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}{/tex} ]
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} I = {tex}4 \times\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{4}}{4}\right]_{1}^{2}-5 \times\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{3}}{3}\right]_{1}^{2}+6 \times\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}\right]_{1}^{2}+9 \times[\mathrm{x}]_{1}^{2}{/tex} 
= {tex}2^{4}-1^{4}-5\left[\frac{2^{3}}{3}-\frac{1^{3}}{3}\right]+6\left[\frac{2^{2}}{2}-\frac{1^{2}}{2}\right]+9(2-1){/tex} 
= {tex}16-1-5\left[\frac{7}{3}\right]+3(3)+9{/tex} 
= {tex}33-\frac{35}{3}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{99-35}{3}=\frac{64}{3}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\sin 2xdx}{/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{ - \cos 2x}}{2}} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{ - \cos \frac{\pi }{2}}}{2} - \left( {\frac{{ - \cos {0^o}}}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex} = \frac{0}{2} - \left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex}= 0 + \frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\sin 2xdx}{/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{ - \cos 2x}}{2}} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{ - \cos \frac{\pi }{2}}}{2} - \left( {\frac{{ - \cos {0^o}}}{2}} \right){/tex}
{tex} = \frac{0}{2} - \left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}} \right){/tex}
{tex}= 0 + \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos 2 x d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos 2 x d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\left[\frac{\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{2}\right]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex} [ {tex}\int \cos x d x=\sin x+c{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{2}\left(\sin 2 \times \frac{\pi}{2}-\sin 2 \times 0\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}(\sin \pi-\sin 0){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (0 - 0) = 0 {tex}\therefore \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos 2 x d x=0{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos 2 x d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\left[\frac{\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{2}\right]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex}  [{tex}\int \cos x d x=\sin x+c{/tex} ]
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{2}\left(\sin 2 \times \frac{\pi}{2}-\sin 2 \times 0\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}(\sin \pi-\sin 0){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(0 - 0) = 0
{tex}\therefore \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos 2 x d x=0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{4}^{5} e^{x} d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{4}^{5} e^{x} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int_{4}^{5} e^{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[e^{x}\right]_{4}^{5}=e^{5}-e^{4}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{4}^{5} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{e}^{4}(\mathrm{e}-1){/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_{4}^{5} e^{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[e^{x}\right]_{4}^{5}=e^{5}-e^{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \int_{4}^{5} \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{e}^{4}(\mathrm{e}-1){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \tan x d x{/tex} .
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \tan x d x{/tex}.
solution_text: {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \tan x d x = [ \log | \sec x | ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 }{/tex} {tex}{ = \log \left| \sec \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right| - \log | \sec 0 | } \\ {/tex} {tex}{ = \log | \sqrt { 2 } | - \log | 1 | } {/tex} {tex}\\ { = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log 2 } {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \tan x d x = [ \log | \sec x | ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 }{/tex}
{tex}{ = \log \left| \sec \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right| - \log | \sec 0 | } \\ {/tex}
{tex}{ = \log | \sqrt { 2 } | - \log | 1 | } {/tex}
{tex}\\ { = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log 2 } {/tex}

solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{6}}^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\cos ecxdx} {/tex} {tex}= \left( {\log \left| {\cos ecx - \cot x} \right|} \right)_{\frac{\pi }{6}}^{\frac{\pi }{4}}{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {\cos ec\frac{\pi }{4} - \cot \frac{\pi }{4}} \right| - \log \left| {\cos ec\frac{\pi }{6} - \cot \frac{\pi }{6}} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right| - \log \left| {2 - \sqrt 3 } \right|{/tex} {tex}= \log \left( {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right) - \log \left( {2 - \sqrt 3 } \right){/tex} {tex}= \log \left( {\frac{{\sqrt 2 - 1}}{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}} \right){/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{6}}^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\cos ecxdx} {/tex}

{tex}= \left( {\log \left| {\cos ecx - \cot x} \right|} \right)_{\frac{\pi }{6}}^{\frac{\pi }{4}}{/tex}

{tex}= \log \left| {\cos ec\frac{\pi }{4} - \cot \frac{\pi }{4}} \right| - \log \left| {\cos ec\frac{\pi }{6} - \cot \frac{\pi }{6}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}= \log \left| {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right| - \log \left| {2 - \sqrt 3 } \right|{/tex}

{tex}= \log \left( {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right) - \log \left( {2 - \sqrt 3 } \right){/tex}

{tex}= \log \left( {\frac{{\sqrt 2 - 1}}{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}} \right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: According to the question , {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \quad \left[ \because \int_b^a \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x = \sin ^ { - 1 } (a) - \sin ^ { - 1 } (b) \right]{/tex} {tex}= sin^{-1}(1)-sin^{-1}(0){/tex} {tex}= \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \sin \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) - \sin ^ { - 1 } ( \sin 0 ) {/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question , {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x{/tex}
= {tex}\left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \quad \left[ \because \int_b^a \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x = \sin ^ { - 1 } (a) - \sin ^ { - 1 } (b) \right]{/tex}
{tex}= sin^{-1}(1)-sin^{-1}(0){/tex}
{tex}= \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \sin \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) - \sin ^ { - 1 } ( \sin 0 ) {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \frac{d x}{1+x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \frac{d x}{1+x^{2}}{/tex} We know that {tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex} Therefore, I = {tex}\left[\tan ^{-1} x\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex} = tan -1 (1) - tan -1 (0) = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} - 0 = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d x}{1+x^{2}}=\frac{\pi}{ 4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \frac{d x}{1+x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \frac{d x}{1+x^{2}}{/tex}
We know that
{tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex} 
Therefore,
I = {tex}\left[\tan ^{-1} x\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex} 
= tan-1(1) - tan-1(0) = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} - 0
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore \int_{0}^{1} \frac{d x}{1+x^{2}}=\frac{\pi}{ 4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_2^3 {\frac{{dx}}{{{x^2} - 1}}} = \int\limits_2^3 {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - {1^2}}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{1}{{2\left( 1 \right)}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right|} \right)_2^3{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{{3 - 1}}{{3 + 1}}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{{2 - 1}}{{2 + 1}}} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{1}{2}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{1}{3}} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\log \frac{1}{2} - \log \frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \frac{{\frac{1}{2}}}{{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{2}\log \frac{3}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_2^3 {\frac{{dx}}{{{x^2} - 1}}} = \int\limits_2^3 {\frac{1}{{{x^2} - {1^2}}}dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \left( {\frac{1}{{2\left( 1 \right)}}\log \left| {\frac{{x - 1}}{{x + 1}}} \right|} \right)_2^3{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{{3 - 1}}{{3 + 1}}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{{2 - 1}}{{2 + 1}}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{1}{2}} \right| - \frac{1}{2}\log \left| {\frac{1}{3}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\log \frac{1}{2} - \log \frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log \frac{{\frac{1}{2}}}{{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{2}\log \frac{3}{2}{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{1 + \cos 2x}}{2}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {1 + \cos 2x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {x + \frac{{\sin 2x}}{2}} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{\pi }{2} + \frac{1}{2}\sin \pi - \left( {0 + \frac{1}{2}\sin {0^o}} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{\pi }{2} + 0 - 0} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{1 + \cos 2x}}{2}dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {1 + \cos 2x} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {x + \frac{{\sin 2x}}{2}} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{\pi }{2} + \frac{1}{2}\sin \pi - \left( {0 + \frac{1}{2}\sin {0^o}} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{\pi }{2} + 0 - 0} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_2^3 {\frac{{xdx}}{{{x^2} + 1}}} = \frac{1}{2}\int\limits_2^3 {\frac{{2x}}{{{x^2} + 1}}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\log \left| {{x^2} + 1} \right|} \right)_2^3{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\log \left| {10} \right| - \log \left| 5 \right|} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\log 10 - \log 5} \right){/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{2}\log \frac{{10}}{5}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\log 2{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left( {\log \left| {{x^2} + 1} \right|} \right)_2^3{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 x+3}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 x+3}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 x+3}{5 x^{2}+1}{/tex} Multiplying by 5 in numerator and denominator {tex}I=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{5(2 x+3)}{5 x^{2}+1} d x=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x+15}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x}{5 x^{2}+1} d x+3 \int_{0}^{1} \frac{5}{5 x^{2}+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = I 1 + I 2 {tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} Let 5x 2 + 1 = t …(i) 10x dx = dt …(ii) When x = 0; t = 1 When x = 1; t = 6 Substituting (i) and (ii) in I 1 {tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5} \int_{1}^{6} \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{5}[\log | t|]_{1}^{6}{/tex} {tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5}(\log |6|-\log |1|)=\frac{1}{5}(\log 6-0){/tex} {tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x}{5 x^{2}+1} d x=\frac{\log 6}{5}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{I}_{2}=3 \int {\frac{5}{5 \mathrm{x}^{2}+1}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{3}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+\frac{1}{5}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} [ {tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex} ] {tex}I_{2}=\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{1}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}}\left[\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5} x\right]_{0}^{1}=\frac{3}{5} \times \sqrt{5}\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}-\tan ^{-1} 0\right){/tex} I 2 = {tex}\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} I = I 1 + I 2 {tex}\therefore \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{5} \log 6+{\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}} \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 x+3}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{5} \log 6+{\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}} \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 x+3}{5 x^{2}+1}{/tex} 
Multiplying by 5 in numerator and denominator
{tex}I=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{5(2 x+3)}{5 x^{2}+1} d x=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x+15}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x}{5 x^{2}+1} d x+3 \int_{0}^{1} \frac{5}{5 x^{2}+1}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = I1 + I2 
{tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} 
Let 5x2 + 1 = t …(i)
10x dx = dt …(ii)
When x = 0; t = 1
When x = 1; t = 6
Substituting (i) and (ii) in I1
{tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5} \int_{1}^{6} \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{5}[\log | t|]_{1}^{6}{/tex}
{tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5}(\log |6|-\log |1|)=\frac{1}{5}(\log 6-0){/tex} 
{tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{10 x}{5 x^{2}+1} d x=\frac{\log 6}{5}{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm{I}_{2}=3 \int {\frac{5}{5 \mathrm{x}^{2}+1}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{3}{5} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+\frac{1}{5}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex}    [{tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex}]
{tex}I_{2}=\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{1}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}}\left[\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5} x\right]_{0}^{1}=\frac{3}{5} \times \sqrt{5}\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}-\tan ^{-1} 0\right){/tex} 
I2 =  {tex}\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}{/tex} 
{tex}\because{/tex} I = I1 + I2
{tex}\therefore \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{5} \log 6+{\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}} \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}{/tex} 
{tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{2 x+3}{5 x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} =  {tex}\frac{1}{5} \log 6+{\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}} \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{5}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x.{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x.{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow d x = \frac { d t } { 2 x }{/tex} Lower limit when x = 0, then t = 0 Upper limit when x = 1, then t = 1. {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { t } \frac { d t } { 2 x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } e ^ { t } d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { t } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { 1 } - e ^ { 0 } \right] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ e - 1 ]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow d x = \frac { d t } { 2 x }{/tex}
Lower limit when x = 0, then t = 0
Upper limit when x = 1, then t = 1.
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { t } \frac { d t } { 2 x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } e ^ { t } d t{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { t } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { 1 } - e ^ { 0 } \right] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ e - 1 ]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex} \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 }{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex} \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 }{/tex}
solution_text: According to the question, {tex} I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex} {tex} I = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + \frac { - 4 x - 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } d x - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = 5 [ x ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } d x{/tex} Using partial fraction, let {tex} \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 3 } + \frac { B } { x + 1 }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A ( x + 1 ) + B ( x + 3 ) } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4x + 3 = A(x + 1) + B(x + 3){/tex} Comparing the coefficients of like from both sides, {tex}\Rightarrow A + B = 4 \Rightarrow A = 4 - B{/tex} and A + 3B = 3 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 - B + 3B = 3{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 },{/tex} then {tex} A = 4 + \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { 9 } { 2 }{/tex} Now, from Equation (i), we get {tex} I = 5 ( 2 - 1 ) - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 9 / 2 } { x + 3 } + \frac { - 1 / 2 } { x + 1 } \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log | x + 3 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | x + 1 | \right] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5\left. {\left[ {\left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 5 - \frac{1}{2}\log 3} \right)} \right. - \left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 4 - \frac{1}{2}\log 2} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } ( \log 5 - \log 4 ) - \frac { 1 } { 2 } ( \log 3 - \log 4 ]\right.{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right]{/tex} {tex} = 5 - \frac { 45 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } + \frac { 5 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex} I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex}
{tex} I = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + \frac { - 4 x - 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } d x - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = 5 [ x ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } d x{/tex}
Using partial fraction,
let {tex} \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 3 } + \frac { B } { x + 1 }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A ( x + 1 ) + B ( x + 3 ) } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4x + 3 = A(x + 1) + B(x + 3){/tex}
Comparing the coefficients of like from both sides,
{tex}\Rightarrow A + B = 4 \Rightarrow A = 4 - B{/tex}
and A + 3B  = 3{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 - B + 3B = 3{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 },{/tex}then {tex} A = 4 + \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { 9 } { 2 }{/tex}
Now, from Equation (i), we get
{tex} I = 5 ( 2 - 1 ) - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 9 / 2 } { x + 3 } + \frac { - 1 / 2 } { x + 1 } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log | x + 3 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | x + 1 | \right] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5\left. {\left[ {\left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 5 - \frac{1}{2}\log 3} \right)} \right. - \left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 4 - \frac{1}{2}\log 2} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } ( \log 5 - \log 4 ) - \frac { 1 } { 2 } ( \log 3 - \log 4 ]\right.{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - \frac { 45 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } + \frac { 5 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\left( {2{{\sec }^2}x + {x^3} + 2} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {{{\sec }^2}xdx + \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {{x^3}dx + 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {1dx} } } {/tex} {tex}= 2\left( {\tan x} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} + \left( {\frac{{{x^4}}}{4}} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} + 2\left( x \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}}{/tex} {tex}= 2\left( {\tan \frac{\pi }{4} - \tan {0^o}} \right) + \frac{{{{\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right)}^4}}}{4} - 0 {/tex} {tex}+ 2\left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 0} \right){/tex} {tex}= 2\left( {1 - 0} \right) + \frac{{\left( {\frac{{{\pi ^4}}}{{256}}} \right)}}{4} + \frac{{2\pi }}{4}{/tex} {tex}= 2 + \frac{{{\pi ^4}}}{{1024}} + \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{\pi ^4}}}{{1024}} + \frac{\pi }{2} + 2{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {\left( {2{{\sec }^2}x + {x^3} + 2} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {{{\sec }^2}xdx + \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {{x^3}dx + 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {1dx} } } {/tex}
{tex}= 2\left( {\tan x} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} + \left( {\frac{{{x^4}}}{4}} \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} + 2\left( x \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}}{/tex}
{tex}= 2\left( {\tan \frac{\pi }{4} - \tan {0^o}} \right) + \frac{{{{\left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right)}^4}}}{4} - 0 {/tex} {tex}+ 2\left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - 0} \right){/tex}
{tex}= 2\left( {1 - 0} \right) + \frac{{\left( {\frac{{{\pi ^4}}}{{256}}} \right)}}{4} + \frac{{2\pi }}{4}{/tex}
{tex}= 2 + \frac{{{\pi ^4}}}{{1024}} + \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{{\pi ^4}}}{{1024}} + \frac{\pi }{2} + 2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2} - {{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {\frac{{1 - \cos x}}{2} - \frac{{1 + \cos x}}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {\frac{{1 - \cos x - 1 - \cos x}}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {\frac{{ - 2\cos x}}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = - \int\limits_0^\pi {\cos xdx} {/tex} {tex} = - \left( {\sin x} \right)_0^\pi {/tex} {tex}= - \left( {\sin \pi - \sin {0^o}} \right){/tex} = -(0 - 0) = 0
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {{{\sin }^2}\frac{x}{2} - {{\cos }^2}\frac{x}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {\frac{{1 - \cos x}}{2} - \frac{{1 + \cos x}}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {\frac{{1 - \cos x - 1 - \cos x}}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex} = \int\limits_0^\pi {\left( {\frac{{ - 2\cos x}}{2}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex} = - \int\limits_0^\pi {\cos xdx} {/tex}
{tex} = - \left( {\sin x} \right)_0^\pi {/tex}
{tex}= - \left( {\sin \pi - \sin {0^o}} \right){/tex}
= -(0 - 0) = 0

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{6 x+3}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{6 x+3}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{2} \frac{6 x+3}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex} {tex}I=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{2 x+1}{x^{2}+4}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+4} d x+3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{1}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = I 1 + I 2 {tex}I_{1}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex} Let x 2 + 4 = t 2x dx = dt When x = 0; t = 4 When x = 2; t = 2 2 + 4 = 8 Substituting t and dt in I 1 {tex}\left.\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=3 \int_{4}^{8} \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{t}}=3[\log | \mathrm{t}]\right]_{4}^{8}{/tex} [ {tex}\int \frac{1}{x} d x=\log x{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I 1 = 3 [log |8| - log |4|] = 3 log {tex}\frac{8}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I 1 = 3 log |2| {tex}\mathrm{I}_{2}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+4} \mathrm{dx}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+2^{2}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} [ {tex}\frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow I_{2}=3 \times \frac{1}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right]_{0}^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} \frac{2}{2}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{0}{2}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} 1-\tan ^{-1} 0\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I_{2}=\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{3 \pi}{ 8}{/tex} Now I = I 1 + I 2 I = 3 log 2 + {tex}\frac{3\pi}{8}{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{6 x+3}{x^{2}+4} d x=3 \log 2+\frac{3 \pi}{8}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{2} \frac{6 x+3}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex} 
{tex}I=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{2 x+1}{x^{2}+4}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+4} d x+3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{1}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = I1 + I2 
{tex}I_{1}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+4} d x{/tex} 
Let x2 + 4 = t
2x dx = dt
When x = 0; t = 4
When x = 2; t = 22 + 4 = 8
Substituting t and dt in I1 
{tex}\left.\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=3 \int_{4}^{8} \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{t}}=3[\log | \mathrm{t}]\right]_{4}^{8}{/tex} [{tex}\int \frac{1}{x} d x=\log x{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I1 = 3 [log |8| - log |4|] = 3 log {tex}\frac{8}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I1 = 3 log |2|  
{tex}\mathrm{I}_{2}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+4} \mathrm{dx}=3 \int_{0}^{2} \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^{2}+2^{2}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} [{tex}\frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{2}=3 \times \frac{1}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right]_{0}^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} \frac{2}{2}-\tan ^{-1} \frac{0}{2}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} 1-\tan ^{-1} 0\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I_{2}=\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{3 \pi}{ 8}{/tex} 
Now I = I1 + I2 
I = 3 log 2 + {tex}\frac{3\pi}{8}{/tex}
{tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{6 x+3}{x^{2}+4} d x=3 \log 2+\frac{3 \pi}{8}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {\left( {x{e^x} + \sin \frac{{\pi x}}{4}} \right)dx} ={/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {x{e^x}dx + \int\limits_0^1 {\sin \frac{{\pi x}}{4}dx} } {/tex} [Applying Product Rule on first definite integral] {tex}= \left( {x{e^x}} \right)_0^1 - \int\limits_0^1 {1.{e^x}dx - \frac{{\left( {\cos \frac{{\pi x}}{4}} \right)_0^1}}{{{ }{} {\text{ }}\frac{\pi }{4}}}} {/tex} {tex} = {e^1} - 0 - \int\limits_0^1 {{e^x}dx - \frac{4}{\pi }\left[ {\cos \frac{\pi }{4} - \cos {0^o}} \right]} {/tex} {tex}= e - \left( {{e^x}} \right)_0^1 - \frac{4}{\pi }\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} - 1} \right){/tex} {tex} = e - \left( {e - {e^0}} \right) - \frac{4}{{\pi \sqrt 2 }} + \frac{4}{\pi }{/tex} {tex} = e - e + 1 - \frac{{2.2}}{{\pi \sqrt 2 }} + \frac{4}{\pi }{/tex} {tex} = 1 + \frac{4}{\pi } - \frac{{2\sqrt 2 }}{\pi }{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_0^1 {\left( {x{e^x} + \sin \frac{{\pi x}}{4}} \right)dx} ={/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {x{e^x}dx + \int\limits_0^1 {\sin \frac{{\pi x}}{4}dx} } {/tex}
[Applying Product Rule on first definite integral]
{tex}= \left( {x{e^x}} \right)_0^1 - \int\limits_0^1 {1.{e^x}dx - \frac{{\left( {\cos \frac{{\pi x}}{4}} \right)_0^1}}{{{ }{} {\text{ }}\frac{\pi }{4}}}} {/tex}
{tex} = {e^1} - 0 - \int\limits_0^1 {{e^x}dx - \frac{4}{\pi }\left[ {\cos \frac{\pi }{4} - \cos {0^o}} \right]} {/tex}
{tex}= e - \left( {{e^x}} \right)_0^1 - \frac{4}{\pi }\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} - 1} \right){/tex}
{tex} = e - \left( {e - {e^0}} \right) - \frac{4}{{\pi \sqrt 2 }} + \frac{4}{\pi }{/tex}
{tex} = e - e + 1 - \frac{{2.2}}{{\pi \sqrt 2 }} + \frac{4}{\pi }{/tex}
{tex} = 1 + \frac{4}{\pi } - \frac{{2\sqrt 2 }}{\pi }{/tex}

options.explanation: Let {tex}I=\int_{1}^{\sqrt{3}} \frac{d x}{x^{2}+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{1}^{\sqrt{3}} \frac{d x}{x^{2}+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[\tan ^{-1} x\right]_{1}^{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{3}-\tan ^{-1} 1\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{3}-\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} ...[ {tex}\because~\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{4 \pi-3 \pi}{12}=\frac{\pi}{12}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{1}^{\sqrt{3}} \frac{d x}{x^{2}+1}=\frac{\pi}{12}{/tex}

Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{2}{3}} \frac{d x}{4+9 x^{2}}{/tex} Taking 9 common from Denominator in I {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{9} \int_{0}^{\frac{2}{3}} \frac{d x}{\frac{4}{9}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{9} \int_{0}^{\frac{2}{3}} \frac{d x}{\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{2}+x^{2}}{/tex} [ {tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+c{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{9} \times \frac{3}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{\frac{2}{3}}\right]_{0}^{\frac{2}{3}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{9} \times \frac{3}{2}\left[\tan ^{-1} \frac{3 x}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{2}{3}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{6}\left[\tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{2} \times \frac{2}{3}-\tan ^{-1} 0\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{6}\left[\tan ^{-1} 1-\tan ^{-1} 0\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{6} \times\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-0\right)=\frac{\pi}{ 24}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{0}^{\frac{2}{3}} \frac{d x}{4+9 x^{2}}=\frac{\pi}{24}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} using substitution.
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} Let x 2 + 1 = t ⇒ 2xdx = dt ⇒ xdx = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} dt When x = 0, t = 1 and when x = 1, t = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x=\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d t}{2 t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int_{1}^{2} \frac{d t}{t}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\log |t| ]_{1}^{2}\right.{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\log 2-\log 1]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} 
Let x2 + 1 = t
⇒ 2xdx = dt
⇒ xdx = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} dt
When x = 0, t = 1 and when x = 1, t = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{1} \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x=\int_{1}^{2} \frac{d t}{2 t}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int_{1}^{2} \frac{d t}{t}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\log |t| ]_{1}^{2}\right.{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\log 2-\log 1]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \log 2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi} \cos ^{5} \phi~ d \phi{/tex} using substitution.
solution_text: Given: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi} \cos ^{5} \phi d \phi{/tex} Let {tex}\mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi} \cos ^{5} \phi \mathrm{d} \phi=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi} \cos ^{4} \phi \cos \phi \mathrm{d} \phi{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi}\left(\cos ^{2} \phi\right)^{2} \cos \phi d \phi{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi}\left(1-\sin ^{2} \phi\right)^{2} \cos \phi d \phi{/tex} Also, let {tex}\sin \phi=t \Rightarrow \cos \phi d \phi=d t{/tex} when {tex}\phi=0, \mathrm{t}=0{/tex} and when {tex}\phi=\frac{\pi}{2}, \mathrm{t}=1{/tex} So, {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{t}\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2} d t{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} t^{\frac{1}{2}}\left(1+t^{4}-2 t^{2}\right) d t{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{1}\left(t^{\frac{1}{2}}+t^{\frac{9}{2}}-2 t^{\frac{5}{2}}\right) d t{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}+\frac{t^{\frac{11}{2}}}{\frac{11}{2}}-\frac{2 t^{\frac{7}{2}}}{\frac{7}{2}}\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{11}-\frac{4}{7}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{154+42-132}{231}=\frac{64}{231}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi} \cos ^{5} \phi d \phi{/tex} 
Let {tex}\mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi} \cos ^{5} \phi \mathrm{d} \phi=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi} \cos ^{4} \phi \cos \phi \mathrm{d} \phi{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi}\left(\cos ^{2} \phi\right)^{2} \cos \phi d \phi{/tex} 
{tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin \phi}\left(1-\sin ^{2} \phi\right)^{2} \cos \phi d \phi{/tex} 
Also, let {tex}\sin \phi=t \Rightarrow \cos \phi d \phi=d t{/tex} 
when {tex}\phi=0, \mathrm{t}=0{/tex} and when {tex}\phi=\frac{\pi}{2}, \mathrm{t}=1{/tex} 
So, {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{t}\left(1-t^{2}\right)^{2} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{1} t^{\frac{1}{2}}\left(1+t^{4}-2 t^{2}\right) d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{1}\left(t^{\frac{1}{2}}+t^{\frac{9}{2}}-2 t^{\frac{5}{2}}\right) d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{3}{2}}}{\frac{3}{2}}+\frac{t^{\frac{11}{2}}}{\frac{11}{2}}-\frac{2 t^{\frac{7}{2}}}{\frac{7}{2}}\right]_{0}^{1}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{11}-\frac{4}{7}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{154+42-132}{231}=\frac{64}{231}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^{2}}\right) d x{/tex} using substitution.
solution_text: Given: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1}\right) d x{/tex} Let x = tan {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = sec 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} d {tex}\theta{/tex} When, x = 0, {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0 and when x = 1, {tex}\theta = \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \tan \theta}{\tan ^{2} \theta+1}\right) \sec ^{2} \theta \mathrm{d} \theta{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sin ^{-1}(\sin 2 \theta) \sec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} 2 \theta \sec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \theta \sec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} By applying product rule as, {tex}\int \mathrm{u} \cdot \mathrm{v} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{u} \cdot \int \mathrm{vdx}-\int \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}} \cdot\left\{\int \mathrm{vdx}\right\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2\left[\theta \int \sec ^{2} \theta \mathrm{d} \theta-\int \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} \theta} \theta \cdot\left\{\int \sec ^{2} \theta \mathrm{d} \theta\right\} \mathrm{d} \theta\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\theta \tan \theta-\int 1 . \tan \theta d \theta\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}2[\theta \tan \theta-\log |\sec \theta|]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{\pi}{4} \tan \frac{\pi}{4}-\log \left|\sec \frac{\pi}{4}\right|-0+\log |\sec \theta|\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \log (2)\right.]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}-\log (2){/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1}\right) d x{/tex} 
Let x = tan {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = sec2 {tex}\theta{/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex} 
When, x = 0, {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0 and when x = 1, {tex}\theta = \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1}\right) d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \tan \theta}{\tan ^{2} \theta+1}\right) \sec ^{2} \theta \mathrm{d} \theta{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \sin ^{-1}(\sin 2 \theta) \sec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} 2 \theta \sec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} 
= {tex}2 \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \theta \sec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} 
By applying product rule as,
{tex}\int \mathrm{u} \cdot \mathrm{v} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{u} \cdot \int \mathrm{vdx}-\int \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}} \cdot\left\{\int \mathrm{vdx}\right\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2\left[\theta \int \sec ^{2} \theta \mathrm{d} \theta-\int \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} \theta} \theta \cdot\left\{\int \sec ^{2} \theta \mathrm{d} \theta\right\} \mathrm{d} \theta\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[\theta \tan \theta-\int 1 . \tan \theta d \theta\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} 
= {tex}2[\theta \tan \theta-\log |\sec \theta|]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[\frac{\pi}{4} \tan \frac{\pi}{4}-\log \left|\sec \frac{\pi}{4}\right|-0+\log |\sec \theta|\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \log (2)\right.]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}-\log (2){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{2} x \sqrt{x+2}{/tex} (Put x + 2 =t 2 ) using substitution.
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{2} x \sqrt{x+2} d x{/tex} Let x + 2 = t 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = 2t dt And x = t 2 - 2 when, x = 0, t = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} and when x = 2, t = 2 So, {tex}\int_{0}^{2} x \sqrt{x+2} d x=\int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{2}-2\right) \sqrt{t^{2}} 2 t d t{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{2}-2\right) t \cdot t d t{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{2}-2\right) t^{2} d t{/tex} = {tex}2 \int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{4}-2 t^{2}\right) d t{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{t^{5}}{5}-\frac{2 t^{3}}{3}\right]_{\sqrt{2}}^2{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{(2)^{5}}{5}-\frac{2(2)^{3}}{3}-\frac{(\sqrt{2})^{5}}{5}+\frac{2(\sqrt{2})^{3}}{3}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{32}{5}-\frac{16}{3}-\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{5}+\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{96-80-12 \sqrt{2}+20 \sqrt{2}}{15}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{16+8 \sqrt{2}}{15}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{16(2+\sqrt{2})}{15}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{16 \sqrt{2}(\sqrt{2}+1)}{15}{/tex} . Which is the required solution.
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{2} x \sqrt{x+2} d x{/tex} 
Let x + 2 = t2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = 2t dt
And x = t2 - 2
when, x = 0, t = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} and when x = 2, t = 2
So, {tex}\int_{0}^{2} x \sqrt{x+2} d x=\int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{2}-2\right) \sqrt{t^{2}} 2 t d t{/tex} 
= {tex}2 \int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{2}-2\right) t \cdot t d t{/tex} 
= {tex}2 \int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{2}-2\right) t^{2} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}2 \int_{\sqrt{2}}^{2}\left(t^{4}-2 t^{2}\right) d t{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[\frac{t^{5}}{5}-\frac{2 t^{3}}{3}\right]_{\sqrt{2}}^2{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[\frac{(2)^{5}}{5}-\frac{2(2)^{3}}{3}-\frac{(\sqrt{2})^{5}}{5}+\frac{2(\sqrt{2})^{3}}{3}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[\frac{32}{5}-\frac{16}{3}-\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{5}+\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}2\left[\frac{96-80-12 \sqrt{2}+20 \sqrt{2}}{15}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2\left[\frac{16+8 \sqrt{2}}{15}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\frac{16(2+\sqrt{2})}{15}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{16 \sqrt{2}(\sqrt{2}+1)}{15}{/tex} .

Which is the required solution.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin x}{1+\cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} using substitution.
solution_text: Given: I = {tex}\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin x}{1+\cos ^2 x} d x{/tex} ...(i) put cos x = t Differentiating w.r.t x {tex}-\sin x=\frac{d t}{d x}{/tex} {tex}d x=\frac{-d t}{\sin x}{/tex} Hence x varies from 0 to {tex}\frac {\pi}{2}{/tex} , t varies from 1 to 0 equation (i) {tex}\Rightarrow \int_1^0 \frac{\sin x}{1+t^2}\left(\frac{-d t}{\sin x}\right){/tex} {tex}\int_1^0 \frac{d t}{1+t^2}{/tex} = -[tan - 1(0) − tan -1 (1)] {tex}=-\left[0-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\left[-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: I = {tex}\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin x}{1+\cos ^2 x} d x{/tex} ...(i)
put cos x = t
Differentiating w.r.t x
{tex}-\sin x=\frac{d t}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}d x=\frac{-d t}{\sin x}{/tex}
Hence x varies from 0 to {tex}\frac {\pi}{2}{/tex}, t varies from 1 to 0
equation (i) {tex}\Rightarrow \int_1^0 \frac{\sin x}{1+t^2}\left(\frac{-d t}{\sin x}\right){/tex}
{tex}\int_1^0 \frac{d t}{1+t^2}{/tex}
= -[tan - 1(0) − tan-1(1)]
{tex}=-\left[0-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=-\left[-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{x+4-x^{2}}{/tex} using substitution.
solution_text: Given: {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{x+4-x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{x+4-x^{2}}=\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{-\left(x^{2}-x-4\right)}{/tex} We can write it as, {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{-\left(x^{2}-x+\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{4}-4\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{-\left[\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}-\frac{17}{4}\right]}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}\right]}{/tex} Let {tex}x-\frac{1}{2}=t \Rightarrow d x=d t{/tex} When {tex}x=0, t=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and when {tex}\mathrm{x}=2, \mathrm{t}=\frac{3}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}\right]}=\int_{-\frac{1}{2}}^{\frac{3}{2}} \frac{d t}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-(t)^{2}\right]}{/tex} because, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\left[(a)^{2}-(x)^{2}\right]}=\frac{1}{2 a} \log \left|\frac{a+x}{a-x}\right|+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{-\frac{1}{2}}^{\frac{3}{2}} \frac{d t}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-(t)^{2}\right]}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{1}{2\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)} \log \frac{\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}+t\right)}{\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}-t}\right]_{-\frac{1}{2}}^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \frac{\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}+\frac{3}{2}\right)}{\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}-\frac{3}{2}}-\log \frac{\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\right)}{\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}+\frac{1}{2}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \frac{(\sqrt{17}+3)}{\sqrt{17}-3}-\log \frac{(\sqrt{17}-1)}{\sqrt{17}+1}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{17}+3)}{\sqrt{17}-3} \times \frac{(\sqrt{17}+1)}{\sqrt{17}-1}\right\}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{17}+3)(\sqrt{17}+1)}{(\sqrt{17}-3)(\sqrt{17}-1)}\right\}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{17+3+4 \sqrt{17}}{17+3-4 \sqrt{17}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{20+4 \sqrt{17}}{20-4 \sqrt{17}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{5+\sqrt{17}}{5-\sqrt{17}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(5+\sqrt{17})(5+\sqrt{17})}{(5-\sqrt{17})(5+\sqrt{17})}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(25+17+10 \sqrt{17})}{25-17}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(42+10 \sqrt{17})}{8}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(21+5 \sqrt{17})}{4}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{x+4-x^{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{x+4-x^{2}}=\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{-\left(x^{2}-x-4\right)}{/tex} 
We can write it as, {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{-\left(x^{2}-x+\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{4}-4\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{-\left[\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}-\frac{17}{4}\right]}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}\right]}{/tex} 
Let {tex}x-\frac{1}{2}=t \Rightarrow d x=d t{/tex} 
When {tex}x=0, t=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and when {tex}\mathrm{x}=2, \mathrm{t}=\frac{3}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{2} \frac{d x}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}\right]}=\int_{-\frac{1}{2}}^{\frac{3}{2}} \frac{d t}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-(t)^{2}\right]}{/tex} 
because, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\left[(a)^{2}-(x)^{2}\right]}=\frac{1}{2 a} \log \left|\frac{a+x}{a-x}\right|+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{-\frac{1}{2}}^{\frac{3}{2}} \frac{d t}{\left[\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)^{2}-(t)^{2}\right]}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{1}{2\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}\right)} \log \frac{\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}+t\right)}{\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}-t}\right]_{-\frac{1}{2}}^{\frac{3}{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \frac{\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}+\frac{3}{2}\right)}{\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}-\frac{3}{2}}-\log \frac{\left(\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\right)}{\frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}+\frac{1}{2}}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \frac{(\sqrt{17}+3)}{\sqrt{17}-3}-\log \frac{(\sqrt{17}-1)}{\sqrt{17}+1}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{17}+3)}{\sqrt{17}-3} \times \frac{(\sqrt{17}+1)}{\sqrt{17}-1}\right\}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}}\left[\log \left\{\frac{(\sqrt{17}+3)(\sqrt{17}+1)}{(\sqrt{17}-3)(\sqrt{17}-1)}\right\}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{17+3+4 \sqrt{17}}{17+3-4 \sqrt{17}}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{20+4 \sqrt{17}}{20-4 \sqrt{17}}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{5+\sqrt{17}}{5-\sqrt{17}}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(5+\sqrt{17})(5+\sqrt{17})}{(5-\sqrt{17})(5+\sqrt{17})}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(25+17+10 \sqrt{17})}{25-17}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(42+10 \sqrt{17})}{8}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}} \log \left[\frac{(21+5 \sqrt{17})}{4}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{x^{2}+2 x+5}{/tex} using substitution.
solution_text: Given: {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{x^{2}+2 x+5}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+2 x+1\right)+4}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{(x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}}{/tex} Let x + 1 = t ⇒ dx = dt When x = -1, t = 0 and when x = 1, t = 2 {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{(x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}}=\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d t}{(t)^{2}+(2)^{2}}{/tex} because, {tex}\int \frac{d t}{x^{2}+a^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \cdot \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{2} \frac{d t}{(t)^{2}+(2)^{2}}=\left[\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}\right]_{0}^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} 1-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} 0{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{8}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{x^{2}+2 x+5}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+2 x+1\right)+4}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{(x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}}{/tex} 
Let x + 1 = t
⇒ dx = dt
When x = -1, t = 0 and when x = 1, t = 2
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d x}{(x+1)^{2}+(2)^{2}}=\int_{0}^{2} \frac{d t}{(t)^{2}+(2)^{2}}{/tex} 
because, {tex}\int \frac{d t}{x^{2}+a^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \cdot \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{a}+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int_{0}^{2} \frac{d t}{(t)^{2}+(2)^{2}}=\left[\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}\right]_{0}^{2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} 1-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} 0{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{8}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_1^2 {\left( {\frac{1}{x} - \frac{1}{{2{x^2}}}} \right){e^{2x}}dx} {/tex} …(i) Putting 2x = t {tex} \Rightarrow 2 = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2dx = dt{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow dx = \frac{{dt}}{2}{/tex} Limits of integration when {tex}x = 1,t = 2{\kern 1pt} \times 1 = 2{/tex} and when {tex}x = 2,t = 2 \times 2 = 4{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int\limits_2^4 {\left( {\frac{1}{{\frac{t}{2}}} - \frac{1}{{2{{\left( {\frac{t}{2}} \right)}^2}}}} \right){e^t}\frac{{dt}}{2}} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\left( {\frac{2}{t} - \frac{2}{{{t^2}}}} \right){e^t}\frac{{dt}}{2}} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\frac{1}{2}.2\left( {\frac{1}{t} - \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}} \right){e^t}{{dt}}{}} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\left( {\frac{1}{t} - \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}} \right){e^t}{{dt}}{}} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\left\{ {f\left( t \right) + f'\left( t \right)} \right\}{e^t}{{dt}}{}} {/tex} {tex}= \left\{ {{e^t}f\left( t \right)} \right\}_2^4{/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{{e^t}}}{t}} \right)_2^4{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{e^4}}}{4} - \frac{{{e^2}}}{2}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{e^4} - 2{e^2}}}{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_1^2 {\left( {\frac{1}{x} - \frac{1}{{2{x^2}}}} \right){e^{2x}}dx} {/tex}…(i)
Putting 2x = t
{tex} \Rightarrow 2 = \frac{{dt}}{{dx}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2dx = dt{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow dx = \frac{{dt}}{2}{/tex}
Limits of integration when {tex}x = 1,t = 2{\kern 1pt} \times 1 = 2{/tex} and when {tex}x = 2,t = 2 \times 2 = 4{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i),
{tex}I = \int\limits_2^4 {\left( {\frac{1}{{\frac{t}{2}}} - \frac{1}{{2{{\left( {\frac{t}{2}} \right)}^2}}}} \right){e^t}\frac{{dt}}{2}} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\left( {\frac{2}{t} - \frac{2}{{{t^2}}}} \right){e^t}\frac{{dt}}{2}} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\frac{1}{2}.2\left( {\frac{1}{t} - \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}} \right){e^t}{{dt}}{}} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\left( {\frac{1}{t} - \frac{1}{{{t^2}}}} \right){e^t}{{dt}}{}} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_2^4 {\left\{ {f\left( t \right) + f'\left( t \right)} \right\}{e^t}{{dt}}{}} {/tex}
{tex}= \left\{ {{e^t}f\left( t \right)} \right\}_2^4{/tex}
{tex}= \left( {\frac{{{e^t}}}{t}} \right)_2^4{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{{e^4}}}{4} - \frac{{{e^2}}}{2}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{{e^4} - 2{e^2}}}{4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: The value of the integral {tex}\int_{\frac{1}{3}}^{1} \frac{\left(x-x^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{x^{4}} d x{/tex} is
question_html: The value of the integral {tex}\int_{\frac{1}{3}}^{1} \frac{\left(x-x^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{x^{4}} d x{/tex} is

options.explanation: Given: {tex}\int_{\frac{1}{3}}^{1}\left(\frac{\left(x-x^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{x^{4}}\right) d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{\frac{1}{3}}^{1}\left(\frac{\left(x-x^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{x^{4}}\right) d x{/tex} Now, let x = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = cos {tex}\theta{/tex} d {tex}\theta{/tex} Now, when {tex}\mathrm{x}=\frac{1}{3}, \theta=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right){/tex} and when {tex}x=1, \theta=\frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{\left(\sin \theta-\sin ^{3} \theta\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{\sin ^{4} \theta}\right) \cos \theta \mathrm{d} \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left (\frac{1}{3} \right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{(\sin \theta)^{\frac{1}{3}} )\left(1-\sin ^{2} \theta\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{\sin ^{4} \theta}\right) \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{(\sin \theta)^{\frac{1}{3}}\left(\cos ^{2} \theta\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{\sin ^{4} \theta}\right) \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{(\sin \theta)^{\frac{1}{3}}(\cos \theta)^{\frac{2}{3}}}{\sin ^{2} \theta \cdot \sin ^{2} \theta}\right) \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{(\cos \theta)^{\frac{2}{3}+1}}{(\sin \theta)^{2-\frac{1}{3}}}\right) \cdot \frac{1}{\sin ^{2} \theta} d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\frac{(\cos \theta)^{\frac{5}{3}}}{(\sin \theta)^{\frac{5}{3}}}\right) \cdot cosec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left((\cot \theta)^{\frac{5}{3}}\right) \cdot cosec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int_{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left((\cot \theta)^{\frac{5}{3}}\right) \cdot \ cosec ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} put, {tex}cot\theta=t,~then~-cosec^2\theta d\theta=dt{/tex} When, {tex}\theta=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right){/tex} , {tex}t=2 \sqrt{2}{/tex} and when {tex}\theta=\frac{\pi}{2}, \mathrm{t}=0{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I {tex}=\int_{2 \sqrt{2}}^{0}-(t)^{\frac{5}{3}} \cdot d t{/tex} {tex}=-\left[\frac{(t)^{\frac{5}{3}+1}}{\frac{5}{3}+1}\right]_{2 \sqrt{2}}^{0}{/tex} {tex}=-\left[\frac{(t)^{\frac{8}{3}}}{\frac{8}{3}}\right]_{2 \sqrt{2}}^{0}{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{3}{8}\left[(0)^{\frac{8}{3}}-(2 \sqrt{2})^{\frac{8}{3}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{3}{8}\left[-(\sqrt{8})^{\frac{8}{3}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3}{8}\left[(8)^{\frac{4}{3}}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3}{8}[16]{/tex} = 6

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If {tex}f(x)=\int_{0}^{x} t \sin t d t{/tex} , then f'(x) is
question_html: If {tex}f(x)=\int_{0}^{x} t \sin t d t{/tex}, then f'(x) is

options.explanation: Given: {tex}f(x)=\int_{0}^{x} t \sin t d t{/tex} Applying product rule, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \mathrm{u} . \mathrm{v} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{u} \cdot \int \mathrm{vdx}-\int \frac{\mathrm{du}}{\mathrm{dx}} \cdot\left\{\int \mathrm{vdx}\right\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} So, {tex}f(x)=[t]_{0}^{x} \int_{0}^{x} \sin t d t-\int_{0}^{x}\left\{\left(\frac{d}{d t} t\right) \cdot \int \sin t d t\right\} d t{/tex} {tex}=[t(-\cos t)]_{0}^{x}-\int_{0}^{x}(-\cos t) d t{/tex} {tex}=[-t(\cos t)+\sin t]_{0}^{x}{/tex} = -x cos x + sin x - 0 ⇒ f(x) = -x cos x + sinx {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})=-\left[\mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \cos \mathrm{x}+\cos \mathrm{x} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \mathrm{x}+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}} \sin \mathrm{x}\right]{/tex} ⇒ f'(x) = -[{x(-sinx )} + cosx ] + cosx = x sin x - cos x + cos x = x sin x

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex} …(i) {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx = } } } \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx} {/tex} …(ii) Adding equation (i) and (ii), {tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {{{\cos }^2}x + {{\sin }^2}x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \left( x \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex}…(i)
{tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx = } } } \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx} {/tex} …(ii)
Adding equation (i) and (ii),
{tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {{{\cos }^2}x + {{\sin }^2}x} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \left( x \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}} d x{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}} d x{/tex}
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}} d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}} d x{/tex} .....(i) as {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}}{\sqrt{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}+\sqrt{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}}{\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}+\sqrt{\sin \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} ......(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin \mathrm{x}}+\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}}{\sqrt{\sin \mathrm{x}}+\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}[1] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}-0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}} d x{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}+\sqrt{\cos x}} d x{/tex} .....(i)
as {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}}{\sqrt{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}+\sqrt{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}}{\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}+\sqrt{\sin \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} ......(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sqrt{\sin \mathrm{x}}+\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}}{\sqrt{\sin \mathrm{x}}+\sqrt{\cos \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}[1] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}-0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x d x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x d x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{/tex}
solution_text: Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x} d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x} d x{/tex} .....(i) as {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right.{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} ......(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}[1] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=[\mathrm{x}]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}-0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x} d x{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x} d x{/tex} .....(i)
as {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right.{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} ......(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x}{\sin ^{\frac{3}{2}} x+\cos ^{\frac{3}{2}} x} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}[1] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=[\mathrm{x}]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}-0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} dx{/tex} …(i) {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) + {{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^{{a }{}} {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)} dx } } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\sin }^5x}}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}dx} {/tex} …(ii) Adding equations (i) and (ii), {tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}} + \frac{{{{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}xdx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} dx{/tex}…(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{{{\sin }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) + {{\cos }^5}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}dx} {/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^{{a }{}} {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)} dx } } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{{{\sin }^5x}}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}dx} {/tex} …(ii)
Adding equations (i) and (ii),
{tex}2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}} + \frac{{{{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {\frac{{{{\cos }^5}x + {{\sin }^5}x}}{{{{\sin }^5}x + {{\cos }^5}x}}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}  
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_{ - 5}^5 {\left| {x + 2} \right|dx} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_{ - 5}^5 {\left| {x + 2} \right|dx} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_{ - 5}^5 {\left| {x + 2} \right|dx} {/tex} …(i) Putting x + 2 = 0 {tex} \Rightarrow x = - 2 \in \left( { - 5,5} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int\limits_{ - 5}^{ - 2} {\left| {x + 2} \right|} dx + \int\limits_{ - 2}^5 {\left| {x + 2} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_{ - 5}^{ - 2} { - \left( {x + 2} \right)dx + \int\limits_{ - 2}^5 {\left( {x + 2} \right)dx} } {/tex} {tex}= - \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right)_{ - 5}^{ - 2} + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right)_{ - 2}^5{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {\left( {\frac{4}{2} - 4} \right) - \left( {\frac{{25}}{2} - 10} \right)} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\left( {\frac{{25}}{2} + 10} \right) - \left( {\frac{4}{2} - 4} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left( { - 2 - \frac{5}{2}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{45}}{2} + 2} \right){/tex} {tex}= 2 + \frac{5}{2} + \frac{{45}}{2} + 2{/tex} {tex} = 4 + 25 = 29{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_{ - 5}^5 {\left| {x + 2} \right|dx} {/tex} …(i)

Putting x + 2 = 0

{tex} \Rightarrow x = - 2 \in \left( { - 5,5} \right){/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i),

{tex}I = \int\limits_{ - 5}^{ - 2} {\left| {x + 2} \right|} dx + \int\limits_{ - 2}^5 {\left| {x + 2} \right|dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int\limits_{ - 5}^{ - 2} { - \left( {x + 2} \right)dx + \int\limits_{ - 2}^5 {\left( {x + 2} \right)dx} } {/tex}

{tex}= - \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right)_{ - 5}^{ - 2} + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right)_{ - 2}^5{/tex}

{tex}= - \left[ {\left( {\frac{4}{2} - 4} \right) - \left( {\frac{{25}}{2} - 10} \right)} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\left( {\frac{{25}}{2} + 10} \right) - \left( {\frac{4}{2} - 4} \right)} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= - \left( { - 2 - \frac{5}{2}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{45}}{2} + 2} \right){/tex}

{tex}= 2 + \frac{5}{2} + \frac{{45}}{2} + 2{/tex}

{tex} = 4 + 25 = 29{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_2^8 {\left| {x - 5} \right|dx} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_2^8 {\left| {x - 5} \right|dx} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_2^8 {\left| {x - 5} \right|dx} {/tex} …(i) Putting x - 5 = 0 {tex} \Rightarrow x = 5 \in \left( {2,8} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int\limits_2^5 {\left| {x - 5} \right|dx + \int\limits_5^8 {\left| {x + 5} \right|dx} } {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_2^5 { - \left( {x - 5} \right)dx + \int\limits_5^8 {\left( {x - 5} \right)dx} } {/tex} {tex}= - \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 5x} \right)_2^5 + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 5x} \right)_{^5}^8{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {\left( {\frac{{25}}{2} - 25} \right) - \left( {2 - 10} \right)} \right] + \left[ {\left( {32 - 40} \right) - \left( {\frac{{25}}{2} - 25} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left( { - \frac{{25}}{2} + 8} \right) + \left( { - 8 + \frac{{25}}{2}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{{25}}{2} - 8 - 8 + \frac{{25}}{2}{/tex} = 25 - 16 = 9
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_2^8 {\left| {x - 5} \right|dx} {/tex}…(i)
Putting x - 5 = 0
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 5 \in \left( {2,8} \right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i),
{tex}I = \int\limits_2^5 {\left| {x - 5} \right|dx + \int\limits_5^8 {\left| {x + 5} \right|dx} } {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_2^5 { - \left( {x - 5} \right)dx + \int\limits_5^8 {\left( {x - 5} \right)dx} } {/tex}
{tex}= - \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 5x} \right)_2^5 + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 5x} \right)_{^5}^8{/tex}
{tex}= - \left[ {\left( {\frac{{25}}{2} - 25} \right) - \left( {2 - 10} \right)} \right] + \left[ {\left( {32 - 40} \right) - \left( {\frac{{25}}{2} - 25} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= - \left( { - \frac{{25}}{2} + 8} \right) + \left( { - 8 + \frac{{25}}{2}} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{25}}{2} - 8 - 8 + \frac{{25}}{2}{/tex}
= 25 - 16 
= 9

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {x{{\left( {1 - x} \right)}^n}dx} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {x{{\left( {1 - x} \right)}^n}dx} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^1 {x{{\left( {1 - x} \right)}^n}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {1 - x} \right)\left\{ {1 - {{\left( {1 - x} \right)}}} \right\}^ndx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx } } } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {1 - x} \right){{\left( {1 - 1 + x} \right)}^n}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {1 - x} \right){x^n}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {{x^n} - {x^{n + 1}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \left( {\frac{{{x^{n + 1}}}}{{n + 1}} - \frac{{{x^{n + 2}}}}{{n + 2}}} \right)_0^1{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{n + 1}} - \frac{1}{{n + 2}} - \left( {0 - 0} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{{n + 2 - n - 1}}{{\left( {n + 1} \right)\left( {n + 2} \right)}} = \frac{1}{{\left( {n + 1} \right)\left( {n + 2} \right)}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^1 {x{{\left( {1 - x} \right)}^n}dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {1 - x} \right)\left\{ {1 - {{\left( {1 - x} \right)}}} \right\}^ndx} {/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx } } } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {1 - x} \right){{\left( {1 - 1 + x} \right)}^n}dx} {/tex}
{tex} = \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {1 - x} \right){x^n}dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {{x^n} - {x^{n + 1}}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \left( {\frac{{{x^{n + 1}}}}{{n + 1}} - \frac{{{x^{n + 2}}}}{{n + 2}}} \right)_0^1{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{1}{{n + 1}} - \frac{1}{{n + 2}} - \left( {0 - 0} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{n + 2 - n - 1}}{{\left( {n + 1} \right)\left( {n + 2} \right)}} = \frac{1}{{\left( {n + 1} \right)\left( {n + 2} \right)}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex}
solution_text: Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} .....(i) as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\mathrm{x}\right)\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} as {tex}\left\{\tan (A-B)=\frac{\tan (A)-\tan (B)}{1+\tan (A) \tan (B)}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{1-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[\frac{2}{1+\tan (x)}\right] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [1+\tan (\mathrm{x})] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{I}{/tex} (from (i)) {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=[\mathrm{x} \log 2]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4} \log 2-0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{8} \log 2{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} .....(i)
as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\mathrm{x}\right)\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
as {tex}\left\{\tan (A-B)=\frac{\tan (A)-\tan (B)}{1+\tan (A) \tan (B)}\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{1-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[\frac{2}{1+\tan (x)}\right] d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [1+\tan (\mathrm{x})] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{I}{/tex} (from (i))
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=[\mathrm{x} \log 2]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4} \log 2-0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{8} \log 2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^2 {x\sqrt {2 - x} dx} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^2 {x\sqrt {2 - x} dx} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^2 {x\sqrt {2 - x} dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^2 {\left( {2 - x} \right)\sqrt {2 - \left( {2 - x} \right)} dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx } } } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^2 {\left( {2 - x} \right)\sqrt x dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^2 {\left( {2{x^{\frac{1}{2}}} - {x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {2.\frac{{{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{3}{2}}} - \frac{{{x^{\frac{5}{2}}}}}{{\frac{5}{2}}}} \right]_0^2{/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{4}{3}{{.2}^{\frac{3}{2}}} - \frac{2}{5}{{.2}^{\frac{5}{2}}}} \right) - \left( {0 - 0} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{4}{3} \times 2\sqrt 2 - \frac{2}{5} \times 4\sqrt 2 {/tex} {tex} = \left( {\frac{8}{3} - \frac{8}{5}} \right)\sqrt 2 {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{16\sqrt 2 }}{{15}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^2 {x\sqrt {2 - x} dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int\limits_0^2 {\left( {2 - x} \right)\sqrt {2 - \left( {2 - x} \right)} dx} {/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx } } } \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^2 {\left( {2 - x} \right)\sqrt x dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \int\limits_0^2 {\left( {2{x^{\frac{1}{2}}} - {x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right)dx} {/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {2.\frac{{{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{3}{2}}} - \frac{{{x^{\frac{5}{2}}}}}{{\frac{5}{2}}}} \right]_0^2{/tex}

{tex}= \left( {\frac{4}{3}{{.2}^{\frac{3}{2}}} - \frac{2}{5}{{.2}^{\frac{5}{2}}}} \right) - \left( {0 - 0} \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{4}{3} \times 2\sqrt 2 - \frac{2}{5} \times 4\sqrt 2 {/tex}

{tex} = \left( {\frac{8}{3} - \frac{8}{5}} \right)\sqrt 2 {/tex}

{tex}= \frac{{16\sqrt 2 }}{{15}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integral, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(2 \log \sin x-\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integral, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(2 \log \sin x-\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex}
solution_text: According to the question , {tex} I=\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } ( 2 \log | \sin x | - \log | \sin 2 x | ) d x{/tex} From {tex}0 \ to \ {\pi\over2} , sinx \ and \ cosx{/tex} are positive. so, {tex}|sinx| = sinx , |cosx| = cosx{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } [ 2 \log ( \sin x ) - \log ( 2 \sin x \cos x ) ] d x{/tex} {tex}[\because sin2x = 2sinx cosx]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } [ 2 \log ( \sin x ) - ( \log 2 + \log ( \sin x ){/tex} {tex} + \log ( \cos x ) ) ] d x{/tex} {tex}[\because log(ABC)= logA+logB+logC]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } [ 2 \log ( \sin x ) - \log 2 - \log ( \sin x ){/tex} {tex} - \log ( \cos x ) ) ] d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } ( \log ( \sin x ) - \log 2 - \log ( \cos x ) ) d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log ( \sin x ) d x - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log 2 d x{/tex} {tex} - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log ( \cos x ) d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \sin \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x \right) d x - \log 2 [ x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex} {tex} - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \cos x d x{/tex} {tex}[\because \int_b^axdx= \int_b^a(a+b-x)dx]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \cos x d x - \log 2 \left[ \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 \right]{/tex} {tex} - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \cos x d x{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = - \frac { \pi } { 2 } \log 2{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question , {tex} I=\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } ( 2 \log | \sin x | - \log | \sin 2 x | ) d x{/tex}
From {tex}0 \ to \ {\pi\over2} , sinx \ and \ cosx{/tex} are positive. so, {tex}|sinx| = sinx , |cosx| = cosx{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } [ 2 \log ( \sin x ) - \log ( 2 \sin x \cos x ) ] d x{/tex}{tex}[\because sin2x = 2sinx cosx]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } [ 2 \log ( \sin x ) - ( \log 2 + \log ( \sin x ){/tex}{tex} + \log ( \cos x ) ) ] d x{/tex}{tex}[\because log(ABC)= logA+logB+logC]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } [ 2 \log ( \sin x ) - \log 2 - \log ( \sin x ){/tex}{tex} - \log ( \cos x ) ) ] d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } ( \log ( \sin x ) - \log 2 - \log ( \cos x ) ) d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log ( \sin x ) d x - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log 2 d x{/tex}{tex} - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log ( \cos x ) d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \sin \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x \right) d x - \log 2 [ x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex}{tex} - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \cos x d x{/tex} {tex}[\because \int_b^axdx= \int_b^a(a+b-x)dx]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \cos x d x - \log 2 \left[ \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 \right]{/tex}{tex} - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \log \cos x d x{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = - \frac { \pi } { 2 } \log 2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx}{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx}{/tex}
