solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx} {/tex} {tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx} {/tex} ...(i) {tex}{\because \int\limits_{ - a}^a {f\left( x \right)dx = 2\int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx,} } }{/tex} when f(x) is even function] {tex}\Rightarrow I = 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx = } } } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex} …(ii) Adding eq. (i) and (ii), {tex}2I = 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {{{\sin }^2}x + {{\cos }^2}x} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex} {tex}= 2\left( x \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}}{/tex} {tex} = 2.\frac{\pi }{2} = \pi {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx} {/tex}
{tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}xdx} {/tex} ...(i)

{tex}{\because \int\limits_{ - a}^a {f\left( x \right)dx = 2\int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx,} } }{/tex} when f(x) is even function]
{tex}\Rightarrow I = 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^2}\left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx = } } } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\cos }^2}xdx} {/tex} …(ii)
Adding eq. (i) and (ii),
{tex}2I = 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left( {{{\sin }^2}x + {{\cos }^2}x} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {1dx} {/tex}
{tex}= 2\left( x \right)_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}}{/tex}
{tex} = 2.\frac{\pi }{2} = \pi {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integral, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x d x}{1+\sin x}{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integral, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x d x}{1+\sin x}{/tex}
solution_text: Given, {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{1+\sin \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} Let, {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x}{1+\sin x} d x{/tex} .....(i) as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-\mathrm{x})}{1+\sin (\pi-\mathrm{x})} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x)}{1+\sin x} d x{/tex} .....(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x}}{1+\sin \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi}{1+\sin \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(1-\sin \mathrm{x})}{(1+\sin \mathrm{x})(1-\sin \mathrm{x})} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(1-\sin \mathrm{x})}{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi}\left\{\frac{1}{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}}-\frac{\sin \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}}\right\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi}\left\{\sec ^{2} x-\tan x \sec x\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\pi[\tan \mathrm{x}-\sec \mathrm{x}]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\pi[2]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\pi{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{1+\sin \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
Let, {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x}{1+\sin x} d x{/tex} .....(i)
as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-\mathrm{x})}{1+\sin (\pi-\mathrm{x})} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x)}{1+\sin x} d x{/tex} .....(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{x}}{1+\sin \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi}{1+\sin \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(1-\sin \mathrm{x})}{(1+\sin \mathrm{x})(1-\sin \mathrm{x})} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(1-\sin \mathrm{x})}{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi}\left\{\frac{1}{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}}-\frac{\sin \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}}\right\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi}\left\{\sec ^{2} x-\tan x \sec x\right\} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\pi[\tan \mathrm{x}-\sec \mathrm{x}]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\pi[2]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^7}xdx} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^7}xdx} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^7}xdx} {/tex} Here f(x) = sin 7 x {tex}\therefore f\left( { - x} \right) = {\sin ^7}\left( { - x} \right){/tex} (-sin x) 7 = -sin 7 x = -f(x) {tex}\therefore {/tex} f(x) is an odd function of x. {tex}\therefore I = \int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^7}xdx} = 0{/tex} {tex}{\left[ {\because \int\limits_{ - a}^a {f\left( x \right)dx = 0} } \right.}{/tex} when f(x) is an odd function]
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^7}xdx} {/tex}

Here f(x) = sin7x

{tex}\therefore f\left( { - x} \right) = {\sin ^7}\left( { - x} \right){/tex}

(-sin x)7

= -sin7x = -f(x)

{tex}\therefore {/tex} f(x) is an odd function of x.

{tex}\therefore I = \int\limits_{\frac{{ - \pi }}{2}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {{{\sin }^7}xdx} = 0{/tex}

{tex}{\left[ {\because \int\limits_{ - a}^a {f\left( x \right)dx = 0} } \right.}{/tex} when f(x) is an odd function]

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{2\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{2\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}\int\limits_0^{2\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx{/tex} {tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^{2a} {f\left( x \right)dx = 2\int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx,if\,\,f\left( {2a - x} \right) = f\left( x \right)} } } \right]{/tex} Here {tex}f\left( x \right) = {\cos ^5}x{/tex} {tex}\therefore f\left( {\pi - x} \right) = {\cos ^5}\left( {\pi - x} \right) = -{\cos ^5}x{/tex} =-f(x) {tex} \Rightarrow\int\limits_0^{\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx=0{/tex} {tex} [\because \int _0^{2a}f(x)dx=0,if\ f(2a-x)=-f(x)]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int\limits_0^{2\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx=0{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\limits_0^{2\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx{/tex}

{tex}= 2\int\limits_0^{\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^{2a} {f\left( x \right)dx = 2\int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx,if\,\,f\left( {2a - x} \right) = f\left( x \right)} } } \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}f\left( x \right) = {\cos ^5}x{/tex}

{tex}\therefore f\left( {\pi - x} \right) = {\cos ^5}\left( {\pi - x} \right) = -{\cos ^5}x{/tex}=-f(x)

{tex} \Rightarrow\int\limits_0^{\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx=0{/tex}{tex} [\because \int _0^{2a}f(x)dx=0,if\ f(2a-x)=-f(x)]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \int\limits_0^{2\pi } {{{\cos }^5}x} dx=0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin x - \cos x}}{{1 + \sin x\cos x}}} dx{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin x - \cos x}}{{1 + \sin x\cos x}}} dx{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin x - \cos x}}{{1 + \sin x\cos x}}dx} {/tex} …(i) {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) - \cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{1 + \sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{1 + \cos x\sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}= - \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin x - \cos x}}{{1 + \cos x\sin x}}} dx{/tex} …(ii) Adding eq. (i) and (ii), we have {tex}2I = 0 \Rightarrow I = 0{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin x - \cos x}}{{1 + \sin x\cos x}}dx} {/tex} …(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right) - \cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}{{1 + \sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)}}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\cos x - \sin x}}{{1 + \cos x\sin x}}} dx{/tex}
{tex}= - \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\frac{{\sin x - \cos x}}{{1 + \cos x\sin x}}} dx{/tex}…(ii)
Adding eq. (i) and (ii), we have {tex}2I = 0 \Rightarrow I = 0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos x) d x{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos x) d x{/tex}
solution_text: Given, {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos x) d x{/tex} Let, {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos x) d x{/tex} .....(i) as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos (\pi-\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1-\cos x) d x{/tex} ......(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi}\{\log (1+\cos \mathrm{x})+\log (1-\cos \mathrm{x})\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log \left(1-\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log \left(\sin ^{2} \mathrm{x}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} 2 \cdot \log (\sin \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}2 \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\pi} \log (\sin \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(iii) because, {tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x) d x=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} if f(2a - x) = f(x) Here, if f(x) = log (sin x) and f( {tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = log ( sin ( {tex}\pi{/tex} - x))= log (sin x) = f(x) {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \sin \mathrm{xdx}{/tex} ......(iv) {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \cos \mathrm{x} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} ......(v) Adding (1) and (2), we get {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\log \sin \mathrm{x}+\log \cos \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\log \sin \mathrm{x}+\log \cos \mathrm{x}+\log 2-\log 2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\log (2 \sin \mathrm{x} \cos \mathrm{x})-\log 2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\log (\sin 2 \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log 2 \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}\right.{/tex} Let 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2dx = dt When x = 0, t = 0 and when x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} , t = {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\left[\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi}(\log (\sin t) d t]-\left(\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2\right)\right.{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[\frac{I}{2}\right]-\left(\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\left(\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-(\pi \log 2){/tex}
solution_html:
Given, {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos x) d x{/tex} 
Let, {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos x) d x{/tex} .....(i)
as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1+\cos (\pi-\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (1-\cos x) d x{/tex} ......(ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi}\{\log (1+\cos \mathrm{x})+\log (1-\cos \mathrm{x})\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log \left(1-\cos ^{2} \mathrm{x}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log \left(\sin ^{2} \mathrm{x}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\pi} 2 \cdot \log (\sin \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}2 \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\pi} \log (\sin \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \log (\sin x) d x{/tex} ......(iii)
because, {tex}\int_{0}^{2 a} f(x) d x=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} if f(2a - x) = f(x)
Here, if f(x) = log (sin x) and f({tex}\pi{/tex} - x) = log ( sin ({tex}\pi{/tex} - x))= log (sin x) = f(x)
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \sin \mathrm{xdx}{/tex} ......(iv)
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \cos \mathrm{x} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex}  ......(v)
Adding (1) and (2), we get
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\log \sin \mathrm{x}+\log \cos \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\log \sin \mathrm{x}+\log \cos \mathrm{x}+\log 2-\log 2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\log (2 \sin \mathrm{x} \cos \mathrm{x})-\log 2) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\log (\sin 2 \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log 2 \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}\right.{/tex}
Let 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2dx = dt
When x = 0, t = 0 and when x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}, t = {tex}\pi{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\left[\frac{1}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi}(\log (\sin t) d t]-\left(\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2\right)\right.{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\left[\frac{I}{2}\right]-\left(\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\left(\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-(\pi \log 2){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt x }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}} dx{/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt x }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}} dx{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt x }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}} dx{/tex} …(i) {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt {a - x} + \sqrt {a - \left( {a - x} \right)} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx = } } } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt {a - x} + \sqrt x }}} dx{/tex} …(ii) Adding eq. (i) and (ii), {tex}2I = \int\limits_0^a {\left( {\frac{{\sqrt x }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }} + \frac{{\sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt {a - x} + \sqrt x }}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^a {\left( {\frac{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}} \right)dx}{/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^a {1dx} = \left( x \right)_0^a = a{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{a}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt x }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}} dx{/tex}…(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt {a - x} + \sqrt {a - \left( {a - x} \right)} }}dx} {/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int\limits_0^a {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_0^a {f\left( {a - x} \right)dx = } } } \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^a {\frac{{\sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt {a - x} + \sqrt x }}} dx{/tex} …(ii)
Adding eq. (i) and (ii),

{tex}2I = \int\limits_0^a {\left( {\frac{{\sqrt x }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }} + \frac{{\sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt {a - x} + \sqrt x }}} \right)dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^a {\left( {\frac{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}{{\sqrt x + \sqrt {a - x} }}} \right)dx}{/tex}
{tex}= \int\limits_0^a {1dx} = \left( x \right)_0^a = a{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{a}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^4 {\left| {x - 1} \right|dx} {/tex}
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int\limits_0^4 {\left| {x - 1} \right|dx} {/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^4 {\left| {x - 1} \right|dx} {/tex} ...(i) Here x - 1 = 0 {tex} \Rightarrow x = 1 \in \left( {0,4} \right){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}I = \int\limits_0^1 {\left| {x - 1} \right|dx + \int\limits_1^4 {\left| {x - 1} \right|} dx} {/tex} {tex}= - \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {x - 1} \right)dx + \int\limits_1^4 {\left( {x - 1} \right)} dx} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = - \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - x} \right)_0^1 + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - x} \right)_1^4{/tex} {tex}= - \left\{ {\left( {\frac{1}{2} - 1} \right) - 0} \right\} + \left\{ {\frac{{16}}{2} - 4 - \left( {\frac{1}{2} - 1} \right)} \right\}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2} + 8 - 4 + \frac{1}{2}{/tex} = 9 - 4 = 5
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^4 {\left| {x - 1} \right|dx} {/tex} ...(i)
Here x - 1 = 0
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 1 \in \left( {0,4} \right){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i),
{tex}I = \int\limits_0^1 {\left| {x - 1} \right|dx + \int\limits_1^4 {\left| {x - 1} \right|} dx} {/tex}
{tex}= - \int\limits_0^1 {\left( {x - 1} \right)dx + \int\limits_1^4 {\left( {x - 1} \right)} dx} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = - \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - x} \right)_0^1 + \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - x} \right)_1^4{/tex}
{tex}= - \left\{ {\left( {\frac{1}{2} - 1} \right) - 0} \right\} + \left\{ {\frac{{16}}{2} - 4 - \left( {\frac{1}{2} - 1} \right)} \right\}{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{2} + 8 - 4 + \frac{1}{2}{/tex}
= 9 - 4
= 5

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Show that {tex}\int_0^a {f(x)g(x)dx = 2\int_0^a {f(x)dx} } {/tex} . If f and g are defined, {tex}f(x) = f(a - x){/tex} and {tex}g(x) + g(a - x) = 4{/tex}
question_html: Show that {tex}\int_0^a {f(x)g(x)dx = 2\int_0^a {f(x)dx} } {/tex}. If f and g are defined, {tex}f(x) = f(a - x){/tex} and {tex}g(x) + g(a - x) = 4{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}I = \int_0^a {f(x).g(x)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int_0^a {f(a - x).g(a - x)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \int_0^a {f(x).[4 - g(x)]dx} {/tex} [Using given condition] {tex} = \int_0^a {4f(x)dx - \int_0^a {f(x).g(x)dx} } {/tex} {tex}I = 4\int_0^a {f(x)dx - I} {/tex} {tex}I = 2\int_0^a {f(x)dx} {/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I = \int_0^a {f(x).g(x)dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int_0^a {f(a - x).g(a - x)dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \int_0^a {f(x).[4 - g(x)]dx} {/tex} [Using given condition]

{tex} = \int_0^a {4f(x)dx - \int_0^a {f(x).g(x)dx} } {/tex}

{tex}I = 4\int_0^a {f(x)dx - I} {/tex}

{tex}I = 2\int_0^a {f(x)dx} {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: The value of {tex}\int_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(x^{3}+x \cos x+\tan ^{5} x+1\right) d x{/tex} is
question_html: The value of {tex}\int_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(x^{3}+x \cos x+\tan ^{5} x+1\right) d x{/tex} is

options.explanation: Given: {tex}\int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(x^{3}+x \cos x+\tan ^{5} x+1\right) d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(x^{3}+x \cos x+\tan ^{5} x+1\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{3}\right) \mathrm{dx}+\int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\mathrm{x} \cos \mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}+\int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left(\tan ^{5} \mathrm{x}\right) \mathrm{dx}+\int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} It is also known that if f(x) is an even function then, {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=0+0+0+2 \cdot \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} ... {tex}\left\{\int_{-a}^{a} f(x) d x=0\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=2 \cdot[\mathrm{x}]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=2 \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: The value of {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\frac{4+3 \sin x}{4+3 \cos x}\right) d x{/tex} is
question_html: The value of {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\frac{4+3 \sin x}{4+3 \cos x}\right) d x{/tex} is

options.explanation: Given Integral is: {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\frac{4+3 \sin x}{4+3 \cos x}\right) d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\frac{4+3 \sin x}{4+3 \cos x}\right) d x{/tex} ......(i) As, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\frac{4+3 \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)}{4+3 \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\mathrm{x}\right)}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\frac{4+3 \cos \mathrm{x}}{4+3 \sin x}\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} ....(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}~~~~~2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}\left\{\log \left(\frac{4+3 \sin \mathrm{x}}{4+3 \cos \mathrm{x}}\right)+\left(\frac{4+3 \cos \mathrm{x}}{4+3 \sin }\right)\right\} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log \left(\frac{4+3 \sin \mathrm{x}}{4+3 \cos \mathrm{x}} \times \frac{4+3 \cos \mathrm{x}}{4+3 \sin x }\right) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \log 1 \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} 0 . \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Given {tex}\frac{1}{x-x^{3}}{/tex} Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{x-x^{3}}=\frac{1}{x\left(1-x^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} Using partial fraction: Let {tex}\frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A}{x}+\frac{B}{1+x}+\frac{C}{1-x}{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A(1+x)(1-x)+B(x)(1-x)+C(x)(1+x)}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A\left(1-x^{2}\right)+B x(1-x)+C x(1+x)}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A\left(1-x^{2}\right)+B x(1-x)+C x(1+x)}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} ⇒ 1 = A - Ax 2 + Bx - Bx 2 + Cx + Cx 2 ⇒ 1 = A + (B + C)x + (-A - B + C)x 2 Equating the coefficients of x, x 2 and constant value. We get: A = 1, B + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = -C -A - B + C = 0 ⇒ -1 - (- C) +C = 0 ⇒ 2C = 1 ⇒ C = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} So, B = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} Put these values in equation (i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{1}{x}+\frac{-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}{1+x}+\frac{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}{1-x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{x} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{1+x} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{1-x} d{/tex} = {tex}\log |\mathrm{x}|-\frac{1}{2} \log |1+\mathrm{x}|+\frac{1}{2} \log |1-\mathrm{x}|{/tex} = {tex}\log |x|-\log \left|(1+x)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right|+\log \left|(1-x)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{x}{(1+x)^{\frac{1}{2}}(1-x)^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right|+C{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{\left(x^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}}{(1+x)(1-x)^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right|+C{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{\left(x^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right|+C{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\left(\frac{x^{2}}{1-x^{2}}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right|+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2} \log \left|\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{1-\mathrm{x}^{2}}\right|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex}\frac{1}{x-x^{3}}{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{x-x^{3}}=\frac{1}{x\left(1-x^{2}\right)}=\frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} 
Using partial fraction:
Let {tex}\frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A}{x}+\frac{B}{1+x}+\frac{C}{1-x}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A(1+x)(1-x)+B(x)(1-x)+C(x)(1+x)}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A\left(1-x^{2}\right)+B x(1-x)+C x(1+x)}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{A\left(1-x^{2}\right)+B x(1-x)+C x(1+x)}{x(1+x)(1-x)}{/tex} 
⇒ 1 = A - Ax2 + Bx - Bx2 + Cx + Cx2
⇒ 1 = A + (B + C)x + (-A - B + C)x2
Equating the coefficients of x, x2 and constant value. We get:
 A = 1, 
B + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = -C
-A - B + C = 0
⇒ -1 - (- C) +C = 0
⇒ 2C = 1 ⇒ C = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
So, B = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}  
Put these values in equation (i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)}=\frac{1}{x}+\frac{-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}{1+x}+\frac{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}{1-x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x(1+x)(1-x)} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{x} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{1+x} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{1-x} d{/tex} 
= {tex}\log |\mathrm{x}|-\frac{1}{2} \log |1+\mathrm{x}|+\frac{1}{2} \log |1-\mathrm{x}|{/tex} 
= {tex}\log |x|-\log \left|(1+x)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right|+\log \left|(1-x)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right|{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\frac{x}{(1+x)^{\frac{1}{2}}(1-x)^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\frac{\left(x^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}}{(1+x)(1-x)^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\frac{\left(x^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}}\right|+C{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\left(\frac{x^{2}}{1-x^{2}}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}\right|+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2} \log \left|\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{1-\mathrm{x}^{2}}\right|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x+a}+\sqrt{x+b}} d x{/tex} . Then, {tex}I=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x+a}+\sqrt{x+b}} \times \frac{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}}{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}} \times d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}}{x+a-x-b} \times d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}}{a-b} \times d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{a-b}\left[\frac{2}{3}(x+a)^{\frac{3}{2}}-\frac{2}{3}(x+b)^{\frac{3}{2}}\right]+c{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2}{3(a-b)}\left[(x+a)^{\frac{3}{2}}-(x+b)^{\frac{3}{2}}\right]+c{/tex} {tex}\therefore \ I=\frac{2}{3(a-b)}\left[(x+a)^{\frac{3}{2}}-(x+b)^{\frac{3}{2}}\right]+c{/tex} . Which is the required solution.
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x+a}+\sqrt{x+b}} d x{/tex}. Then,
{tex}I=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x+a}+\sqrt{x+b}} \times \frac{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}}{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}} \times d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}}{x+a-x-b} \times d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{\sqrt{x+a}-\sqrt{x+b}}{a-b} \times d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{a-b}\left[\frac{2}{3}(x+a)^{\frac{3}{2}}-\frac{2}{3}(x+b)^{\frac{3}{2}}\right]+c{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2}{3(a-b)}\left[(x+a)^{\frac{3}{2}}-(x+b)^{\frac{3}{2}}\right]+c{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \ I=\frac{2}{3(a-b)}\left[(x+a)^{\frac{3}{2}}-(x+b)^{\frac{3}{2}}\right]+c{/tex}    .

Which is the required solution.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function {tex}\frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}}{/tex} [Hint: Put x = {tex}\frac{a}{t}{/tex} ]
question_html: Integrate the function {tex}\frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}}{/tex} [Hint: Put x = {tex}\frac{a}{t}{/tex}]
solution_text: Given: {tex}\frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}}{/tex} Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}}{/tex} Put {tex}x=\frac{a}{t} \Rightarrow d x=-\frac{a}{t^{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\frac{a}{t} \sqrt{\frac{a \cdot a}{t}-\left(\frac{a}{t}\right)^{2}}} \cdot-\frac{a}{t^{2}} d t{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{-1}{a t} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{\frac{1}{t}-\left(\frac{1}{t}\right)^{2}}} d t{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{a} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\frac{t^{2}}{t}-\left(\frac{t}{t}\right)^{2}}} d t{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{a} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t-1}} d t{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{a} \int(t-1)^{-\frac{1}{2}} d t{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{a}\left[\frac{\sqrt{(t-1)}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]+C{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{2}{a}[\sqrt{\left(\frac{a}{x}-1\right)}]+C{/tex} ; because {tex}t=\frac{a}{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{2}{\mathrm{a}}[\sqrt{\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}]+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}}{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}}{/tex} 
Put {tex}x=\frac{a}{t} \Rightarrow d x=-\frac{a}{t^{2}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x \sqrt{a x-x^{2}}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\frac{a}{t} \sqrt{\frac{a \cdot a}{t}-\left(\frac{a}{t}\right)^{2}}} \cdot-\frac{a}{t^{2}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}=\int \frac{-1}{a t} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{\frac{1}{t}-\left(\frac{1}{t}\right)^{2}}} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{a} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\frac{t^{2}}{t}-\left(\frac{t}{t}\right)^{2}}} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{a} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t-1}} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{a} \int(t-1)^{-\frac{1}{2}} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{a}\left[\frac{\sqrt{(t-1)}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]+C{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{2}{a}[\sqrt{\left(\frac{a}{x}-1\right)}]+C{/tex}  ; because {tex}t=\frac{a}{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=-\frac{2}{\mathrm{a}}[\sqrt{\left(\frac{\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}]+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{x^{2} \cdot\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex} Taking {tex}x^{4}{/tex} common from the denominator, we get {tex}I =\int \frac{1 d x}{x^{2}\left(x^{4}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}\left(x^{3}\right)\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{d x}{x^{5}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex} {tex}\text { Let } t=1+\frac{1}{x^{4}} \Rightarrow-\frac{d t}{4}=\frac{d x}{x^{5}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x^{2} \cdot\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}} \cdot d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{4}\left(\frac{t^{\frac{1}{4}}}{\frac{1}{4}}\right)+C{/tex} = {tex}-t^{\frac{1}{4}}+c{/tex} = {tex}-\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}+c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{x^{2} \cdot\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex} 
Taking {tex}x^{4}{/tex} common from the denominator, we get
{tex}I =\int \frac{1 d x}{x^{2}\left(x^{4}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}\left(x^{3}\right)\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{d x}{x^{5}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex}
{tex}\text { Let } t=1+\frac{1}{x^{4}} \Rightarrow-\frac{d t}{4}=\frac{d x}{x^{5}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x^{2} \cdot\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}} \cdot d x{/tex}  {tex}=\frac{-1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t^{\frac{3}{4}}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-1}{4}\left(\frac{t^{\frac{1}{4}}}{\frac{1}{4}}\right)+C{/tex} 
= {tex}-t^{\frac{1}{4}}+c{/tex}
={tex}-\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{4}}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function {tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} [Hint: {tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}=\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{3}}\left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)}{/tex} Put x = t 6 ]
solution_text: Given: {tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} or we can write it as {tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}=\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{3}}\left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)}{/tex} Let x = t 6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = 6t 5 dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{3}}\left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)} \cdot d x=\int \frac{6 t^{5}}{t^{2}(1+t)} \cdot d t{/tex} = {tex}\text { 6. } \int \frac{t^{3}}{(1+t)} \cdot d t{/tex} After division we get, {tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left[\left(t^{2}-t+1\right)-\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right] \cdot d t{/tex} = {tex}6 .\left\{\int \mathrm{t}^{2} . \mathrm{dt}-\int \mathrm{t} . \mathrm{dt}+\int 1 . \mathrm{dt}-\int\left[\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right] \cdot \mathrm{dt}\right\}{/tex} = {tex}6\left[\left(\frac{t^{3}}{3}\right)-\left(\frac{t^{2}}{2}\right)+t-\log (1+t)\right]{/tex} = {tex}6\left[\left(\frac{\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{3}}{3}\right)-\left(\frac{\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{2}}{2}\right)+\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)-\log \left(1+\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)\right)\right]+c{/tex} = {tex}\left[\left(2 x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)-\left(3 x^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)+6 \cdot x^{\frac{1}{6}}-6 \cdot \log \left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)\right]+C{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{x}-3 x^{\frac{1}{3}}+6 x^{\frac{1}{6}}-6 \log \left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)+c{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} or we can write it as {tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}=\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{3}}\left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)}{/tex} 
Let x = t6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = 6t5dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{3}}\left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)} \cdot d x=\int \frac{6 t^{5}}{t^{2}(1+t)} \cdot d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\text { 6. } \int \frac{t^{3}}{(1+t)} \cdot d t{/tex} 
After division we get,
{tex}\frac{1}{x^{\frac{1}{2}}+x^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left[\left(t^{2}-t+1\right)-\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right] \cdot d t{/tex} 
= {tex}6 .\left\{\int \mathrm{t}^{2} . \mathrm{dt}-\int \mathrm{t} . \mathrm{dt}+\int 1 . \mathrm{dt}-\int\left[\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right] \cdot \mathrm{dt}\right\}{/tex} 
= {tex}6\left[\left(\frac{t^{3}}{3}\right)-\left(\frac{t^{2}}{2}\right)+t-\log (1+t)\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}6\left[\left(\frac{\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{3}}{3}\right)-\left(\frac{\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)^{2}}{2}\right)+\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)-\log \left(1+\left(x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)\right)\right]+c{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\left(2 x^{\frac{1}{2}}\right)-\left(3 x^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)+6 \cdot x^{\frac{1}{6}}-6 \cdot \log \left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)\right]+C{/tex} 
= {tex}2 \sqrt{x}-3 x^{\frac{1}{3}}+6 x^{\frac{1}{6}}-6 \log \left(1+x^{\frac{1}{6}}\right)+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Given: {tex}\frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} Let {tex}I=\frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} Using partial fraction: Let {tex}\frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B x+C}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A\left(x^{2}+9\right)+(B x+C)(x+1)}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = A(x 2 + 9) + (Bx + C)(x + 1) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = Ax 2 + 9A + Bx 2 + Bx + Cx + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 9A + C + (B + C)x + (A + B)x 2 Equating the coefficients of x, x 2 and constant value. We get: 9A + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = -9A B+C = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 5 - C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 5 - (-9A) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 5 + 9A A + B = 0 ⇒ A = -B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = -(5 + 9A) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10A = -5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}{/tex} and C = {tex}\frac{9}{2}{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} Put these values in equation (i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B x+C}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=\frac{-\frac{1}{2}}{(x+1)}+\frac{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) x+\frac{9}{2}}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{(x+1)}+\frac{1}{2} \cdot\left(\frac{x+9}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x+\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x+\frac{9}{2} \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x+I_{1}+\frac{9}{2} \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+\left(3^{2}\right))\right.} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \log |\mathrm{x}+1|+\mathrm{I}_{1}+\frac{9}{2} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{3}\right){/tex} ...(ii) First solve for I 1 : {tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} Put x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2xdx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(\mathrm{t}+9)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(\mathrm{t}+9)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\mathrm{x}^{2}+9\right|{/tex} Put the value in equ. (ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \log |\mathrm{x}+1|+\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\mathrm{x}^{2}+9\right|+\frac{3}{2} \cdot\left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{3}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} 
Using partial fraction:
Let {tex}\frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B x+C}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A\left(x^{2}+9\right)+(B x+C)(x+1)}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = A(x2 + 9) + (Bx + C)(x + 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = Ax2 + 9A + Bx2 + Bx + Cx + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x = 9A + C + (B + C)x + (A + B)x2 
Equating the coefficients of x, x2 and constant value. We get:
9A + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = -9A
B+C = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 5 - C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 5 - (-9A) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = 5 + 9A
A + B = 0 ⇒ A = -B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = -(5 + 9A) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10A = -5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}{/tex} and C = {tex}\frac{9}{2}{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}  
Put these values in equation (i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=\frac{A}{(x+1)}+\frac{B x+C}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=\frac{-\frac{1}{2}}{(x+1)}+\frac{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) x+\frac{9}{2}}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex}  
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}=-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{(x+1)}+\frac{1}{2} \cdot\left(\frac{x+9}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)}\right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x+\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x+\frac{9}{2} \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(x+1)} d x+I_{1}+\frac{9}{2} \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+\left(3^{2}\right))\right.} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \log |\mathrm{x}+1|+\mathrm{I}_{1}+\frac{9}{2} \cdot\left(\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{3}\right){/tex} ...(ii)
First solve for I1:
{tex}I_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{x}{\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} 
Put x2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2xdx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(\mathrm{t}+9)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \int \frac{1}{(\mathrm{t}+9)} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\mathrm{x}^{2}+9\right|{/tex}
Put the value in equ. (ii) 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{5 x}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \log |\mathrm{x}+1|+\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\mathrm{x}^{2}+9\right|+\frac{3}{2} \cdot\left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{3}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Given Integrand is: {tex}\frac{\sin x}{\sin (x-a)}{/tex} Let {tex}\mathrm{I}=\int\frac{\sin \mathrm{x}}{\sin (\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})}{/tex} Let x - a = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = t + a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\sin x}{\sin (x-a)} d x=\int \frac{\sin (t+a)}{\sin (t)} d t{/tex} As, {sin (A + B) = sin A cos B + cos A sin B} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\sin x}{\sin (x-a)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\sin t \cos a+\cos t \sin a}{\sin (t)} d t{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\sin t \cos a}{\sin t}+\frac{\cos t \sin a}{\sin t} d t{/tex} = {tex}\int(\cos a+\cot t \sin a) d t{/tex} = {tex}\int(\cos a) d t+\int(\cot t \sin a) d t{/tex} = {tex}\cos a \int 1 . \mathrm{dt}+\sin \mathrm{a} . \int(\cot t) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{t}{/tex} = {tex}(\cos a) \cdot(x-a)+\sin a \cdot \log |\sin (x-a)|+c{/tex} = {tex}\sin a \cdot \log |\sin (x-a)|+x \cdot \cos a-a \cdot \cos a+c{/tex} = {tex}\sin a \cdot \log |\sin (x-a)|+x \cdot \cos a+c_{1}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given Integrand is: {tex}\frac{\sin x}{\sin (x-a)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}\mathrm{I}=\int\frac{\sin \mathrm{x}}{\sin (\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a})}{/tex} 
Let x - a = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = t + a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\sin x}{\sin (x-a)} d x=\int \frac{\sin (t+a)}{\sin (t)} d t{/tex} 
As, {sin (A + B) = sin A cos B + cos A sin B}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\sin x}{\sin (x-a)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\sin t \cos a+\cos t \sin a}{\sin (t)} d t{/tex} 
{tex}=\int \frac{\sin t \cos a}{\sin t}+\frac{\cos t \sin a}{\sin t} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\int(\cos a+\cot t \sin a) d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\int(\cos a) d t+\int(\cot t \sin a) d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\cos a \int 1 . \mathrm{dt}+\sin \mathrm{a} . \int(\cot t) \mathrm{d} \mathrm{t}{/tex} 
= {tex}(\cos a) \cdot(x-a)+\sin a \cdot \log |\sin (x-a)|+c{/tex}
= {tex}\sin a \cdot \log |\sin (x-a)|+x \cdot \cos a-a \cdot \cos a+c{/tex} 
= {tex}\sin a \cdot \log |\sin (x-a)|+x \cdot \cos a+c_{1}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Integrate the function {tex}\int {\frac{{{e^{5\log x}} - {e^{4\log x}}}}{{{e^{3\log x}} - {e^{2\log x}}}}} dx{/tex}
question_html: Integrate the function {tex}\int {\frac{{{e^{5\log x}} - {e^{4\log x}}}}{{{e^{3\log x}} - {e^{2\log x}}}}} dx{/tex}
solution_text: {tex}I=\int {\frac{{{e^{5\log x}} - {e^{4\log x}}}}{{{e^{3\log x}} - {e^{2\log x}}}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{{{e^{\log {x^5}}} - {e^{\log {x^4}}}}}{{{e^{\log {x^3}}} - {e^{\log {x^2}}}}}} dx{/tex} {tex}=\int {\left( {\frac{{{x^5} - {x^4}}}{{{x^3} - {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {e^{\log \theta }} = \theta } \right]{/tex} {tex} = \int {\frac{{{x^4}(x - 1)}}{{{x^2}(x - 1)}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \int {{x^2}dx} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int {\frac{{{e^{5\log x}} - {e^{4\log x}}}}{{{e^{3\log x}} - {e^{2\log x}}}}} dx{/tex}

{tex} = \int {\frac{{{e^{\log {x^5}}} - {e^{\log {x^4}}}}}{{{e^{\log {x^3}}} - {e^{\log {x^2}}}}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}=\int {\left( {\frac{{{x^5} - {x^4}}}{{{x^3} - {x^2}}}} \right)} dx{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because {e^{\log \theta }} = \theta } \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \int {\frac{{{x^4}(x - 1)}}{{{x^2}(x - 1)}}} dx{/tex}

{tex} = \int {{x^2}dx} {/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\frac{\cos x}{\sqrt{4-\sin ^{2} x}}{/tex} Put sin x = t ⇒ cos x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\cos x}{\sqrt{4-\sin ^{2} x}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4-t^{2}}} d t{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(2^{2}-t^{2}\right)}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x}{2}\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\frac{\cos x}{\sqrt{4-\sin ^{2} x}}{/tex} 
Put sin x = t ⇒ cos x dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\cos x}{\sqrt{4-\sin ^{2} x}} d x=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{4-t^{2}}} d t{/tex} 
{tex}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(2^{2}-t^{2}\right)}} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{2}\right)+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin x}{2}\right)+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Given function is: {tex}\frac{1}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}{/tex} Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}{/tex} Multiply and divide by sin (a - b), we get {tex}I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (a-b+x-x)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin [(x+a)-(x+b)]}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} As, {sin (A - B) = sin A cos B - cos A sin B} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (x+a) \cdot \cos (x+b)-\cos (x+a) \cdot \sin (x+b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (x+a) \cdot \cos (x+b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}-\frac{\cos (x+a) \cdot \sin (x+b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (x+a)}{\cos (x+a)}-\frac{\sin (x+b)}{\cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot[\tan (x+a)-\tan (x+b)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot[\tan (x+a)-\tan (x+b)] d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left\{\int \tan (x+a) d x-\int \tan (x+b) d x\right\}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[-\log |\cos (x+a)|-(-\log |\cos (x+b)|)]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[-\log |\cos (x+a)|+\log |\cos (x+b)|]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{\sin (\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})} \cdot \log \left|\frac{\cos (\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{b})}{\cos (\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{a})}\right|+\mathrm{c}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given function is: {tex}\frac{1}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}{/tex} 
Multiply and divide by sin (a - b), we get
{tex}I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (a-b+x-x)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin [(x+a)-(x+b)]}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} 
As, {sin (A - B) = sin A cos B - cos A sin B}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (x+a) \cdot \cos (x+b)-\cos (x+a) \cdot \sin (x+b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (x+a) \cdot \cos (x+b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}-\frac{\cos (x+a) \cdot \sin (x+b)}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot\left(\frac{\sin (x+a)}{\cos (x+a)}-\frac{\sin (x+b)}{\cos (x+b)}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot[\tan (x+a)-\tan (x+b)]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\cos (x+a) \cos (x+b)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \cdot[\tan (x+a)-\tan (x+b)] d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left\{\int \tan (x+a) d x-\int \tan (x+b) d x\right\}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[-\log |\cos (x+a)|-(-\log |\cos (x+b)|)]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}[-\log |\cos (x+a)|+\log |\cos (x+b)|]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{\sin (\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})} \cdot \log \left|\frac{\cos (\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{b})}{\cos (\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{a})}\right|+\mathrm{c}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}I=\frac{x^{3}}{\sqrt{1-x^{8}}}{/tex} Now, let x 4 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x 3 dx = dt And x 3 dx = {tex}\frac{dt}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}^{3}}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{x}^{8}}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{t}^{2}}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{4}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1^{2}-t^{2}}} \cdot d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \sin ^{-1} t+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{4} \sin ^{-1}\left(\mathrm{x}^{4}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\frac{x^{3}}{\sqrt{1-x^{8}}}{/tex} 
Now, let x4 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x3 dx = dt
And x3 dx = {tex}\frac{dt}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{x}^{3}}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{x}^{8}}} \mathrm{dx}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\mathrm{t}^{2}}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{4}\right){/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1^{2}-t^{2}}} \cdot d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \sin ^{-1} t+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{4} \sin ^{-1}\left(\mathrm{x}^{4}\right)+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let, {tex}I=\frac{e^{x}}{\left(1+e^{x}\right)\left(2+e^{x}\right)}{/tex} Let e x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} e x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}{\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}\right)\left(2+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}\right)} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\int \frac{1}{(1+\mathrm{t})(2+\mathrm{t})} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{(1+t)}-\frac{1}{(2+t)}\right] d t{/tex} = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right] d t-\int\left[\frac{1}{(2+t)}\right] d t{/tex} = {tex}\log |(1+t)|-\log |(2+t)|+c{/tex} = {tex}\log \left|\frac{1+\mathfrak{t}}{2+\mathfrak{t}}\right|+{C}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\log \left|\frac{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}{2+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}\right|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let, {tex}I=\frac{e^{x}}{\left(1+e^{x}\right)\left(2+e^{x}\right)}{/tex} 
Let ex = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ex dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}{\left(1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}\right)\left(2+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}\right)} \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}=\int \frac{1}{(1+\mathrm{t})(2+\mathrm{t})} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{(1+t)}-\frac{1}{(2+t)}\right] d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{(1+t)}\right] d t-\int\left[\frac{1}{(2+t)}\right] d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\log |(1+t)|-\log |(2+t)|+c{/tex} 
= {tex}\log \left|\frac{1+\mathfrak{t}}{2+\mathfrak{t}}\right|+{C}{/tex}  
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\log \left|\frac{1+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}{2+\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}\right|+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Given: {tex}\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} Using partial fraction: Let {tex}\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} .....(i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(A x+B)\left(x^{2}+4\right)+(C x+D)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = (Ax + B)(x 2 + 4) + (Cx + D)(x 2 + 1) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = Ax 3 + 4Ax + Bx 2 + 4B + Cx 3 + Cx + Dx 2 + D {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = (A + C)x 3 +(B + D)x 2 +(4A + C)x + (4B + D) Equating the coefficients of x, x 2 , x 3 and constant value. We get: A + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = -A B + D = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = -D 4A + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4A = -C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4A = A {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3A = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = 0 4B + D = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4B - B = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} D = {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex} Put these values in equation (i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}={/tex} {tex}\frac{(0) x+\frac{1}{3}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{(0) x+\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{\frac{1}{3}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1^{2}\right)} d x-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+2^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \cdot \tan ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{3} \cdot \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{x}-\frac{1}{6} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
Using partial fraction:
Let {tex}\frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} .....(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(A x+B)\left(x^{2}+4\right)+(C x+D)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{(x+1)\left(x^{2}+9\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}1 = (Ax + B)(x2 + 4) + (Cx + D)(x2 + 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = Ax3 + 4Ax + Bx2 + 4B + Cx3 + Cx + Dx2 + D
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = (A + C)x3 +(B + D)x2 +(4A + C)x + (4B + D)
Equating the coefficients of x, x2, x3 and constant value. We get:
A + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = -A
B + D = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = -D
4A + C = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4A = -C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4A = A {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3A = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = 0
4B + D = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4B - B = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} D = {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex}
Put these values in equation (i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}={/tex} {tex}\frac{(0) x+\frac{1}{3}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{(0) x+\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{\frac{1}{3}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)} d x{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+1^{2}\right)} d x-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \int \frac{1}{\left(x^{2}+2^{2}\right)} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{3} \cdot \tan ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{3} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{3} \cdot \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{x}-\frac{1}{6} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{x}}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

solution_text: {tex}I=\int {{{\cos }^3}x.{e^{\log \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex}\because {e^{\log \theta }} = \theta {/tex} {tex}\therefore {e^{\log \sin x}} = \sin x{/tex} {tex} I= \int {{{\cos }^3}} x.\sin xdx{/tex} Put cos x = t {tex} - \sin x\,dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\sin x\,dx = - dt{/tex} {tex}I = \int { - {t^3}dt} {/tex} {tex} = - \frac{{{t^4}}}{4} + c = - \frac{{{{\cos }^4}x}}{4} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int {{{\cos }^3}x.{e^{\log \sin x}}} dx{/tex}

{tex}\because {e^{\log \theta }} = \theta {/tex}

{tex}\therefore {e^{\log \sin x}} = \sin x{/tex}

{tex} I= \int {{{\cos }^3}} x.\sin xdx{/tex}

Put cos x = t

{tex} - \sin x\,dx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\sin x\,dx = - dt{/tex}

{tex}I = \int { - {t^3}dt} {/tex}

{tex} = - \frac{{{t^4}}}{4} + c = - \frac{{{{\cos }^4}x}}{4} + c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
solution_text: Let {tex}\mathrm{I}=\mathrm{e}^{3 \log \mathrm{x}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{4}+1\right)^{-1}{/tex} {tex}=e^{\log x^{3}}\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{-1}=\frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}+1}{/tex} Let x 4 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x 3 dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 3 dx = {tex}\frac{dt}{4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{3 \log x}\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{-1}=\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}+1} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{t+1} \cdot \frac{d t}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \cdot \int \frac{1}{t+1} \cdot d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log (t+1)+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{4} \log \left(x^{4}+1\right)+c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\mathrm{I}=\mathrm{e}^{3 \log \mathrm{x}}\left(\mathrm{x}^{4}+1\right)^{-1}{/tex} 
{tex}=e^{\log x^{3}}\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{-1}=\frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}+1}{/tex} 
Let x4 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x3 dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x3 dx = {tex}\frac{dt}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{3 \log x}\left(x^{4}+1\right)^{-1}=\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}+1} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{t+1} \cdot \frac{d t}{4}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \cdot \int \frac{1}{t+1} \cdot d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log (t+1)+C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{1}{4} \log \left(x^{4}+1\right)+c{/tex}

solution_text: Let f(ax + b) = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a.f'(ax + b)dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow \int \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{ax}+\mathrm{b})\left[\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{ax}+\mathrm{b})^{\mathrm{n}}\right]=\int \mathrm{t}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{a}}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{a} \int t^{n} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a} \cdot \frac{t^{n+1}}{n+1}+c{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a} \cdot \frac{(f(a x+b))^{n+1}}{n+1}+C{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{a(n+1)} \cdot(f(a x+b))^{n+1}+c{/tex}

Let f(ax + b) = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a.f'(ax + b)dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{ax}+\mathrm{b})\left[\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{ax}+\mathrm{b})^{\mathrm{n}}\right]=\int \mathrm{t}^{\mathrm{n}}\left(\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{a}}\right){/tex}

solution_text: I = {tex}\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^3}x.\sin (x + \alpha )} }}} {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^4}x.\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }} = \int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}dx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} } {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}xdx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin x.\cos \alpha + \cos x.\sin \alpha }}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex} {tex}=\int {\frac{{\cos e{c^2}2dx}}{{\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha } }}} {/tex} Put {tex}\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha = t{/tex} {tex}0 - \ cose{c^2}x.\sin \alpha dx = dt{/tex} {tex}\ cose{c^2}xdx = \frac{{ - dt}}{{\sin \alpha }}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex} I= \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}} \times \frac{{{t^{1/2}}}}{{1/2}} + c{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt t }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x\sin \alpha } }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^3}x.\sin (x + \alpha )} }}} {/tex}

{tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{\sqrt {{{\sin }^4}x.\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex}

{tex}=\int {\frac{{dx}}{{{{\sin }^2}x\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }} = \int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}dx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin (x + \alpha )}}{{\sin x}}} }}} } {/tex}

{tex}=\int {\frac{{\ cose{c^2}xdx}}{{\sqrt {\frac{{\sin x.\cos \alpha + \cos x.\sin \alpha }}{{\sin x}}} }}} {/tex}
{tex}=\int {\frac{{\cos e{c^2}2dx}}{{\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha } }}} {/tex}

Put {tex}\cos \alpha + \cot x.\sin \alpha = t{/tex}

{tex}0 - \ cose{c^2}x.\sin \alpha dx = dt{/tex}

{tex}\ cose{c^2}xdx = \frac{{ - dt}}{{\sin \alpha }}{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex} I= \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}\int {\frac{{dt}}{{\sqrt t }} = \frac{{ - 1}}{{\sin \alpha }}} \times \frac{{{t^{1/2}}}}{{1/2}} + c{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt t }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{{ - 2\sqrt {\cos \alpha + \cot x\sin \alpha } }}{{\sin \alpha }} + c{/tex}
